## PAGE 00162: (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 1

- page_id: `286557486`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/286557486/2024x+Refresh3+2024x+Refresh3+Hot+Fix+1
- version_number: 41
- version_date: `2026-04-15T12:50:34.320+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News > (2024x Refresh3) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools

###### Released on: February 20, 2026

2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2024x Refresh3) Component changes and addressed vulnerabilities' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when downloading its plugins. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

##### Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- Oracle DB and PostgreSQL drivers are no longer distributed with the modeling tool. Download and add the needed drivers according to the database type and version. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR3/pages/227154531/Code+Engineering+Sets)
- Issues related to tagged value export to Eclipse UML2 have been fixed.
- The issue where copying and pasting to a table cell does not work after clicking the Excel/CSV Sync > Sync Options > Refresh button has been fixed.
- The issue where the requirement ID and text tag values are not retained after the **Any to Any** model transformation has been fixed.

###### Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud

- The issue causing duplicated data after updating used projects has been fixed.
- The issue where used projects, in some cases, failed to update has been resolved.
- The issue where launching the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard from the command line fails has been fixed.
- The issue that sometimes caused duplicated tag values after a model merge has been fixed.

###### Collaboration powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- The issue where not all related standard profiles are added to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform together with a BPMN2 project has been fixed .
- The issue that occasionally caused failures when importing .szip files to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform due to recovered elements has been resolved.
- The problem that caused the project merge to fail after the project migration has been resolved.

##### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- Fixed an issue where the Simulation UI index page did not work when it contained non-Latin characters.
- Fixed an issue where the simulation got stuck at a time event following an **OpaqueAction**that invoked the **ALH.callBehavior** method.
- Fixed an issue where a non-existent verification pass value was exported to an instance when constraints were expanded in the Variables pane.

##### MagicCollaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator

- Fixed Teamwork Cloud authentication issue where usernames with Boolean values failed to authenticate.
- Improved Cassandra schema update mechanism.
- Fixed a small issue where, in certain cases, the Resources app did not display the option to open a document in Cameo Collaborator.
- Fixed a bug where the Prince logo appeared in exported Cameo Collaborator files.

##### Cameo DataHub

###### DOORS Next Integration (DNG)

Usability & Configuration Improvements:

- Added a scrollbar in the Create GC Configuration as Data Source panel for easier navigation through RM streams and baselines.
- Removed the requirement for a mandatory Java argument when testing backlink scenarios.
- Improved reliability when creating GC configurations as data sources, resolving connection errors and HTTP 404 issues.
- Fixed connection issues when working with RM Global Configuration Management (GCM) streams that include Test Management or Workflow Manager streams.

Project Structure & Node Management:

- Fixed issues preventing folder or node creation when a Team area is assigned to root or folder nodes.
- Resolved synchronization failures when creating new folders with assigned Team Areas.
- Fixed unnecessary change detection for requirements containing images.
- Improved drag-and-drop stability to prevent internal errors.

###### ENOVIA Requirements****

Performance Enhancements:

- Improved performance of link transfer between revisions.
- Reduced delays when expanding ENOVIA REQ nodes.
- Improved loading speed when displaying elements in the DataHub Explorer.
- Fixed delays when using Show All Elements and Show Revisions .
- Improved handling of large package synchronization (e.g., 1000+ requirements in cloud environments).

Synchronization & Data Consistency:

- Fixed the synchronization failures when revising requirements and chapters.
- Resolved issues where synchronization incorrectly marked nodes as Excluded.
- Fixed the need to run Check Changes twice on revised elements.
- Fixed an issue where the Data Synchronization process completed panel did not display correctly.
- Fixed missing sync icon display after revisions.
- Resolved multi-list attribute sync issue (all list entries now saved correctly).
- Fixed issues with individual mapping synchronization, including links.
- Corrected the issue where variation points were not created during new node sync with variants.

Attribute & Widget Improvements:

- Fixed editing issues for attributes assigned with NLS.
- Fixed the title display issue when special characters were used.
- Resolved the issue where the widget attribute values did not copy or display correctly.
- Fixed list-type widget attributes displaying as text fields instead of selectable lists.
- Improved support for Turkish and other special characters in properties and containment trees.

Link Management:

- Resolved missing link display issues in customized specification folders and requirement specifications.
- Fixed display issues for links containing angle brackets.
- Improved link visibility and consistency in DataHub Explorer.
- Provided clearer and more readable error messages for link transfer commands.

Explorer & Tree Improvements:

- Fixed the missing specification folders for specific users.
- Corrected structure order in DataHub Tree to match the 3DEXPERIENCE platform.
- Fixed display issues underthe ENOVIA REQ node after connection.
- Improved credential switching to ensure correct updates to Collaboration Space.
- Resolved internal errors when accessing contextual commands.
- Fixed the Show All and Show Owned Elements functionality.
- Fixed the issue where the OSLC preview displayed a login page during requirement loading.

###### JAMA Connect

API & Connectivity:

- Added environment option to switch between API v1 and v2 .
- Improved OAuth connectivity after API migration.
- Enhanced overall API migration support.

Synchronization & Structure Handling:

- Fixed issues where moved nodes were incorrectly treated as new entities during synchronization.
- Resolved link deletion inconsistencies when moving sets or components.
- Fixed link copy issues from Cameo to Jama.
- Resolved copy-with-sync failures for folders.
- Fixed expand operation issues for defect elements in the DataHub Tree.
- Resolved null pointer error during copy-with-sync operations.

###### IBM DOORS Integration

- Fixed synchronization issue when working with OLE objects and UTF-8 encoding that caused numeric errors.

###### Core DataHub improvements and fixes (CDH)

- User Experience Improvements.
- Added missing paste functionality in the Cameo DataHub Explorer search box.
- Fixed search results issue when searching for text containing spaces.
- Improved HTTP request retry handling to reduce connection-related exceptions.

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 11, 2025

#### TIP: Welcome to the version news site for CATIA Magic / No Magic products!

Welcome to the version news site for CATIA Magic / No Magic products!

Get started by selecting a product below and its version in the site header.

#### NOTE: 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 2 is available

2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 2 is available

Due to the Kryo serialization vulnerability issues (CVE-2026-785), the****2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 2 for **Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services was**released on May 29, 2026. [Learn more >>](https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613)

##### Hot Fix for version 2024x Refresh3

- 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 1 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 1 was released on February 20, 2026, for the CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes,and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2024x Refresh3) Component changes and addressed vulnerabilities' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>
- **2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 2** 
Due to theKryo serialization vulnerability issues (CVE-2026-785), the 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 2 for**Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services**was released on May 29, 2026. [Learn more >>](https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613)

## PAGE 00164: (2024x Refresh3) Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `272746504`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272746504/2024x+Refresh3+Cameo+Collaborator+for+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:25:25.166+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 11, 2025

Cameo Collaborator enhancements include the ability to choose project version in the table comparison view and share links to the latest table version, providing clearer version management.

##### cc1Enhanced Table Comparer

In this Cameo Collaborator update, users working with editable documents in the table comparison view can now switch between document and project versions. When a user changes the project via the document, it updates the project version but not the document version. The project version switcher makes it easy to switch between not only document versions, but project versions related to that particular document version too, allowing you to compare documents published for editing.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting project version in table comparer.

##### cc2Sharing Link to the Latest Table Version

In addition to obtaining a dynamic link to diagrams, users can now share a link to the latest version of a table. This allows recipients to access the newest table version for better collaboration in documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Getting link to the latest version of a table.

##### Other Improvements

- When creating the URL for diagrams or adding content (inserting), specific project version is now remembered and stored, ensuring consistent version tracking.
- The state of tables—such as filters, sorting, and column width changes—will now be preserved until you reopen or refresh the document. This ensures that when you return to a table, it is restored to its previous state.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud' space='TWCloud2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272746580 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00165: (2024x Refresh3) Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `272746580`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272746580/2024x+Refresh3+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:26:51.337+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Released on: July 11, 2025

In version 2024x Refresh3, Cameo DataHubpresents a two major enhancements. This release introduces enhancements related to the ENOVIA Requirements and DOORS NG data sources.

### ENOVIA Requirements

You can now access all requirements you own immediately after logging into the **ENOVIA Requirements**data source. All the Folders, Specifications, and Requirements owned by you are displayed in the Cameo DataHub Explorer Panel. You can also view all the requirements accessible to you on the server. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Data Sources for ENOVIA Requirements' space='CDH2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All the Folders, Specification and Requirements owned by you displayed in CDH Explorer Panel.

### DOORS Next Generation

Cameo DataHub now supports the Team Area feature enabled for Artifacts in the DOORS NG. You can now read, access and edit the objects in the Team Area.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo DataHub logo_2.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);">Released on: July 11, 2025</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-magenta-bolder,#50253f);">In version 2024x Refresh3, Cameo DataHub</span><span> </span>presents a two major enhancements. This release introduces enhancements related to the ENOVIA Requirements and DOORS NG data sources.</span></span></p><h1>ENOVIA Requirements</h1><p>You can now access all requirements you own immediately after logging into the <strong>ENOVIA Requirements </strong>data source. All the Folders, Specifications, and Requirements owned by you are displayed in the Cameo DataHub Explorer Panel. You can also view all the requirements accessible to you on the server. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding Data Sources for ENOVIA Requirements" ri:space-key="CDH2024xR3" /></ac:link> page.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Requirements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All the Folders, Specification and Requirements owned by you displayed in CDH Explorer Panel.</h6><h1>DOORS Next Generation</h1><p>Cameo DataHub now supports the Team Area feature enabled for Artifacts in the DOORS NG. You can now read, access and edit the objects in the Team Area.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272746796 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00168: (2024x Refresh3) Data Markings and Classification Plugin

- page_id: `272746796`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272746796/2024x+Refresh3+Data+Markings+and+Classification+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-02-19T14:17:50.264+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: July 11, 2025

The Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2024x Refresh3 release introduces the Update Recursively command, allowing you to efficiently update banners across all diagrams owned by a selected element. This ensures up-to-date information, especially after element updates, removals, or data marking changes.To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR3/Downloading+installation+files).

##### Update Diagram Banners Recursively

This release introduces the**Update Recursively**command, accessible via the shortcut menu of any model element containing diagrams with banners. This command efficiently updates the banners of all diagrams owned by the selected element at once, ensuring they reflect the latest information. It is especially useful after element updates, removals, and data marking changes, providing a convenient way to maintain accurate and up-to-date diagram banners.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Diagram Banners' space='DMP2024xR3']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings and Classification Plugin' space='DMP2024xR3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This ensures up-to-date information, especially after element updates, removals, or data marking changes.To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR3/Downloading+installation+files">Downloading installation files</a>. </p><p><br /></p><h3>Update Diagram Banners Recursively</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This release introduces the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Update Recursively</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> command, accessible via the shortcut menu of any model element containing diagrams with banners. It is especially useful after element updates, removals, and data marking changes, providing a convenient way to maintain accurate and up-to-date diagram banners. </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Updating the Diagram Banner"><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Managing Diagram Banners" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_banner_update.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><hr /><p><strong>Documentation</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2024xR3" ri:content-title="Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2024x Refresh3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul>

## PAGE 00171: (2024x Refresh3) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `272746407`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272746407/2024x+Refresh3+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-06-01T10:19:37.784+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

Released on: July 11, 2025

The latest update to Teamwork Cloud and Cameo Collaborator brings several key improvements for enhanced user experience and efficiency.

The Teamwork Cloud Admin UI now offers Cameo Collaborator and Simulation Toolkit licenses management options as well as a simplified SAML configuration, including the ability to set up and test configurations.

Cameo Collaborator enhancements include the ability to choose project version in the table comparison view and share links to the latest table version, providing clearer version management.

#### NOTE: 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 2 available

2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 2 available

Due to the Kryo serialization vulnerability issues (CVE-2026-785), the 2024x Refresh3 HF2 has been released on May 29, 2026.

For more information, see the Knowledge Base article at[https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613](https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613).

****

- 
- 
- 

****

- 
- 
- 

### Teamwork Cloud

##### aLicense Management Improvements

This update allows you to manage Cameo Collaborator and Simulation Toolkit web licenses directly through the Settings application in Teamwork Cloud Admin. The feature supports both DSLS and FlexNet licenses, making license management more accessible.

For administrative convenience, you can still configure licenses via the old method using the *webappplatform.properties* file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying server license using Teamwork Cloud Admin user interface.

##### bSAML Configuration Upgrades

From now on, SAML is configured via the UI and no longer from the properties file. You can set up SAML configuration directly through the user interface in the Settings application, with configurations stored in Cassandra. The update also introduces new features that allow users to test SAML configurations directly within the UI, simplifying the setup and testing process and improving the overall user experience.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Configuring SAML in Teamwork Cloud Admin Settings.

##### cImproved User Group Customization

When creating or editing conditional user groups, available attributes are now displayed for easier customization.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Viewing available attributes for conditional user group.

### Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

##### cc1Enhanced Table Comparer

In this Cameo Collaborator update, users working with editable documents in the table comparison view can now switch between document and project versions. When a user changes the project via the document, it updates the project version but not the document version. The project version switcher makes it easy to switch between not only document versions, but project versions related to that particular document version too, allowing you to compare documents published for editing.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting project version in table comparer.

##### cc2Sharing Link to the Latest Table Version

In addition to obtaining a dynamic link to diagrams, users can now share a link to the latest version of a table. This allows recipients to access the newest table version for better collaboration in documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Getting link to the latest version of a table.

##### Other Improvements

- When creating the URL for diagrams or adding content (inserting), specific project version is now remembered and stored, ensuring consistent version tracking.
- The state of tables—such as filters, sorting, and column width changes—will now be preserved until you reopen or refresh the document. This ensures that when you return to a table, it is restored to its previous state.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272746202 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00173: (2024x Refresh3) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `272746202`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272746202/2024x+Refresh3+Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:25:11.572+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

### Magic Cyber Systems Engineer

Released on: July 11, 2025

The 2024x Refresh3 release adds a range of enhancements to improve usability and performance of the simulation features. The Results Player now offers a multiple improvements to enhance the experience. You can automatically generate Time Series Charts, view the active state for selected parts, and even see state images next to labels for better visualization. Additionally, Requirement Pattern Verification is now supported, ensuring more accurate validation. While generatingFMU, you can use a personal access token instead of storing a username and password in the FMU file.The FMU Import Options dialog introduces a new Structured Import checkbox, which organizes FMU properties using dot notation.Lastly, the new Export States property allows you to export a part’s active state when generating a CSV file.

The 2024x Refresh3 release introduces key enhancements to collaborative modeling on both Teamwork Cloud and the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. Teamwork Cloud now supports partial project usages as a technology preview, enabling more flexible reuse of specific project parts, along with improved SSO support across command-line tools. On the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform, standard profile migration has been simplified, and project opening performance with local cache has been significantly improved. Both Teamwork Cloud and the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform will benefit from a new option to detect changes in derived properties during project merging or comparison.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024xR3']. For further information, check the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='MD2024xR3'].

****

- 
  - 
  - 
  - 
  - 
- 
  - 
  - 
  - 
  - 
  - 
- 
- 

****

- ****
  - 
    - 
    - 
  - 
    - 
    - 
    - 
  - 

### Simulation Features

#### Results Player Improvements

###### Requirement Verification

You can now verify requirements in the Results Player when you export the CSV file. You can verify requirements that are specified only with the help of the Requirement patterns. You can see different color coded labels for the passed and failed requirements and also a tooltip for the failed requirement. If some requirements have failed, the failing region(s) are highlighted in red on the Slider. Additionally, you can export all the mapped data back to a new CSV file. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A red label with tooltip, a green label, and failing region on the Slider displayed in the Block diagram and on the Results Player, respectively.

###### States Pictures Support

The Results Player now displays an image of the active state next to the state label as a shape icon. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Active State Image displayed when the file is loaded in the Results Player.

###### Active States Support

The Results Player now displays the active states for selected parts when loading a CSV file in the Results Player. Active states are displayed as the labels near a part shape when results are loaded.To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Active State displayed when the file is loaded in the Results Player.

###### Auto-opening simulation charts

When automatically loading generated CSV filewith a Time Series chart in the simulation configuration, the chart is automatically opened with the same values as the original configuration. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

#### FMU SupportImprovements

###### Token support for FMU Generation

You can nowembeda personal access token instead of a username and password in the generated FMU file. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating FMU - Technology preview' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Token support available in the Generate FMU(Technology Preview) dialog.

###### StructuredFMUImport Support

A new Structured Import checkbox is introduced in the FMU Import Options dialog. FMU properties are split using dot notation and the properties are represented as Flow or Value properties owned by an Interface Block. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FMU import' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **Structured Import** checkbox available in the FMU Import Options dialog.

###### FMI 3.0 Import Support

The modeling tool now supports FMU 3.0 import.

The simulation of FMU 3.0 using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.

###### Merge Arrays Support

If the FMU file has arrays (properties with the same name and index), then selecting the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FMU import' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] checkbox in the FMU Import Options dialog merges all the arrays into a single property with the multiplicity set.

The simulation of merged arrays using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All the arrays present in the FMU file merged into a single property with the multiplicity set.

###### Enumeration Support

The Parameters with Enumeration type are now supported for FMU Import and Simulation.

#### Exporting a Part's active state to a CSV file

A new Export States property is introduced to export Part's active state. If you set the Export States property to true, you can export the active State names for the selected part. If you set the Export States property to false, the instance name is exported. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CSV export' space='CST2024xR3'] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **Export States** property available in the Specification of CSVExport clearance dialog.

#### Other Improvements

- The new argument to override execution target on Simulation Configuration element is introduced. To learn more, refer to the Optional Arguments section of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using simulation command line and showing test results through Jenkins' space='CST2024xR3'] table.
- The CSV export has been improved to be more reliable and effective, especially while working with Dynamic Objects.

### Modeling and Infrastructure

- You can now disable all automatic and manual usage analytics, prevent internal error submissions, and remove related environment properties by modifying the system properties. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing Usage Analytics information' space='MD2024xR3']>>]]>
- This release introduces the ability to install custom browser extensions in JxBrowser.

### Collaboration

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### Partial Project Usages (Technology Preview)

We’re excited to introduce a significant enhancement to project usages, now available as a **technology preview**. Previously, you could only use an entire project (full usage), but with this release, you can define a usage scope and use only a specific part of a project. Additionally, you can seamlessly switch between full and partial usage at any time and adjust the usage scope as needed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting the scope of a partial project usage.

##### Improvements to Single Sign-On Authentication

For improved security, the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard now only accepts access tokens generated by your user when using single sign-on (SSO) authentication. Additionally, SSO is now supported in other command-line tools, like Report Wizard and Cameo Collaborator.

#### **3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

##### Easier Standard Profile Migration

The standard profile migration has been simplified allowing you to migrate all standard profiles in one go. Now, you can use the project migration dialogto select the profiles from both the server and your modeling tool installation. During migration:

- Standard profiles stored in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform are migrated.
- Standard profiles existing in the modeling tool installation but not yet added to the the server can be selected and added to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform.
- Any dependent profiles are also migrated or added.

##### Performance Improvements

In version 2024x Refresh3, project opening performance has been significantly improved when a local cache is already present on your machine, as demonstrated in the chart below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparison of project load times with local cache.

##### Other Improvements

- The following predefined templates have been introduced for publishing Cameo Collaborator documents:
  - UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator
  - UPDM Template for Cameo Collaborator
  - BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator
- You can now specify the project branch, revision, and iteration when logged in via the command line interface.

#### General Collaboration Improvements

- The new Detect Changes in Derived Properties environment option allows you to specify which derived properties to compare when detecting changes during project merge or compare.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh3 Version News' space='NMDOC']>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:anchor="FMU Support Improvements" /></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="3d50c55e-1149-4d0a-ac91-dcc009fd7837"><span data-ref="28ad5bb5-bf09-4b97-9435-f56fe5986939"><ac:link ac:anchor="Token support for FMU Generation" /></span></li><li data-uuid="ff0931b0-bd2f-41c9-82de-0d8820e69bf5"><ac:link ac:anchor="Structured FMU Import Support" /></li><li data-uuid="34736c11-3bd1-4112-96eb-7159e3c8f0c9"><ac:link ac:anchor="FMI 3.0 Import Support" /></li><li data-uuid="4007baf7-fae0-4516-9a7c-7239b1a700c2"><ac:link ac:anchor="Merge Arrays Support" /></li><li data-uuid="1f3e2c1a-1a39-434e-9318-e8c8721d1fa9"><ac:link ac:anchor="Enumeration Support" /></li></ul></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting a Part's active state to a CSV file" /></p></li><li data-uuid="b3885b02-c5d0-47a3-881f-73f192731c33"><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Other Improvements" /></p></li></ul><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="mi"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modeling and Infrastructure]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><ul><li><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Collaboration" /></strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="61e50bdb-b10e-4af9-a369-120d8fd3a7e8"><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Teamwork Cloud" /></strong><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Partial Project Usages (Technology Preview)" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Improvements to Single Sign-On Authentication" /></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="cdb633bb-037c-4029-861d-cc2928655c7c"><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="3DEXPERIENCE Platform" /></strong><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Easier Standard Profile Migration" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Performance Improvements" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Other Improvements" /></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="d32c224d-7684-42ed-8f1c-a4a10e551869"><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="General Collaboration Improvements" /></strong></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><hr /><h1>Simulation Features</h1><h2>Results Player Improvements</h2><h4>Requirement Verification</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can now verify requirements in the Results Player when you export the CSV file. You can verify requirements that are specified only with the help of the Requirement patterns. You can see different color coded labels for the passed and failed requirements and also a tooltip for the failed requirement. If some requirements have failed, the failing region(s) are highlighted in red on the Slider. Additionally, you can export all the mapped data back to a new CSV file. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" ri:content-title="Results Player" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:height="404" ac:width="978"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Failed label_Tooltip.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A red label with tooltip, a green label, and failing region on the Slider displayed in the Block diagram and on the Results Player, respectively.</h6><h4><span data-ref="b497e735-8dc9-4189-a4ae-0b1a3ab1b961">States Pictures Support</span></h4><p>The Results Player now displays an image of the active state next to the state label <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">as a shape icon</span>. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" ri:content-title="Results Player" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:alt="Active State Images Shown.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/169672713/Active%20State%20Images%20Shown.png?version=5&amp;modificationDate=1740397607264&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Active State Image displayed when the file is loaded in the Results Player.</h6><h4><span data-ref="fecf9819-b3f4-4e61-8630-51de5997ec77">Active States Support</span></h4><p>The Results Player now displays the active states for selected parts when loading a CSV file in the Results Player. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Active states are displayed as the labels near a part shape when results are loaded. </span>To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" ri:content-title="Results Player" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Active state support.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Active State displayed when the file is loaded in the Results Player.</h6><h4>Auto-opening simulation charts</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When automatically loading generated CSV file </span>with a Time Series chart in the simulation configuration, the chart is automatically opened with the same values as the original configuration. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" ri:content-title="Results Player" /></ac:link> page.</p><h2><span data-ref="28ad5bb5-bf09-4b97-9435-f56fe5986939">FMU Support </span><span>Improvements</span></h2><h4><span data-ref="28ad5bb5-bf09-4b97-9435-f56fe5986939">Token support for FMU Generation</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can now<span> embed </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">a personal access token instead of a username and password in the generated FMU file.</span> To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" ri:content-title="Generating FMU - Technology preview" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate FMU dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Token support available in the Generate FMU(Technology Preview) dialog.</h6><h4><span data-ref="27e31107-7ae0-401e-80b3-6933b6fbcabc">Structured </span><span data-ref="27e31107-7ae0-401e-80b3-6933b6fbcabc">FMU </span><span>Import Support</span></h4><p>A new Structured Import checkbox is introduced in the FMU Import Options dialog. FMU properties are split using dot notation and the properties are represented as Flow or Value properties owned by an Interface Block. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="FMU import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FMU Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="FMU Import.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Structured Import</strong> checkbox available in the FMU Import Options dialog.</h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">FMI 3.0 Import Support</span></h4><p>The modeling tool now supports FMU 3.0 import.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a30479d1-caf0-4396-8340-e855210f637b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The simulation of FMU 3.0 using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Merge Arrays Support</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the FMU file has arrays (properties with the same name and index), then selecting the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="FMU import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Merge Arrays]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> checkbox in the FMU Import Options dialog merges all the arrays into a single property with the multiplicity set.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a827ffd0-4007-4ef1-a2fa-2af87a28d9c8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The simulation of merged arrays using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Merge Array result_Comparison.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All the arrays present in the FMU file merged into a single property with the multiplicity set.</h6><h4>Enumeration Support</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Parameters with Enumeration type are now supported for FMU Import and Simulation.</span></p><h2>Exporting a Part's active state to a CSV file</h2><p>A new Export States property is introduced to export Part's active state. If you set the Export States property to true, you can export the active State names for the selected part. If you set the Export States property to false, the instance name is exported. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" ri:content-title="CSV export" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CSV Export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specification of CSVExport clearance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Export States</strong> property available in the Specification of CSVExport clearance dialog.</h6><h2><span data-ref="2d044de3-0445-4de4-b998-8729769e0e4f">Other Improvements</span></h2><ul><li data-uuid="7c7a57ce-f03f-4421-a7f5-03a0d310cbe4">The new argument to override execution target on Simulation Configuration element is introduced. To learn more, refer to the Optional Arguments section of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" ri:content-title="Using simulation command line and showing test results through Jenkins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Simulate command arguments and parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table.</li><li data-uuid="ee4e7649-49f1-469f-9218-6312129d0eb9">The CSV export has been improved to be more reliable and effective, especially while working with Dynamic Objects.</li></ul><br /><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h1>Modeling and Infrastructure</h1><ul><li data-uuid="7306f2ae-7ad6-4b12-84cb-8f300a856660"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can now disable all automatic and manual usage analytics, prevent internal error submissions, and remove related environment properties by modifying the system properties. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR3" ri:content-title="Sharing Usage Analytics information" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="21053542-a0ac-46cf-bf93-9ec17e8edb85"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This release introduces the ability to install custom browser extensions in JxBrowser.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h1><span>Collaboration</span></h1><h2><span>Teamwork Cloud</span></h2><h3>Partial Project Usages (Technology Preview)</h3><p>We’re excited to introduce a significant enhancement to project usages, now available as a <strong>technology preview</strong>. Previously, you could only use an entire project (full usage), but with this release, you can define a usage scope and use only a specific part of a project. Additionally, you can seamlessly switch between full and partial usage at any time and adjust the usage scope as needed.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="partial_project_usages.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting the scope of a partial project usage.</h6><h3>Improvements to Single Sign-On Authentication</h3><p>For improved security, the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard now only accepts access tokens generated by your user when using single sign-on (SSO) authentication. Additionally, SSO is now supported in other command-line tools, like Report Wizard and Cameo Collaborator.</p><h2><strong>3D</strong><span>EXPERIENCE Platform</span></h2><h3>Easier Standard Profile Migration</h3><p>The standard profile migration has been simplified allowing you to migrate all standard profiles in one go. Now, you can use the project migration <span>dialog </span>to select the profiles from both the server and your modeling tool installation. During migration:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a2654d05-bf9b-4e13-825f-a2353855966a">Standard profiles stored in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform are migrated.</li><li data-uuid="c4a88b26-1606-4ef8-ae1c-aaa2a67f8775">Standard profiles existing in the modeling tool installation but not yet added to the the server can be selected and added to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</li><li data-uuid="ee9246a7-c8d8-460e-9a18-e872fc5d9877">Any dependent profiles are also migrated or added.</li></ul><h3>Performance Improvements</h3><p>I<span>n version 2024x Refresh3, project opening performance has been significantly improved when a local cache is already present on your machine, as demonstrated in the chart below.<br /><br /></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_opening_performance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Comparison of project load times with local cache.</h6><h3>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="c9c67eb2-96fe-41bc-8fdc-1b168d365370">The following predefined templates have been introduced for publishing Cameo Collaborator documents:<ul><li>UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator</li><li>UPDM Template for Cameo Collaborator</li><li>BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="34296a70-654a-40b9-b478-d94e00761542">You can now specify the project branch, revision, and iteration when logged in via the command line interface.</li></ul><h2>General Collaboration Improvements</h2><ul><li data-uuid="fb708e04-3514-4d0e-8fd2-eedb3ce0ec50">The new <strong>Detect Changes in Derived Properties </strong>environment option allows you to specify which derived properties to compare when detecting changes during project merge or compare.</li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><hr /><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="2024x Refresh3 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Version news of servers and plugins >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272746684 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00174: (2024x Refresh3) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- page_id: `272746684`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272746684/2024x+Refresh3+Magic+Model+Analyst+Cameo+Simulation+Toolkit
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:27:00.163+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Released on: July 11, 2025

The 2024x Refresh 3 release adds a range of enhancements to improve usability and performance. The Results Player now offers a multiple improvements to enhance the experience. You can automatically generate Time Series Charts, view the active state for selected parts, and even see state images next to labels for better visualization. Additionally, Requirement Pattern Verification is now supported, ensuring more accurate validation. While generatingFMU, you can use a personal access token instead of storing a username and password in the FMU file.The FMU Import Options dialog introduces a new Structured Import checkbox, which organizes FMU properties using dot notation.Lastly, the new Export States property allows you to export a part’s active state when generating a CSV file.

- 
- 
- 
- 

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

### Results Player Improvements

###### Requirement Verification

You can now verify requirements in the Results Player when you export the CSV file. You can verify requirements that are specified only with the help of the Requirement patterns. You can see different color coded labels for the passed and failed requirements and also a tooltip for the failed requirement. If some requirements have failed, the failing region(s) are highlighted in red on the Slider. Additionally, you can export all the mapped data back to a new CSV file. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A red label with tooltip, a green label, and failing region on the Slider displayed in the Block diagram and on the Results Player, respectively.

###### States Pictures Support

The Results Player now displays an image of the active state next to the state label as a shape icon. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Active State Image displayed when the file is loaded in the Results Player.

###### Active States Support

The Results Player now displays the active states for selected parts when loading a CSV file in the Results Player. Active states are displayed as the labels near a part shape when results are loaded.To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Active State displayed when the file is loaded in the Results Player.

###### Auto-opening simulation charts

When automatically loading generated CSV filewith a Time Series chart in the simulation configuration, the chart is automatically opened with the same values as the original configuration. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

### FMU SupportImprovements

###### Token support for FMU Generation

You can nowembeda personal access token instead of a username and password in the generated FMU file. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating FMU - Technology preview' space='CST2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Token support available in the Generate FMU(Technology Preview) dialog.

###### StructuredFMUImport Support

A new Structured Import checkbox is introduced in the FMU Import Options dialog. FMU properties are split using dot notation and the properties are represented as Flow or Value properties owned by an Interface Block. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FMU import' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **Structured Import**checkbox available in the FMU Import Options dialog.

###### FMI 3.0 Import Support

The modeling tool now supports FMU 3.0 import.

The simulation of FMU 3.0 using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.

###### Merge Arrays Support

If the FMU file has arrays (properties with the same name and index), then selecting the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FMU import' space='SYSMLP2024xR3'] checkbox in the FMU Import Options dialog merges all the arrays into a single property with the multiplicity set.

The simulation of merged arrays using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All the arrays present in the FMU file merged into a single property with the multiplicity set.

###### Enumeration Support

The Parameters with Enumeration type are now supported for FMU Import and Simulation.

### Exporting a Part's active state to a CSV file

A new Export States property is introduced to export Part's active state. If you set the Export States property to true, you can export the active State names for the selected part. If you set the Export States property to false, the instance name is exported. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CSV export' space='CST2024xR3'] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **Export States** property available in the Specification of CSVExport clearance dialog.

### Other Improvements

- The new argument to override execution target on Simulation Configuration element is introduced. To learn more, refer to the Optional Arguments section of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using simulation command line and showing test results through Jenkins' space='CST2024xR3'] table.
- The CSV export has been improved to be more reliable and effective, especially while working with Dynamic Objects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Results Player" ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Active state support.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Active State displayed when the file is loaded in the Results Player.</h6><h4>Auto-opening simulation charts</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When automatically loading generated CSV file </span>with a Time Series chart in the simulation configuration, the chart is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="04b5e4be-d0d1-4b0b-b353-a8e77c299eab">automatically</ac:inline-comment-marker> opened with the same values as the original configuration. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Results Player" ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" /></ac:link> page.</p><h1><span data-ref="28ad5bb5-bf09-4b97-9435-f56fe5986939">FMU Support </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.01em;">Improvements</span></h1><h4><span data-ref="28ad5bb5-bf09-4b97-9435-f56fe5986939">Token support for FMU Generation</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can now<span> embed </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">a personal access token instead of a username and password in the generated FMU file.</span> To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating FMU - Technology preview" ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate FMU dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Token support available in the Generate FMU(Technology Preview) dialog.</h6><h4><span data-ref="27e31107-7ae0-401e-80b3-6933b6fbcabc">Structured </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.01em;" data-ref="27e31107-7ae0-401e-80b3-6933b6fbcabc">FMU </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.01em;">Import Support</span></h4><p>A new Structured Import checkbox is introduced in the FMU Import Options dialog. FMU properties are split using dot notation and the properties are represented as Flow or Value properties owned by an Interface Block. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="FMU import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FMU Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="FMU Import.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="389866e0-8062-4155-b170-d7e05264534b">Structured Import</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="389866e0-8062-4155-b170-d7e05264534b"> checkbox available in the FMU Import Options dialog.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">FMI 3.0 Import Support</span></h4><p>The modeling tool now supports FMU 3.0 import.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a30479d1-caf0-4396-8340-e855210f637b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The simulation of FMU 3.0 using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="389866e0-8062-4155-b170-d7e05264534b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Merge Arrays Support</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the FMU file has arrays (properties with the same name and index), then selecting the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR3" ri:content-title="FMU import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Merge Arrays]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> checkbox in the FMU Import Options dialog merges all the arrays into a single property with the multiplicity set.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a827ffd0-4007-4ef1-a2fa-2af87a28d9c8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The simulation of merged arrays using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Merge Array result_Comparison.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All the arrays present in the FMU file merged into a single property with the multiplicity set.</h6><h4>Enumeration Support</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Parameters with Enumeration type are now supported for FMU Import and Simulation.</span></p><h1>Exporting a Part's active state to a CSV file</h1><p>A new Export States property is introduced to export Part's active state. If you set the Export States property to true, you can export the active State names for the selected part. If you set the Export States property to false, the instance name is exported. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CSV export" ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CSV Export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specification of CSVExport clearance.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Export States</strong> property available in the Specification of CSVExport clearance dialog.</h6><h1><span data-ref="2d044de3-0445-4de4-b998-8729769e0e4f">Other Improvements</span></h1><ul><li>The new argument to override execution target on Simulation Configuration element is introduced. To learn more, refer to the Optional Arguments section of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using simulation command line and showing test results through Jenkins" ri:space-key="CST2024xR3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Simulate command arguments and parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table.</li><li data-uuid="17ac704d-9cc0-4e04-991e-cd47ab5d7725">The CSV export has been improved to be more reliable and effective, especially while working with Dynamic Objects.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272745975 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00175: (2024x Refresh3) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- page_id: `272745975`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272745975/2024x+Refresh3+Magic+Software+Architect+MagicDraw
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:25:02.791+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Magic Software Architect

Released on: July 11, 2025

The 2024x Refresh3 release introduces key enhancements to collaborative modeling on both Teamwork Cloud and the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. Teamwork Cloud now supports partial project usages as a technology preview, enabling more flexible reuse of specific project parts, along with improved SSO support across command-line tools. On the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform, standard profile migration has been simplified, and project opening performance with local cache has been significantly improved. Both Teamwork Cloud and the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform will benefit from a new option to detect changes in derived properties during project merging or comparison.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024xR3']. For further information, check the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MagicDraw Documentation' space='MD2024xR3'].

****

****

- 
  - 
  - 
- 
  - 
  - 
  - 
- 

### miModeling and Infrastructure

- You can now disable all automatic and manual usage analytics, prevent internal error submissions, and remove related environment properties by modifying the system properties. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing Usage Analytics information' space='MD2024xR3']>>]]>
- This release introduces the ability to install custom browser extensions in JxBrowser.

### Collaboration

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### Partial Project Usages (Technology Preview)

We’re excited to introduce a significant enhancement to project usages, now available as a **technology preview**. Previously, you could only use an entire project (full usage), but with this release, you can define a usage scope and use only a specific part of a project. Additionally, you can seamlessly switch between full and partial usage at any time and adjust the usage scope as needed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting the scope of a partial project usage.

##### Improvements to Single Sign-On Authentication

For improved security, the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard now only accepts access tokens generated by your user when using single sign-on (SSO) authentication. Additionally, SSO is now supported in other command-line tools, like Report Wizard and Cameo Collaborator.

#### **3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

##### Easier Standard Profile Migration

The standard profile migration has been simplified allowing you to migrate all standard profiles in one go. Now, you can use the project migration dialogto select the profiles from both the server and your modeling tool installation. During migration:

- Standard profiles stored in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform are migrated.
- Standard profiles existing in the modeling tool installation but not yet added to the the server can be selected and added to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform.
- Any dependent profiles are also migrated or added.

##### Performance Improvements

In version 2024x Refresh3, project opening performance has been significantly improved when a local cache is already present on your machine, as demonstrated in the chart below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparison of project load times with local cache.

##### Other Improvements

- The following predefined templates have been introduced for publishing Cameo Collaborator documents:
  - UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator
  - UPDM Template for Cameo Collaborator
  - BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator
- You can now specify the project branch, revision, and iteration when logged in via the command line interface.

#### General Collaboration Improvements

- The new Detect Changes in Derived Properties environment option allows you to specify which derived properties to compare when detecting changes during project merge or compare.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh3 Version News' space='CATIA']>]]>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272746069 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00176: (2024x Refresh3) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `272746069`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272746069/2024x+Refresh3+Magic+Systems+of+Systems+Architect+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-27T17:25:06.101+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2024x Refresh3) 2024x Refresh3 Version News
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Magic Systems of Systems Architect

Released on: July 11, 2025

The**2024x Refresh3**release comes with Gantt Chart improvements such as the ability to control sub-resource visibility and depth in the Capability Provisions panel, and to manage their In Service / Out of Service dates directly. To ensure date alignment in Gantt charts, a new validation rule was implemented.You can also now reuse existing Actual Project Milestones in different Gantt charts.Additionally, the release includes enhanced UAF element numbering, which allows to use of multi-level and hierarchical numbering of UAF elements.

Moreover, the release introduces key enhancements to collaborative modeling on both Teamwork Cloud and the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. Teamwork Cloud now supports partial project usages as a technology preview, enabling more flexible reuse of specific project parts, along with improved SSO support across command-line tools. On the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform, standard profile migration has been simplified, and project opening performance with local cache has been significantly improved. Both Teamwork Cloud and the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform will benefit from a new option to detect changes in derived properties during project merging or comparison.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024xR2']. For further information, check the product documentation.

****

- 
- 
- 

****

****

- 
  - 
  - 
- 
  - 

### UAF Features

Gantt Chart Improvements

- A new project option allows you to set the depth of displayed sub-resources in the Strategic Phasing/CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3/Cr Gantt Chart Capability Provisions panel. This lets you control how many sub-resource levels are displayed in the Capability Provisionarea.[Learn more >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR3/Setting+project+options) 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- A new toolbar button in the Gantt Chart allows you to toggle the visibility of sub-resources in the Capability Provisions panel. [Learn more >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR3/Working+with+Strategic+Phasing+diagram) 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- When you enable sub-resource visibility in the Strategic Phasing Gantt chart, you can now add, edit, or remove In Service / Out of Service dates for sub-resources directly in the Capability Provision area.[Learn more >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR3/Working+with+Strategic+Phasing+diagram) 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Now you can select an existing InService/ Out of Service Actual Project Milestones in the following Gantt charts:
  - Strategic Phasing
  - Resources Evolution
  - Personnel Evolution
  - Services Roadmap

### UAF Element Numbering

In this version, the UAF element numbering was enhanced. The multi-levelnumbering is now available for UAF elements. Now, you can apply:

- hierarchical numbering to UAF elements (such as Driver, Operational Information),
- hierarchical numbering with dynamic prefixes for the UAF requirements (such as Enterprise Goal, Enterprise Objective) based on SysML elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[Learn more >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR3/UAF+element+numbering)

### Other Enhancements

- A new Correctness validation rule was implemented to check whether the Capability Provision's actual start/end aligns with the Capability Provision's consolidated start/end dates. Learn more >>>

### miModeling and Infrastructure

- You can now disable all automatic and manual usage analytics, prevent internal error submissions, and remove related environment properties by modifying the system properties. [Learn more >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR3/Sharing+Usage+Analytics+information)
- This release introduces the ability to install custom browser extensions in JxBrowser.

### Collaboration

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### Partial Project Usages (Technology Preview)

We’re excited to introduce a significant enhancement to project usages, now available as a**technology preview**. Previously, you could only use an entire project (full usage), but with this release, you can define a usage scope and use only a specific part of a project. Additionally, you can seamlessly switch between full and partial usage at any time and adjust the usage scope as needed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting the scope of a partial project usage.

##### Improvements to Single Sign-On Authentication

For improved security, the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard now only accepts access tokens generated by your user when using single sign-on (SSO) authentication. Additionally, SSO is now supported in other command-line tools, like Report Wizard and Cameo Collaborator.

#### **3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

##### Easier Standard Profile Migration

The standard profile migration has been simplified allowing you to migrate all standard profiles in one go. Now, you can use the project migrationdialogto select the profiles from both the server and your modeling tool installation. During migration:

- Standard profiles stored in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform are migrated.
- Standard profiles existing in the modeling tool installation but not yet added to the the server can be selected and added to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform.
- Any dependent profiles are also migrated or added.

##### Performance Improvements

In version 2024x Refresh3, project opening performance has been significantly improved when a local cache is already present on your machine, as demonstrated in the chart below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparison of project load times with local cache.

##### Other Improvements

- The following predefined templates have been introduced for publishing Cameo Collaborator documents:
  - UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator
  - UPDM Template for Cameo Collaborator
  - BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator
- You can now specify the project branch, revision, and iteration when logged in via the command line interface.

#### General Collaboration Improvements

- The new Detect Changes in Derived Properties environment option allows you to specify which derived properties to compare when detecting changes during project merge or compare.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Refresh2 Version News' space='NMDOC']>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
