<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180342 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00207: (2026x Refresh1) Data Markings and Classification Plugin

- page_id: `314180342`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314180342/2026x+Refresh1+Data+Markings+and+Classification+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-18T07:22:02.501+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

The Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2026xRefresh1 release introducesthe 'subsumes' attribute for Data Markings, allowing you to specify which Data Marking overrules another, even across different Data Marking Categories. Additionally,you can now extend custom and DoD Data Marking Categories with additional Data Markings from separate Categories.When exporting a table to an Excel file, the table’s Diagram Banners are now automatically included in the exported file. In addition, the exported table's name now reflects the portion markings of the Data Markings applied to the table in the model.A new project option, Default Data Markings for New Sensitive Elements, has been introduced, allowing you to select which Data Markings are automatically applied to newly created sensitive elements, based on the element types specified in the Sensitive Element Typesproject option.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&version=2026x%20Refresh1).

##### The*subsumes*Representation Attribute

The 2026x Refresh1 release introduces the *subsumes*attribute for Data Markings. It allows you to specify which Data Marking overrules another, even across different Data Marking Categories. When an element with a subsumed marking is removed during Sensitive Element Redaction, all elements marked with the subsuming Data Marking are automatically removed as well.This provides clearer Data Marking relationships and predictable redaction results, preventing inconsistent or partial redaction. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=subsumes-representation-attribute&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Data Marking 'Secret' subsumes the Data Marking 'For Official Use Only'. 
If you perform Sensitive Element Redaction preview, removing elements marked 'For Official Use Only' also removes those marked 'Secret', but not vice versa.

##### Extend Data Markings Across Categories

You can now extend custom and DoD Data Marking Categories with additional Data Markings from separate Categories. The new Category serves only as a container for new Data Markings, which are grouped as submarkings under the parent Data Marking in another Category, as specified through the «permissiveSubDataMarkingCategory» stereotype's *groupWith* tag. These submarkings are not shown as a separate Category in menus and can only be applied through their parent Data Marking. This enables flexible extension of Data Markings without modifying existing Categories, keeping configurations stable while supporting new or evolving Data Marking requirements. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=Extending-Data-Markings&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The 'Authorized For Release To' Data Marking from the 'Dissemination Control' Category is extended with submarkings 'CAN', 'GBR', and 'USA'. 
These submarkings are available for selection in the Add/Remove Data Markings menu, under the 'Authorized For Release To' Data Marking.

##### Diagram Banners and Table Data Markings in Exported Tables

When exporting a table to an Excel file, the table’s Diagram Banners are now automatically included in the exported file. In addition, the exported table's name now reflects the portion markings of the Data Markings applied to the table in the model. This ensures that marking information remains visible and consistent outside the modeling environment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Other Improvements

- A new project option, Default Data Markings for New Sensitive Elements , has been introduced. It allows you to select which Data Markings are automatically applied to newly created sensitive elements, based on the element types specified in the Sensitive Element Types project option. This project option is user-specific and does not affect other users. Learn more >>

##### Documentation

- Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2026x Refresh1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: June 26, 2026</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>The Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2026x</span><span> Refresh1 release introduces </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">the 'subsumes' attribute for Data Markings, allowing you to specify which Data Marking overrules another, even across different Data Marking Categories. Additionally, </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">you can now extend custom and DoD Data Marking Categories with additional Data Markings from separate Categories. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">When exporting a table to an Excel file, the table’s Diagram Banners are now automatically included in the exported file. In addition, the exported table's name now reflects the portion markings of the Data Markings applied to the table in the model. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A new project option, Default Data Markings for New Sensitive Elements, has been introduced, allowing you to select which Data Markings are automatically applied to newly created sensitive elements, based on the element types specified in the Sensitive Element Types</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> project option.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Downloading installation files</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span> </span></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span style="font-size: 16.0px;letter-spacing: -0.006em;text-align: left;">The </span><em style="font-size: 16.0px;letter-spacing: -0.006em;text-align: left;">subsumes </em><span style="font-size: 16.0px;letter-spacing: -0.006em;text-align: left;">Representation Attribute</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The 2026x Refresh1 release introduces the <em>subsumes </em>attribute for Data Markings. It allows you to specify which Data Marking overrules another, even across different Data Marking Categories. When an element with a subsumed marking is removed during Sensitive Element Redaction, all elements marked with the subsuming Data Marking are automatically removed as well. </span>This provides clearer Data Marking relationships and predictable redaction results, preventing inconsistent or partial redaction. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=subsumes-representation-attribute&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="The subsumes Representation Attribute.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Data Marking 'Secret' subsumes the Data Marking 'For Official Use Only'. <br />If you perform Sensitive Element Redaction preview, removing elements marked 'For Official Use Only' also removes those marked 'Secret', but not vice versa. </h6><h3>Extend Data Markings Across Categories</h3><p>You can now extend custom and DoD Data Marking Categories with additional Data Markings from separate Categories. The new Category serves only as a container for new Data Markings, which are grouped as submarkings under the parent Data Marking in another Category, as specified through the «permissiveSubDataMarkingCategory» stereotype's <em>groupWith</em> tag. These submarkings are not shown as a separate Category in menus and can only be applied through their parent Data Marking. This enables flexible extension of Data Markings without modifying existing Categories, keeping configurations stable while supporting new or evolving Data Marking requirements. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=Extending-Data-Markings&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="977"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extend Data Markings Across Categories.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The 'Authorized For Release To' Data Marking from the 'Dissemination Control' Category is extended with submarkings 'CAN', 'GBR', and 'USA'. <br />These submarkings are available for selection in the Add/Remove Data Markings menu, under the 'Authorized For Release To' Data Marking.</h6><h3>Diagram Banners and Table Data Markings in Exported Tables</h3><p>When exporting a table to an Excel file, the table’s Diagram Banners are now automatically included in the exported file. In addition, the exported table's name now reflects the portion markings of the Data Markings applied to the table in the model. This ensures that marking information remains visible and consistent outside the modeling environment.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Diagram Banners and Table Data Markings in Exported Tables.png" /></ac:image></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="cb79c72a-7858-4828-902e-9b1cc0f83f95">A new project option, <strong>Default Data Markings for New Sensitive Elements</strong>, has been introduced. It allows you to select which Data Markings are automatically applied to newly created sensitive elements, based on the element types specified in the <strong>Sensitive Element Types</strong> project option. This project option is user-specific and does not affect other users. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=project-options&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="8c8d1e0a-744e-4c1a-a4c3-49091fa3fe9c"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=data-markings-and-classification-plugin&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2026x Refresh1</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314179937 space=VN version=4 -->
## PAGE 00210: (2026x Refresh1) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `314179937`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314179937/2026x+Refresh1+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-06-29T12:53:12.158+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

One of the most important additions in 2026x Refresh1 isMagicLab Collaborator, introduced to enable collaborative work on SysML v2 projects with branch-based feedback, commenting, and multi-branch review capabilities.

2026x Refresh1 release also delivers a brand new Resource Usage Map for SysML v2 resources that enhances impact analysis and a modernized Web Application Platform home page with a cleaner design and improved usability. Additional improvements include automatic SAML login redirection and configurable parallel execution of synchronization jobs.

Cameo Collaborator introduces a new Reader mode for distraction-free viewing, text comparison directly within tables, enhanced PDF export options with automatic tables of figures, diagrams, and tables, smoother zooming, and the ability to edit the To Do property.

#### MagicLab Collaborator

We introduce MagicLab Collaborator, a service that enables teams to collaborate on SysML v2 projects. Building on the capabilities of MagicLab, which is a read-only viewer, MagicLab Collaborator includes flexible collaboration features such as branch-specific feedback, commenting with search and editing, and support for working across multiple branches. Users can also open and review both current and historical branches, improving traceability and team coordination.

##### Commenting

Commenting features, available with a Cameo Collaborator license, provide flexible ways to collaborate on specific elements and to view all comments of the project with easy navigation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing comments for a specific element in the element Specification panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commenting features of MagicLab Collaborator.

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### Resource Usage Map for SysML v2 Resources

Support for SysML v2 resources has been enhanced with the implementation of a dedicated Resource Usage Map. When selecting a SysML v2 resource in the Resources application, users can now access the new “Open in Resource Usage Map” action. The generated map visualizes all dependencies of the selected SysML v2 resource, providing improved transparency and impact analysis.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying SysML v2 project usages in the Resource Usage Map.

##### Teamwork Cloud SysML v2 REST API extensions

This release introduces two additional SysML v2-focused API extensions for Teamwork Cloud, enabling powerful CI/CD workflows for MBSE models.

###### **Textual API Services**

The textual API provides services for retrieving the textual representation of a SysML v2 model from a specified namespace. Additionally, the textual representation can be used in the payload to update a namespace of an existing SysML v2 model.

###### **Graphical API Services**

The graphical API provides a service to retrieve the visual representation of a SysML v2 diagram by its View ID. The service returns an SVG image that preserves the original diagram layout and the content of the elements used to build it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

These services are available to Magic Collaboration Studio and Teamwork Cloud Enterprise edition users.

##### Multi-Threaded Cross-Cluster Synchronization

A new mechanism has been implemented to allow multiple synchronization configurations to run simultaneously, with the number of concurrent executors now fully configurable. This allows for a considerably faster synchronization process.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing multiple resources.

##### Modernized Web Application Platform Home Page

The Web Application Platform home page has been refreshed with a cleaner, more modern design to enhance the overall user experience. The updated interface features a visually polished appearance, making it easier for users to access the apps and information. This modernization improves usability, performance, and provides a more intuitive digital experience.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Modernized Web Application Platform home page.

##### Other

- A new improvement introduces automatic redirection to the configured Identity Provider’s login page when SAML is the only authentication option available on the authentication server login page. This addresses a common customer request for a more streamlined login experience.

#### Cameo Collaborator

##### New Reading Mode in Collaborator

A new Reading mode has been introduced in Cameo Collaborator to provide a focused, distraction-free viewing experience.When the Reading mode is enabled, commenting, editing, and simulation tabs are hidden, and existing comments are no longer displayed within the content. This allowsusers to review documents in a clean, read-only format, making it easier to concentrate on the content without interactive elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling Reading mode in Cameo Collaborator.

##### Text Comparer in Tables

When comparing tables in Cameo Collaborator, a new “Compare texts” option is now available, enabling users to view differences directly in plain text within the table for added convenience and clarity.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparing texts in a table in Cameo Collaborator.

##### Additional Table Export Options in Document Exporter

PDF export has been enhanced with the ability to automatically generate a Table of Figures, Table of Diagrams, and Table of Tables, providing improved document structure and navigation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### New Document Exporter options.

##### Authentication Using Personal Access Token

When publishing documents from the command line, you now have the option to use your personal access token instead of your username and password. This increases security as you do not have to input your data in plain text and eliminates the need to update the properties file in case your password changes.

##### Other

- Zooming in Cameo Collaborator is now smoother for a better viewing experience.
- It is now possible to edit the To Do property directly in Cameo Collaborator.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Users can also open and review both current and historical branches, improving traceability and team coordination.</span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Commenting</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Commenting features, available with a Cameo Collaborator license, provide flexible ways to collaborate on specific elements and to view all comments of the project with easy navigation.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="magiclab_individual_element_comments.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Viewing comments for a specific element in the element Specification panel.</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:height="856" ac:width="1400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="magiclab_collaborator_comments.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Commenting features of MagicLab Collaborator.</span></h6><h2>Teamwork Cloud</h2><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Resource Usage Map for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4afb749c-13c4-449f-88bf-e78fb21179b4">SysML v2</ac:inline-comment-marker> Resources</span></h3><p><span>Support for SysML v2 resources has been enhanced with the implementation of a dedicated Resource Usage Map. The generated map visualizes all dependencies of the selected SysML v2 resource, providing improved transparency and impact analysis.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sysmlv2_project_usages.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Displaying SysML v2 project usages in the Resource Usage Map.</span></h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Teamwork Cloud SysML v2 REST API extensions</span></h3><p style="font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><span>This release introduces two additional SysML v2-focused API extensions for Teamwork Cloud, enabling powerful CI/CD workflows for MBSE models.</span></span></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><span><strong>Textual API Services</strong></span></span></h4><p>The textual API provides services for retrieving the textual representation of a SysML v2 model from a specified namespace. Additionally, the textual representation can be used in the payload to update a namespace of an existing SysML v2 model.</p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><span><strong>Graphical API Services</strong></span></span></h4><p style="font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><span>The graphical API provides a service to retrieve the visual representation of a SysML v2 diagram by its View ID. The service returns an SVG image that preserves the original diagram layout and the content of the elements used to build it.</span></span></p><p style="font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="teamwork_cloud_sysml2_api.png" /></ac:image></span></span></p><p style="font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;"><br /></p><p style="font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><span>These services are available to Magic Collaboration Studio and Teamwork Cloud Enterprise edition users.</span></span></p><h3>Multi-Threaded Cross-Cluster Synchronization</h3><p><span>A new mechanism has been implemented to allow multiple synchronization configurations to run simultaneously, with the number of concurrent executors now fully configurable. This allows for a considerably faster synchronization process.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:height="667" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="multi_threaded_sync.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing multiple resources.</h6><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Modernized Web Application Platform Home Page</span></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="27d99c58-398d-4478-a04e-d4742a1e0707">The Web Application Platform home page has been refreshed with a cleaner, more modern design to enhance the overall user experience. This addresses a common customer request for a more streamlined login experience.</span></li></ul><h2>Cameo Collaborator</h2><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">New Reading Mode in Collaborator</span></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a12e0f65-f237-4ebc-a5d6-000d09a94126">A new Reading mode has been introduced in Cameo Collaborator to provide a focused, distraction-free viewing experience. </ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a12e0f65-f237-4ebc-a5d6-000d09a94126">When the Reading mode is enabled, c</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a12e0f65-f237-4ebc-a5d6-000d09a94126">ommenting, editing, and simulation tabs are hidden, and e</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a12e0f65-f237-4ebc-a5d6-000d09a94126">xisting comments are no longer displayed within the content. This allows </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a12e0f65-f237-4ebc-a5d6-000d09a94126">users to review documents in a clean, read-only format, making it easier to concentrate on the content without interactive elements.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a12e0f65-f237-4ebc-a5d6-000d09a94126"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reading_mode.png" /></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><h6 style="text-align: /></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f0dac8e-d23f-4196-9d5f-9cd72034732e">Comparing texts in a table in Cameo Collaborator.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></h6><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Additional Table Export Options in Document Exporter</span></h3><p>PDF export has been enhanced with the ability to automatically generate a Table of Figures, Table of Diagrams, and Table of Tables, providing improved document structure and navigation.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:height="782" ac:width="493"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_table_options_in_collaborator_export.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">New Document Exporter options.</h6><h3>Authentication Using Personal Access Token</h3><p>When publishing documents from the command line, you now have the option to use your personal access token instead of your username and password. This increases security as you do not have to input your data in plain text and eliminates the need to update the properties file in case your password changes.</p><h3>Other</h3><ul><li data-uuid="9b3fb775-4f5d-496f-a6d5-20d1dbf3f61f"><p>Zooming in Cameo Collaborator is now smoother for a better viewing experience.</p></li><li data-uuid="a2715b21-7ae7-44a9-8a3c-57981d03ab8b">It is now possible to edit the To Do property directly in Cameo Collaborator.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00211: (2026x Refresh1) Magic Concept Modeler / Concept Modeler Plugin

- page_id: `314180112`

- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

This version was released for compatibility purposes only. It does not contain new capabilities.

Check the product compatibility [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product Compatibility' space='PC'].

##### Documentation

- Magic Concept Modeler / Concept Modeler Plugin 2026x Refresh1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Released on: June 26, 2026</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">This version was released for compatibility purposes only. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314179731 space=VN version=5 -->
## PAGE 00212: (2026x Refresh1) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `314179731`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314179731/2026x+Refresh1+Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-07-10T14:54:10.904+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### top

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

The 2026x Refresh1 release introduces multiple improvements to SysML v1 modeling, simulation, and client-side collaborative modeling capabilities.

SysML v1 modeling now offers enhanced import from Excel files, supporting nested connectors.

Model simulation improvements include instance selection for behavioral diagrams, allowing you to filter animation for a single object in State Machine, Activity, and Sequence diagrams. In addition, UI mockups now support unit symbols, and a new**Show Units**property lets you control whether units are displayed in diagrams and mockups.

In client-side collaborative modeling, Partial Project Usages is now out of the technology preview phase and offers a smoother user experience for defining usage scope. Additionally, the feature for saving projects to an earlier version has been improved so that all used projects are saved together with the open project, and Teamwork Cloud projects can now be saved to an earlier version as .mdzip files.

#### SysML v1 Features

##### Nested Connector Import

Import from Excel files has been enhanced to support nested connectors. You can now define connectors between elements located at different levels of the model structure, and the tool will automatically construct the corresponding nested connector paths during import. This improvement enables accurate reconstruction of complex internal structures directly from external data sources, reducing manual adjustments and improving modeling efficiency. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Importing-data-from-Excel-and-CSV-files&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=default)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Simulation Features

##### Instance Selection in Behavioral Diagrams

The 2026x Refresh1 version of the plugin allows you to filter animation for one specific object in State Machine, Activity, and Sequence diagrams.If several instances of the same behavior exist, the diagram automatically shows animation only for the first instance annotated in the diagram. This prevents the diagram from merging events from multiple concurrent instances and improves clarity during simulation analysis. You can switch the displayed instance from the contextual menu.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 Selecting the Instance to be animated in a State Machine Diagram.

##### UI Mockup Improvements

This release introduces the ability to display the unit symbol in UI mockups. In addition, a new Simulation Configuration property — **Show Units** — allows you to control whether units are displayed in diagrams and UI mockups.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying units in UI mockups

##### Other Improvements

- Time Series and Timeline Charts now support negative time values.
- UI mockups, graphs, and other elements generated during model execution are displayed with an automatic offset, making them easier to rearrange. In addition, the adjusted layout is now preserved between execution runs.

#### Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud

##### Partial Project Usages

We are excited to announce that Partial Project Usages is now out of the technology preview phase, featuring a significantly improved overall user experience. As in previous releases, the feature allows you to define the usage scope by selecting a specific part of a project, and enables seamless switching between full and partial usages as well as easy adjustment of the usage scope at any time.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/CM/?contextKey=using-server-projects&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Partial project usage

##### Saving Projects to an Earlier Version

The previous release introduced the ability to save projects to an earlier major version—for example, from version 2026x to 2024x—making it easier to collaborate with teams using older versions of the modeling tool.

In version 2026x Refresh1, this functionality brings the following improvements:

- If your project uses other projects, all used projects are saved together with the open project.
- You can now save Teamwork Cloud projects to an older version as .mdzip files without first saving them locally.

##### SysML v2 Project Migration to Version 2026x Refresh1

The new release supports several options for migrating **SysML v2 projects** to version 2026x Refresh1. You can migrate all or selected projects on the Teamwork Cloud server in bulk via the **Collaborate** menu, or migrate standalone Teamwork Cloud or local repository projects by opening them and agreeing to update standard libraries.

#### Documentation

- Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler 2026x Refresh1

In addition, UI mockups now support unit symbols, and a new**Show Units**property lets you control whether units are displayed in diagrams and mockups.

The 2026x Refresh1 version of the plugin allows you to filter animation for one specific object in State Machine, Activity, and Sequence diagrams.If several instances of the same behavior exist, the diagram automatically shows animation only for the first instance annotated in the diagram. ### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### top

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

The 2026x Refresh1 release comes with multiple improvements to UAF 1.3 modeling, SysML v1 modeling, and client-side collaborative modeling capabilities.

UAF 1.3 usability has been enhanced in alignment with the UAF v1.3 Mission Engineering Profile.

SysML v1 modeling now offers enhanced import from Excel files, supporting nested connectors.

In client-side collaborative modeling, Partial Project Usages is now out of the technology preview phase and offers a smoother user experience for defining usage scope. Additionally, the feature for saving projects to an earlier version has been improved so that all used projects are saved together with the open project, and Teamwork Cloud projects can now be saved to an earlier version as .mdzip files.

#### UAF 1.3 Features

##### UAF 1.3 Usability Enhancements

In this version, a dedicated **Environment Properties** compartment has been implemented to group and display Environment properties separately from other properties. This enhancement brings greater clarity to diagrams and makes it easier to work with Environment properties.

Additionally, Environment properties now have dedicated **Symbol Properties**, which can be managed independently from other properties and attributes throughout the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

##### 600400

#### SysML v1 Features

##### Nested Connector Import

Import from Excel files has been enhanced to support nested connectors. You can now define connectors between elements located at different levels of the model structure, and the tool will automatically construct the corresponding nested connector paths during import. This improvement enables accurate reconstruction of complex internal structures directly from external data sources, reducing manual adjustments and improving modeling efficiency. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Importing-data-from-Excel-and-CSV-files&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=default)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud

##### Partial Project Usages

We are excited to announce that Partial Project Usages is now out of the technology preview phase, featuring a significantly improved overall user experience. As in previous releases, the feature allows you to define the usage scope by selecting a specific part of a project, and enables seamless switching between full and partial usages as well as easy adjustment of the usage scope at any time.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/CM/?contextKey=using-server-projects&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Partial project usage

##### Saving Projects to an Earlier Version

The previous release introduced the ability to save projects to an earlier major version—for example, from version 2026x to 2024x—making it easier to collaborate with teams using older versions of the modeling tool.

In version 2026x Refresh1, this functionality brings the following improvements:

- If your project uses other projects, all used projects are saved together with the open project.
- You can now save Teamwork Cloud projects to an older version as .mdzip files without first saving them locally.

##### SysML v2 Project Migration to Version 2026x Refresh1

The new release supports several options for migrating **SysML v2 projects** to version 2026x Refresh1. You can migrate all or selected projects on the Teamwork Cloud server in bulk via the **Collaborate** menu, or migrate standalone Teamwork Cloud or local repository projects by opening them and agreeing to update standard libraries.

#### Documentation

- Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2026x Refresh1

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00300006-f71d-4a8a-9ea2-19bebdf6f8ba"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: June 26, 2026</h6><p><br /></p><p class="isSelectedEnd" style="text-align: left;"><span>The 2026x Refresh1 release comes with multiple improvements to UAF 1.3 modeling, SysML v1 modeling, and client-side collaborative modeling capabilities.</span></p><p class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchorContainer"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><span>UAF 1.3 usability has been enhanced in alignment with the UAF v1.3 Mission Engineering Profile.</span></span></span></p><p class="isSelectedEnd" style="text-align: left;"><span>SysML v1 modeling now offers enhanced import from Excel files, supporting nested connectors.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span>In client-side collaborative modeling, Partial Project Usages is now out of the technology preview phase and offers a smoother user experience for defining usage scope. Additionally, the feature for saving projects to an earlier version has been improved so that all used projects are saved together with the open project, and Teamwork Cloud projects can now be saved to an earlier version as .mdzip files.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>UAF 1.3 Features</h2><h3 style="text-align: left;">UAF 1.3 Usability Enhancements</h3><p>In this version, a dedicated <strong>Environment Properties</strong> compartment has been implemented to group and display Environment properties separately from other properties. This enhancement brings greater clarity to diagrams and makes it easier to work with Environment properties.</p><p>Additionally, Environment properties now have dedicated <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>, which can be managed independently from other properties and attributes throughout the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog.</p><h3 style="text-align: center;"> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dac87a2c-3eaa-4f67-b1e0-6461b40dd9d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XCFtSfTw-3k" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></h3><h2>SysML v1 Features</h2><h3>Nested Connector Import</h3><p>Import from Excel files has been enhanced to support nested connectors. You can now define connectors between elements located at different levels of the model structure, and the tool will automatically construct the corresponding nested connector paths during import. <span>This improvement enables accurate reconstruction of complex internal structures directly from external data sources, reducing manual adjustments and improving modeling efficiency. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Importing-data-from-Excel-and-CSV-files&amp;version=2026x Refresh1&amp;variant=default">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nested Connector Import.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><span>Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud</span></h2><h3>Partial Project Usages</h3><p><span>We are excited to announce that Partial Project Usages is now out of the technology preview phase, featuring a significantly improved overall user experience. As in previous releases, the feature allows you to define the usage scope by selecting a specific part of a project, and enables seamless switching between full and partial usages as well as easy adjustment of the usage scope at any time.</span></p><p><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/CM/?contextKey=using-server-projects&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="partial_project_usage_GA.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Partial project usage</h6><h3>Saving Projects to an Earlier Version</h3><p><span>The previous release introduced the ability to save projects to an earlier major version—for example, from version 2026x to 2024x—making it easier to collaborate with teams using older versions of the modeling tool.</span></p><p>In version 2026x Refresh1, this functionality brings the following improvements:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="5f3fb547-60fa-4e6b-a6da-f2236fbc2841">If your project uses other projects, all used projects are saved together with the open project.</li><li data-uuid="140127dd-f24d-4e02-b609-b7acd765bd75">You can now save Teamwork Cloud projects to an older version as .mdzip files without first saving them locally.</li></ul><h3>SysML v2 Project Migration to Version 2026x Refresh1</h3><p>The new release supports several options for migrating <strong>SysML v2 projects</strong> to version 2026x Refresh1. You can migrate all or selected projects on the Teamwork Cloud server in bulk via the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, or migrate standalone Teamwork Cloud or local repository projects by opening them and agreeing to update standard libraries.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="118096c0-739f-4ad8-82be-5c6c36f791bd"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=modeling-tools-documentation&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1&amp;variant=Magic%20Systems%20of%20Systems%20Architect%20%2F%20Cameo%20Enterprise%20Architecture">Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2026x Refresh1</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180244 space=VN version=3 -->
## PAGE 00217: (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 Plugin

- page_id: `314180244`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314180244/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+v1+Plugin
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-26T10:50:05.320+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

SysML v1 Plugin 2026xRefresh1 release introduces enhanced import from Excel files to support nested connectors.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&version=2026x%20Refresh1).

##### Nested Connector Import

Import from Excel files has been enhanced to support nested connectors. You can now define connectors between elements located at different levels of the model structure, and the tool will automatically construct the corresponding nested connector paths during import. This improvement enables accurate reconstruction of complex internal structures directly from external data sources, reducing manual adjustments and improving modeling efficiency. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Importing-data-from-Excel-and-CSV-files&version=2026x Refresh1&variant=default)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Documentation

- SysML v1 Plugin 2026x Refresh1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

You can now define connectors between elements located at different levels of the model structure, and the tool will automatically construct the corresponding nested connector paths during import. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This improvement enables accurate reconstruction of complex internal structures directly from external data sources, reducing manual adjustments and improving modeling efficiency. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Importing-data-from-Excel-and-CSV-files&amp;version=2026x Refresh1&amp;variant=default">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nested Connector Import.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="d98263a4-4706-46d0-977f-4c945203d5db"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=sysml-v1-modeling&amp;version=2026x Refresh1&amp;variant=default">SysML v1 Plugin 2026x Refresh1</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180279 space=VN version=8 -->
## PAGE 00218: (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin

- page_id: `314180279`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314180279/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+v2+Evaluation+Plugin
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-06-29T15:50:45.371+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

The 2026x Refresh1 release expands the SysML v2 Evaluation plugin with new capabilities, including support for constraints written in scripting languages, support for binding connectors in feature chain expressions, evaluation of part definitions, and the ability to define initial values directly in model views. It also improves usability by displaying units alongside evaluated values and showing individual evaluation results for each requirement constraint, making model analysis more efficient.

##### Textual Representation Support for Constraints

The new version of the SysML v2 Evaluation plugin enables the use of textual representation elements to evaluate constraints written in other scripting languages. As a result, scripting languages can now be used not only for standard calculations, but also to evaluate both requirements and standalone constraints.

partvehicle{attributespeed:Real=100;assertconstraint{language"groovy"/*speed==100 */} 
}

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=using-textual-representation&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

##### Support for Binding Connectors

The 2026x Refresh1 release introduces support for binding connectors used with feature chains in expressions. This enhancement enables you to evaluate calculations and execute performActions in model views. During evaluation, output values are calculated using eitherdefault valuesorvalues obtained through binding connectors.

##### Defining Initial Values During Evaluation

You can now enter initial values during evaluation using model views, even if no value is defined in the model. Such features and attributes are displayed with “:=” and an empty field in both shapes and compartments.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=manipulating-runtime-values&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

##### Displaying Units for Evaluated Values

Views now display units next to calculated values when units are defined in the model. This improves clarity and makes results easier to interpret.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=displaying-evaluation-information&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying units next to evaluated values in model views.

##### Evaluating Part Definitions

In addition to part usages, the new plugin version now allows you to evaluate part definitions as well. When a part definition is evaluated directly, all of its features are evaluated recursively. When evaluating an entire model view, only the first-level part definitions are processed. This enhancement helps you quickly analyze the structure of a part definition without having to create part usages.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Initiating the evaluation of a part definition in a model view.

##### Evaluation Results for Individual Requirement Constraints

When a requirement has multiple constraints, model views now display the evaluation result for each constraint individually. This improvement provides clearer insight into how the overall requirement result is determined and makes it easier to understand evaluation results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample requirement displaying evaluation results for individual constraints.

##### Documentation

- SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin 2026x Refresh1
