````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: June 26, 2026</h6><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The SysML v2 Plugin 2026x Refresh1 release introduces model validation with customizable validation suites and rules, helping ensure the accuracy, completeness, and consistency of models through automated validation across views and the Textual Editor.</span></p><p>The release expands tabular view capabilities with hierarchical display modes, non-persistent tables, enhanced column and row management, and improved cell editing. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Symbolic views have been enhanced with additional visualization capabilities, including support for displaying traceability patterns such as satisfy, verify, perform, include, and others as paths, along with note support, representation control for templated buttons in custom symbolic view palettes, and improved display of port and parameter information.</span></p><p>Style sheet functionality has been significantly expanded, enabling style sheet creation, application, extraction, and management directly in symbolic views, as well as rule specification through the Specification panel.</p><p>The release also introduces model-based customization of Element Creation and Relationship Creation dialogs, providing greater control over project-specific modeling workflows.</p><p>The Textual Editor delivers substantial performance improvements, enhanced navigation, multiple-file import support, in-editor task notifications, and new environment options.</p><p>The Specification panel has been expanded with an in-line value editor for compartment elements, color selection, element direction management, and other usability enhancements. The Specification Window now also includes search functionality.</p><p>Additional highlights include enhancements to the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Plugin with broader model and diagram coverage, and the introduction of the SysML v2 Open API technology preview.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Downloading installation files</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span> </span></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc86ee14-c8f3-436e-b8a4-d5de71309a65"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>SysML v2 Features</h1><h3>Model Validation</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This release introduces model validation, which helps ensure </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the accuracy, completeness, and consistency of your models. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The modeling tool checks elements against validation rules delivered through SysML, KerML, and Dassault Systèmes predefined validation rules. By automatically identifying modeling issues, validation helps maintain model quality and supports compliance with modeling standards. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=validation&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e2989e9-9a4f-4f18-a6f0-83d4ea52996c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0BCAk6hI4SY&amp;t=1s" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">450</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></p><h4><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Validating Elements in Views</span></span></h4><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Failing elements are highlighted in views, enabling immediate identification of failures directly in the view. You can also invoke the <strong>Validation Results pop-up </strong>via the <strong>Show Validation Results </strong>button in the failing element's smart manipulator. The pop-up displays </span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the element's validation results and provides details explaining the failure, helping you quickly understand the issue</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Reviewing-validation-results&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a><br /></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation Results pop-up.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="885b4a36-ec0e-45b5-b596-9aae9c82ba92"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Additionally, you can review the validation results for all failed elements displayed in the view by opening the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Validation Results panel</strong></span>. You can access it either from the <strong>Validation Results pop-up </strong>or via the <strong>Open Validation Results for the View </strong>button. This provides a consolidated overview of issues within the current view, improving efficiency when addressing multiple validation failures. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Reviewing-validation-results&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span><br /></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validating Elements in Views.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Validating Elements in Textual Editor</h4><p>Textual notation is validated directly in the Textual Editor when the <strong>Run Validation</strong> option is enabled (default). The opened namespace is validated automatically, and failing elements are underlined. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Hovering over an underlined element displays a Validation Results pop-up with details about the failure. The editor scroll bar also includes markers indicating failure locations, with colors reflecting severity. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This enables you to identify and review issues immediately within the Textual Editor, improving efficiency and reducing the need to switch between views. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Reviewing-validation-results&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validating Elements in Textual Editor.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h4>Validating Elements</h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can validate individual elements with their recursive contents using the <strong>Validate </strong>command. The command is available in the element shortcut menu, accessible from both the Containment tree and the element symbol in a view. The command validates the selected element and all elements contained within it, then opens the <strong>Validation Results panel </strong>for review. This enables efficient validation of entire model structures in a single action. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Validating-the-model&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validating Elements.png" /></ac:image></p><h4><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Custom Validation Suites and Rules</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Instead of, or in addition to the predefined validation suites, you can create your own validation suites with custom validation rules. This allows you to tailor model validation to your project-specific modeling standards. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Custom-validation-suites-and-rules&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Custom Validation Suites and Rules.png" /></ac:image></p><h4><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Validation Suites Management</span></h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Additionally, you can enable or disable validation suites for your project through the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Project Validation Suites </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">dialog. The predefined validation suites are enabled by default for all SysML v2 projects, but you can easily disable them and/or activate other suites as needed to match your project needs. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Enabling-validation-suites&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Enabling Validation Suites.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3><span>Tabular Views Improvements</span></h3><h4>Element Display Modes for Tables</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can now view tables in a hierarchical (tree) structure, where rows are organized by ownership. Specify the display mode via the <strong>Options </strong>button in the table toolbar or by redefining the dedicated attributes via the Textual Editor. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Two hierarchical display modes are available:<br /><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Displaying-elements-in-tabular-views&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a><br /></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="f5c48ad5-4b42-428f-948b-578c7424c493"><strong>Complete tree </strong>displays the row elements in a hierarchy where all element owners are represented in separate rows.  </li><li data-uuid="8e37e6b9-d8bf-47f6-922b-827d1cc9c195"><strong>Compact tree</strong><span><span> </span>displays the row elements in a hierarchy where common element owners are grouped in a single row.<br /><br /></span></li></ul><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Display Modes for Tables.png" /></ac:image><br style="text-align: left;" /></span></p><h4>Open in Table Command</h4><p>The newly introduced <strong>Open in Table</strong> command allows you to view the selected element's content in any tabular view available in the active <strong>View Creation </strong>dialog without creating a tabular view in the model. This functionality can be useful when you want to quickly review parts of the model in a tabular view without impacting the model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=open-in-table&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Open in Tables.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Column Configuration Interface</h4><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A new user interface has been introduced to manage table columns. The table toolbar now includes the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Columns </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button, whose dropdown menu displays all existing columns, allowing you to select or deselect them to show or hide table columns. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display hide columns.png" /></ac:image><br /><span style="font-size: 12.0px;font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Columns <span>displayed in the table </span>are highlighted in the Columns dropdown menu, while those that are hidden are not.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="e0398ab8-541c-4264-b3a3-04fc9bea47ee"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Columns </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">dropdown menu also contains a command for opening the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, which allows you to:</span><ul><li data-uuid="f6b4f495-6a2b-4a35-b178-98fb6343ff60"><span>Delete existing columns or create columns from the suggested columns list, which includes meta-features. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configure Table Columns dialog_create column.png" /></ac:image></span><br /></span></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li data-uuid="9ba925a0-c64b-46f6-b21b-1771a709d3a2"><p style="text-align: left;">You can customize the suggested column list in the<span> </span><strong>Configure Table Columns</strong><span> </span>dialog for a specific table (tabular view usage or definition). Indicate a specific metaclass or metadata to make it available in the suggested column list, including its meta-features. The meta-features are grouped under the metaclass/metadata and are available for selection. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Customize the suggested column list.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li data-uuid="80045071-c1da-4d86-ac50-76bb62f319f0"><span>Create custom columns using the <strong>Add Custom Column </strong>button.<span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configure Table Columns dialog_create custom column.png" /></ac:image></span><br /></span></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li data-uuid="2795b929-83de-4c37-acf4-0758737b51b1"><span>Preview custom column expressions. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configure Table Columns dialog_preview expression.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h4>Enhanced ColumnByFeatureView Columns</h4><p><em>ColumnByFeatureView </em>columns<strong> </strong>now support user-defined features. Columns based on user-defined features are editable when their values are of primitive types (String, Integer, Real, or Boolean). <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This provides greater flexibility when creating tables and enables more efficient editing of column cell values directly within the view. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Enhanced ColumnByFeatureView Columns.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Table Row Management</h4><p><span>The tabular view toolbar has been supplemented with new buttons, providing convenient table element management:</span></p><ul><li><span>The <strong>Add New </strong>button displays a dropdown menu for selecting an element to be created in the model and displayed in the table. The tool automatically adjusts the element selection to match the table’s filters. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-rows&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li></ul><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Table Row Management Add New Button.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li data-uuid="01b776ce-0495-4370-b323-399827b201d3"><span>Although the project’s active Element Creation dialog is used to suggest elements in the <strong>Add New</strong> element selection dropdown menu, you can customize it by specifying a custom Element Creation dialog for a specific table. The elements you specify for the dropdown menu are also made available as the suggested columns in the <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog, including the meta-features of the specified metaclass/metadata. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-rows&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="689cd598-a8aa-4478-a897-dda52bc3b010"><span>The <strong>Delete </strong>button deletes the selected row elements from the table and the model. </span>The command is also available via the table cell shortcut menu<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-rows&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li><li data-uuid="5aa6acc1-c695-485b-834d-44ee57a31977"><span>The <strong>Remove From Table </strong>button hides the selected row elements from the table. This is achieved by filtering out the selected element from the table. </span>The command is also available via the table cell shortcut menu. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-rows&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li></ul><h4>Table Cell Value Management</h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">From now on, you can easily manage table cell values. Invoke </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Review and Modify</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog from the table cell to review, rename, create, delete, reorder, and filter cell values. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-cells&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="19538126-50eb-4ffc-ba00-dccc1db3cfa5">Although multi-valued cells display up to 10 values by default, you can modify this limit via the <em>maxDisplayedValues</em> attribute. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-cells&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="maxDisplayedValues attribute.png" /></ac:image></p><h4><span>Other Tabular Views Improvements</span></h4><ul><li data-uuid="4bd1a778-c1f5-4e53-acd1-52a22e8669ef"><p><span>The tabular view's <strong>column header shortcut menu </strong>has been enhanced with new commands for improved table column management:</span></p><ul><li><span>The <strong>Pin Column </strong></span><span>command allows you to pin the column to the left or the right side of the table. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li><li><span>The <strong>Hide Column </strong></span><span>command hides the selected column from the table's diagrammatic representation without removing it from the model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li><li data-uuid="33c40119-c619-4bb1-bc42-0d41f449834e">The <strong>Delete Column </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command deletes the selected column from the table and the model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Modeling-tabular-view-columns&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li><li data-uuid="9629688e-1c5c-4842-a672-cd762e023755"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Open in Textual Editor </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command selects the column in the Containment tree and opens the Textual Editor tab, highlighting the selected column. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-textual-editor&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a><br /></span></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="column header shortcut menu.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><span>Additional commands have been added to the <strong>table cell shortcut menu</strong> for easier table element management:</span><ul><li data-uuid="bb3c90b0-abe4-4737-9bef-9810ad92ba6b"><span>The <strong>Select in Containment Tree </strong>command selects the row element or the cell value in the Containment tree. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-rows&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="d9077c73-d128-48b7-baed-1b9a4fd21547"><span>The <strong>Specification </strong>command opens the Specification panel for the row element or the cell value. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-rows&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table cell shortcut menu.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="f01cdb15-fd47-47f2-a9c6-8d666ef23303">You can now<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> add elements to tables by dragging and dropping</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> elements from the Containment tree or element symbols from views onto the diagrammatic representation of a tabular view. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Managing-tabular-view-rows&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li><li data-uuid="06131401-2e9b-40cc-8381-ac00e7e3620f"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Tabular views are now limited to displaying up to 50,000 rows. If building a table exceeds this limit, the tool displays a notification suggesting that you narrow the table scope or adjust the display mode settings and try again. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Displaying-elements-in-tabular-views&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Symbolic Views Improvements</h3><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span>Representation Control for Templated Buttons in Custom Symbolic View Palette</span></h4><p>When creating custom symbolic views with custom view palettes, you can now define the representation of elements created via custom templated buttons. The new representation attribute allows you to specify how elements are displayed in the view (for example, as a shape, path, n-ary path, or shape on border). <span>This enables a more precise view customization to match specific modeling needs. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Creating-custom-symbolic-view-definitions&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Representation Control for Templated Buttons in Custom Symbolic View Palette.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h4>Reference As Path</h4><p class="p1">In SysML v1, traceability relationships such as satisfy, verify, allocate (behavioral), and others were modeled as specialized dependencies between model elements. In SysML v2, these traceability aspects are expressed differently - using a dedicated usage element (e.g., satisfy requirement or perform action) combined with a reference-subsetting relationship to the target element (such as a requirement or action). For example, 'satisfy' is represented by a satisfy requirement usage, where the satisfying element is implied by its owner or subject value, and the satisfied requirement is linked via reference-subsetting. This release adds support for rendering these SysML v2 traceability patterns as paths for event, perform, include, exhibit, satisfy, verify, assume, require, assert, frame, and render. You can now create these elements as paths via the view palette and display them using the dedicated <strong>Display References</strong> command.</p><p class="p1" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Reference As Path.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Notes in Views</h4><p>Notes are now fully supported in SysML v2 views. They can be created directly from the view palette's <strong>Common</strong> section and displayed in a view. Notes can also be attached to symbols, preserving contextual annotations in the model.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Notes in Views.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Show Parameter/Port Features in Tooltips</h4><p class="p1">Parameters/Ports on the shape border are rendered as compact symbols, which leaves too little space to show their details directly in the view. This release brings SysML v1-style usability to SysML v2 parameters/ports by displaying nested elements on demand. Hovering over a parameter/port symbol displays a tooltip showing both owned and inherited features, including attributes and items, so you can quickly understand the port’s interface without cluttering the view.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show Parameter Port Features in Tooltips.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Style Sheets Improvements</h3><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad4081e7-2ff3-4aec-b89e-cce4d3520512"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://youtu.be/UsKmqI3P91Y" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">450</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4>Style Sheet Management in Symbolic Views</h4><p>You can now create, apply, extract, and display style sheets directly in symbolic views. The <strong>Style Sheets</strong> command menu in the view toolbar now allows you to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="092a1a1e-6e6e-4deb-a275-34d5794e3e99">Display the style sheets applied to a symbolic view, making it clear which styling rules drive the visualization. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=applying-style-sheets&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li data-uuid="4645faa6-d51c-4f11-964b-d53bb08ae7ad">Create and apply a new style sheet directly from the view, enabling a simplified and efficient workflow. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=creating-style-sheets&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li data-uuid="49dd494c-ea49-4a99-9c92-5be0712ebdba">Automatically extract style sheets based on the content currently displayed in the view, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="63d8c3a2-22c6-409d-8b44-d4a9ce457e1f">including applied metadata, metadata feature values, specialized library elements, and library element feature values.</ac:inline-comment-marker> This helps capture consistent styling rules with minimal manual effort. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=extracting-style-sheets&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Style Sheet Management in Symbolic Views.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 class="p1">Style Sheet Rule Specification Through the Specification Panel</h4><p class="p1">The Specification panel now makes it easier to review and work with style sheet rules. You can now specify the condition and style attributes of a style sheet rule directly in the Specification panel.</p><ul><li class="p1" data-uuid="b20f0a90-9f50-4487-8a08-87921528ab89">Easily specify the rule condition as an explicit element, element kind, metadata annotation, view type, or custom expression. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=specifying-a-condition-for-a-rule&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li class="p1" data-uuid="93ff8af9-78c6-4752-bc2a-b3e98c126b62">Customize styles in the Style Attributes section by specifying the required style definitions and modifying owned or inherited style attributes. You can redefine them and assign values using the same workflow as for any inherited SysML v2 attribute. For color properties, a built-in color picker is available for quickly selecting and applying the desired color value. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=specifying-styles-for-a-rule&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Style Sheet Rule Specification Through the Specification Panel.png" /></ac:image></p><p class="p1" style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p class="p1" style="text-align: right;"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Creation Dialogs Customization</h3><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24cdf482-d183-4032-be1b-0672c5b1b748"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://youtu.be/1DV64W03MAc" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">450</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4><span>Model-Based Relationship Creation Dialog</span></h4><p><span>You can now model a custom, model-based Relationship Creation dialog that lists only the relationship creation commands</span><span> of your choice.</span></p><p>There are two options for customizing the Relationship Creation dialog:</p><ul><li data-uuid="0226491e-8e30-4a43-9f69-d0c1cf57b415"><p>Customize the predefined <em style="text-align: left;">SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog </em>configuration in the <em>SysML Customization</em> model library. This allows you to inherit its configuration and tailor the Relationship Creation dialog as needed. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Customizing-Relationship-Creation-dialog&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p></li><li data-uuid="eeff08d5-55da-4a0c-9ae5-5ec6cd16d577"><p>Create a Relationship Creation dialog from scratch <span>via the conceptual <em>RelationshipCreationDialog </em>definition</span>. This gives you full control of designing the dialog entirely according to your own needs, without relying on predefined settings. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Creating-Relationship-Creation-dialog&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p></li></ul><p>With either approach, you can define a model-based Relationship Creation dialog that includes only the commands you need, as well as specifying how they are structured into custom command categories. This enables full customization of the dialog to match the needs of your specific project.  </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Model-Based Relationship Creation Dialog.png" /></ac:image><br />The image on the left shows the Relationship Creation dialog according to the predefined <em>SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog</em> configuration. The image on the right shows the customized Relationship Creation submenu.  </h6><h4><span>Relationship Creation Dialog Management</span></h4><p><span>Additionally, you can activate different Relationship Creation dialog configurations for your project via the <strong>Active Create Relationship Dialog</strong>. Use the <strong>Manage Create Relationship Dialog</strong> command from the configuration element shortcut menu or the <strong>Active Relationship Creation Dialog</strong> project option. While the predefined <em style="text-align: left;">SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog</em> configuration is enabled by default for all SysML v2 projects, you can easily activate another configuration. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Activating-Relationship-Creation-dialog&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Activating Relationship Creation dialog.png" /></ac:image><br />The Manage Create Relationship Dialog command invokes the Active Create Relationship Dialog, where the custom Relationship Creation dialog's <em>CustomizedRelationshipCreationDialog</em> configuration is activated for the project.</h6><h4>Model-Based Element Creation Dialog</h4><p><span>You can now model a custom, model-based Element Creation dialog that lists only the element creation commands</span><span> of your choice.</span></p><p>There are two options for customizing the Element Creation dialog:</p><ul><li data-uuid="206b31ef-6257-4932-a857-4e583e69ddae"><p>Customize the predefined <em style="text-align: left;">SysMLElementCreationDialog </em>configuration in the <em>SysML Customization</em> model library. This allows you to inherit its configuration and tailor the Element Creation dialog as needed. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Customizing-Element-Creation-dialog&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p></li><li data-uuid="42ea2d72-a7c0-4863-b454-0f7c3e572173"><p>Create an Element Creation dialog from scratch <span>via the conceptual <em>ElementCreationDialog </em>definition</span>. This gives you full control of designing the dialog entirely according to your own needs, without relying on predefined settings. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Creating-Element-Creation-dialog&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p></li></ul><p>With either approach, you can define a model-based Element Creation dialog that includes only the commands you need, as well as specifying how they are structured into custom command categories. This enables full customization of the dialog to match the needs of your specific project.  </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1123"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Model-Based Element Creation Dialog.png" /></ac:image><br />The image on the left shows the Element Creation submenu according to the predefined <em>SysMLElementCreationDialog</em> configuration. The image on the right shows the custom Element Creation submenu.  </h6><h4><span>Element Creation Dialog Management</span></h4><p><span>Additionally, you can activate different Element Creation dialog configurations for your project via the <strong>Active Create Element Dialog</strong>. Use the <strong>Manage Create Element Dialog</strong> command from the configuration element shortcut menu or the <strong>Active Element Creation Dialog</strong> project option. While the predefined <em style="text-align: left;">SysMLElementCreationDialog</em> configuration is enabled by default for all SysML v2 projects, you can easily activate another configuration. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Activating-Element-Creation-dialog&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Activating Element Creation dialog.png" /></ac:image><br />The Manage Create Element Dialog command invokes the Active Create Element Dialog, where the custom Element Creation dialog's <em>NewCustomElementCreationDialog</em> configuration is activated for the project.</h6><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>Textual Editor Improvements</h3><h4>In-Editor Notifications for Background Tasks</h4><p>When a longer-running background task is in progress, a notification is displayed directly in the Textual Editor. While the notification is active, synchronization and context switching between the Textual Editor and other views are temporarily disabled to prevent conflicting actions. This helps maintain model consistency and prevents unintended user interactions during background processing.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="In-Editor Notifications for Background Tasks.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Multiple File Import</h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The SysML v2 textual notation import now supports importing multiple .</span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">sysml</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> files at once, as well as entire directories. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This streamlines bulk model import and simplifies working with modularized textual models. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Textual-notation-import-export&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Multiple File Import.png" /></ac:image></p><h4><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select in Containment Tree and Cross-Highlighting When the Editor is Not Synchronized</span></h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The Select in Containment Tree command now works even when the Textual Editor is not synchronized with the model. If an element is modified outside the Textual Editor (for example, renamed in the Containment tree), you can still target and select the correct element using the command. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Similarly, selecting an element in the Containment tree highlights the corresponding range in the Textual Editor, provided the element exists in both. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This ensures reliable navigation and element targeting across views, even if the Textual Editor is not synchronized. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Textual-Editor-features&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select in Containment Tree and Cross-Highlighting When the Editor is Not Synchronized.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Textual Editor Performance Improvements</h4><p>2026x Refresh1 delivers significant performance improvements in the Textual Editor:</p><ul><li data-uuid="aaf6c704-7ed0-44a9-9e2d-0684f0ffcd5a">Synchronization, i.e., the creation or update of the persistent model from the Textual Editor.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Textual Editor Performance Improvements Synchronization.png" /></ac:image>Full synchronization is done when a full model is added to an empty Textual Editor and saved.<br />Incremental synchronization is done when the existing model is edited and saved in the Textual Editor.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="3bdd92c3-6a9b-4edf-9e08-389e8699f3eb"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Textual Editor opening time.</span> </li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Textual Editor Performance Improvements Open Textual Editor.png" /></ac:image><br />The Textual Editor opening time comprises model-to-text and text-to-model time. Model-to-text covers the transformation from the SysML v2 model to textual notation. <br />Text-to-model covers the creation of an in-memory transient model from textual notation.<br /> Model-to-text with persistent qualified names means the qualified names are available and can be used during the generation. <br />Model-to-text with generated qualified names means persistent qualified names are not available and must be generated.</h6><h4>Other Textual Editor Improvements</h4><ul><li data-uuid="a7d93a6f-6b37-49cf-9d41-8930312619f5">A new environment option, <strong>Enable Minimap</strong>, has been introduced, allowing you to choose whether the minimap should be shown in the Textual Editor. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Environment-options&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li data-uuid="499e03cc-a1b7-4c69-a97e-a54df790a5b0">Name resolution performance. A new environment option, <strong>Name Resolution Mode</strong>, allows you to select the desired resolution mode:<ul><li><span>'Fast' for maximum speed with rare corner-case inaccuracies. </span></li><li>'Balanced' for a reliable compromise between performance and accuracy. </li><li>'Full Precision' for guaranteed accuracy with slower execution.<br /><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Environment-options&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span>Specification Panel Improvements</span></h3><h4 style="text-align: left;">In-Line Value Editor for Compartment Elements</h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Attributes and ref elements displayed in compartments now support in-line value display and editing in addition to showing their declared names. You can modify values directly within the compartment without navigating to the element itself. Inherited elements are automatically redefined when edited. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This simplifies value management and speeds up editing of owned and inherited elements. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=specification-panel&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="In-Line Value Editor For Compartment Elements.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Search Functionality in the Specification Window</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The Specification window now includes a search bar, enabling quick and easy search for element properties. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=specification-window&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Search Functionality in the Specification window.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">Color Picker Component</h4><p>Color-typed element values can now be specified directly in the Specification panel using a dedicated color picker in the value editor field. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This provides a more intuitive and precise way to define color values</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=specification-panel&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Color Picker Component.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">Element Direction Management</h4><p>The Direction property for item, attribute, and ref elements can now be specified directly in the Specification panel. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This streamlines property management and reduces the need for additional navigation.</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=specification-panel&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Direction Management.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">Other Specification Panel Improvements</h4><ul><li data-uuid="ff96b3c0-f169-44bc-a5bc-9a7a3f841497"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><strong>Open elements and expressions in the Textual Editor</strong>. </span>You can now open the Textual Editor directly from the Specification panel's Navigation bar. You can also open it for compartment elements, elements specified in the Type field, and Result Expressions. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-textual-editor&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li data-uuid="8759464e-2461-472e-9c8e-631e0407d88a"><strong>Single-type unspecification</strong>. When multiple types are set for an element, you can now unspecify a single type without affecting the others. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=specification-panel&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li data-uuid="583eb5df-f98c-4a1a-b826-cb1696741651"><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Value editor improvements</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The Value field now uses an editor appropriate to the specified attribute definition's type. <span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=specification-panel&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li><li data-uuid="8f2d0acb-9c7c-45e5-8b54-23b8168c75fd"><span><strong>Responsive Documentation and Expression fields</strong>. The Documentation and Result Expression fields automatically adjust to their content (up to five lines) for improved readability.</span></li><li data-uuid="dcaafeab-b60b-4334-819f-12a0d053a318"><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Metadata compartment visibility</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The Metadata compartment is now always displayed when at least one metadata definition is applied to an element.</span></li><li data-uuid="7e83e2cc-ebde-4e1a-ba19-3b906073a003"><strong>Compact hints</strong>. Hints are now more compact and no longer occupy unnecessary space in the Specification panel.</li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>Local Repository Performance Improvements</h3><p>The 2026x Refresh1 release brings significant improvements to project-related operations in the local repository. You can now create, open, and save local projects much faster.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="local_repository_operations.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Comparison of local repository operation performance between 2026x HF1 and 2026x Refresh1.</h6><h3>SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Improvements</h3><p>The SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Plugin has been enhanced to migrate symbolic diagrams into corresponding SysML v2 views. Diagrams for Requirements, Use Cases, States, and other symbolic diagram types are now automatically transformed into their SysML v2 view equivalents. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">All symbolic view types and symbols currently supported by the CAMEO SysML v2 solution are included, ensuring improved visual and structural continuity between SysML v1 and SysML v2 models.</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Element order is now preserved during migration. This maintains the original model structure, improves traceability, and significantly reduces the need for manual reordering after migration.</span></p><p>Additionally, the migration plugin now supports: </p><ul><li data-uuid="4e86ff1a-a231-4e37-9bb2-de8a834bcf69"><p><strong>States migration</strong> – State elements and related structures are migrated to SysML v2, improving support for state-based behavioral models.</p></li><li data-uuid="1a75da37-8a17-4ddf-81b8-ab37f17b1937"><p><strong>Parametric models migration</strong> – <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">M</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">igration now supports parametric elements, including </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">constraint blocks, parameters, properties, and constraints.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="9c9f89c8-3316-4767-b40f-7e3c08c633ad"><p><strong>Trace relationships migration</strong> – Migrated as SysML v2 dependencies.</p></li><li data-uuid="7f29bea4-902b-4c7b-b1d5-a9b26f6c8a2c"><p><strong>Stereotypes and primitive tagged values migration.</strong></p></li><li data-uuid="b3e2bb6e-9ce5-4d91-8f5e-aea0599b6abf"><p><strong>Variables migration.</strong></p></li><li data-uuid="6901b654-ee05-4bca-9c7a-b74f50df85d2"><p><strong>TimeExpression, TimeEvent, and ChangeEvent migration.</strong></p></li><li data-uuid="28b67585-ac0b-45a7-9c0c-d7e776f677b8"><p><strong>Allocation relationships migration</strong> – Logical, functional, and physical mappings are retained.</p></li><li data-uuid="ee23ab50-ad11-41fa-b352-f2eb29e97af7"><p><strong>Behavioral elements migration</strong> – Opaque behaviors and opaque actions are supported.</p></li><li data-uuid="43c3b166-4143-42aa-b880-e70e805fdbe2"><p><strong>Object flows migration</strong> – Object flows from Activity parameter nodes are supported.</p></li><li data-uuid="85875ec1-88ae-4f1e-8c98-8da9ce676b3f"><p><strong>Units migration (core support)</strong> – Commonly used units, including core SI units, are mapped.<br /><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=Migration-from-SysML-v1-to-v2&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1317"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Improvements.png" /></ac:image><br />The image displays symbolic diagram migration for SysML Parametric and State Machine diagrams.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h3>SysML v2 Open API</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The SysML v2 Open API is now available as a technology preview, providing programmatic access to key SysML v2 modeling capabilities. As SysML v2 support continues to evolve, the Open API should be considered preview functionality and may be refined or changed in future releases. We encourage you to explore the API and share your feedback as this capability continues to mature.</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/DEVG/?contextKey=SysML-v2&amp;version=2026x Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span>Other SysML v2 Plugin Improvements</span></h3><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><span><strong>Dynamic <em>baseType</em> evaluation for semantic metadata</strong>. The <em>baseType</em> of semantic metadata can now be specified using complex expressions. The expression is evaluated after the metadata is applied, and the returned type is then used as the base type. This is important for advanced metadata library development and usage, such as the next-generation UAF.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="2c9505f3-ff17-4f89-a68b-ae07ea10516c"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=sysml-v2-plugin-documentation&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">SysML v2 Plugin 2026x Refresh1</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180796 space=VN version=18 -->
## PAGE 00220: (2026x Refresh1) SysML v2 Simulation Plugin

- page_id: `314180796`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314180796/2026x+Refresh1+SysML+v2+Simulation+Plugin
- version_number: 18
- version_date: `2026-06-30T09:47:59.349+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

The 2026x Refresh1 release introduces the Technology Preview version of the new SysML v2 Simulation Plugin, which enables execution of SysML v2 models for behavioral verification against system requirements and early validation of operational concepts. It supports state execution, structure execution, including dynamic interaction between system elements, and automated requirements verification.

##### Supported Structural and Connectivity Elements

The SysML v2 Simulation Plugin supports execution of key SysML v2 structural and connectivity elements, allowing engineers****to seamlessly simulate dynamic interactions between complex system components.

Supported elements include:

- Parts : Elements defining system structure and decomposition.
- Ports : Interaction points between system components.
- Connections : Links between structural elements.
- Interfaces : Specification of interactions between components.
- Flows : Value transfers defined within interfaces.
- Attributes : Typed properties of system elements.
- Enumerations : Discrete value sets used in system modeling.

Interfaces support automatic value propagation across connected ports and features, while binding connections ensure primitive attribute values remain consistent between linked features throughout simulation execution.

These capabilities enable SysML v2 models to be executed as fully interconnected system architectures, allowing engineers to verify structural designs through simulated interactions between system elements.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=supported-structural-and-connectivity-elements&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Water tank system simulation

##### State Simulation

The simulation engine supports SysML v2 state execution, allowing you to analyze and visualize behaviors defined by states. You can use the SysML v2 Simulation Plugin to run states directly from the model and observe how states, transitions, triggers, and actions behave dynamically. During execution, active states are highlighted and transitions are fired according to the precise semantic rules of the model, providing an immediate visual trace of system behavior.

State execution enables you to:

- Verify state transitions and guard logic
- Observe how payloads and events propagate between elements
- Identify issues in behavioral models before implementation
- Demonstrate system behavior interactively
- Validate system behavior early in the design process

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=simulating-states&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of annotating the state simulation in a model view.

##### Simulation Information in Model Views

During simulation, model views display runtime information associated with model elements. This information is dynamically updated during execution and reflects the current state of the simulated system, making it easier to understand how the system evolves over time.

**Supported capabilities include:**

- Simulation annotations in views Model elements in views are annotated during execution to reflect simulation progress. Elements are marked as Active (currently executing), Visited (already executed), or Last Visited (most recently executed).
- Displaying runtime values Runtime values are displayed in blue and update continuously as execution progresses. These values replace default values and are displayed next to attributes, references, and satisfy requirements.
- Automated requirement verification Model simulation supports automated requirement verification. Unsatisfied requirements are highlighted in red, while satisfied requirements are highlighted in green. Tooltips display requirement details when hovering over elements associated with failing requirements.

These capabilities enable you to observe simulation results directly in model views, providing immediate feedback on system behavior and supporting early verification of both system behavior and requirements.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=displaying-simulation-information&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying simulation information in a model view.

##### Manipulating Simulation Information

You can interact with the simulation data displayed in views to improve understanding and analysis of system behavior during execution. Runtime information can be modified without stopping the simulation, allowing you to observe how the system responds to changing conditions. During simulation, you can use model views to:

- Change runtime values You can modify initial runtime values (marked with “:=”) directly in model views during simulation. Runtime values can be edited based on their type: numerical values can be entered directly, enumeration values can be selected from a list, and Boolean values can be toggled within the view. Changes are applied automatically and reflected in the simulation results in real time. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Send payloads from transitions If supported, payloads can be triggered directly from transition symbols in a view by clicking the**Fire Transition**icon. This allows transitions to be fired manually during simulation to observe the resulting system behavior. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=manipulating-simulation-information&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

##### Time Series Chart

The **Variables** panel of the **SysML v2 Simulation** window provides the ability to visualize numerical variable values over time during simulation using a Time Series Chart. The chart displays time-series data for selected attributes and references and updates as the simulation progresses. In an open Time Series Chart, you can:

- Add new variables Additional numerical variables can be added to an open chart during simulation, allowing combined analysis of multiple value trends in a single visualization.
- Configure chart display options Chart visualization can be adjusted to improve readability. Options include linear interpolation, showing or hiding data points, and displaying or hiding numeric values on the chart.
- Export chart data Time Series Chart data can be exported for external use in formats including PNG, JPEG, SVG, CSV, and XLS. Exported data can be used for analysis, reporting, or documentation purposes.

Time Series Charts provide deeper insight into system behavior and enable easier communication of simulation results through export to various formats.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=time-series-chart&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Time Series Chart for the water tank system simulation.

##### Exporting Simulation Data to CSV

You can export simulation data from the **Variables** panel of the **SysML v2 Simulation** windowfor further analysis outside the modeling tool environment. Exported data is saved in CSV format and can be opened in standard spreadsheet or data analysis tools.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=exporting-simulation-data-to-csv&version=2026x%20Refresh1)

##### Documentation

- SysML v2 Simulation Plugin 2026x Refresh1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: June 26, 2026</h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The 2026x Refresh1 release introduces the Technology Preview version of the new SysML v2 Simulation Plugin, which enables execution of SysML v2 models <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">for behavioral verification against system requirements and early validation of operational concepts. It supports state execution, structure execution, including dynamic interaction between system elements, and automated requirements verification.</span></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 class="isSelectedEnd">Supported Structural and Connectivity Elements</h3><p class="isSelectedEnd"><span>The SysML v2 Simulation Plugin supports execution of key SysML v2 structural and connectivity elements<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, allowing engineers</span><strong style="text-align: left;"> </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">to seamlessly simulate dynamic interactions between complex system components.</span></span></p><p class="isSelectedEnd">Supported elements include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="ee5bd561-eefd-461e-bdf9-3aa5b0ff4da5"><strong><span>Parts</span></strong><span>: Elements defining system structure and decomposition.</span></li><li data-uuid="0f91e4ca-00b2-4b95-aebc-9572b3f50015"><strong><span>Ports</span></strong><span>: Interaction points between system components.</span></li><li data-uuid="c22917f6-2d91-4c1b-8d18-b22ac9f90fa8"><strong><span>Connections</span></strong><span>: Links between structural elements.</span></li><li data-uuid="1891278f-e94c-4065-91b3-ac3fe1156cc3"><strong><span>Interfaces</span></strong><span>: Specification of interactions between components.</span></li><li data-uuid="9d7cf264-0ce9-4d57-8109-c3a5b0af8de4"><strong><span>Flows</span></strong><span>: Value transfers defined within interfaces.</span></li><li data-uuid="d2d2a0cc-d6fd-4482-bdd1-3520966f3618"><strong><span>Attributes</span></strong><span>: Typed properties of system elements.</span></li><li data-uuid="a75ef304-c040-4559-9450-e53814c52db1"><strong><span>Enumerations</span></strong><span>: Discrete value sets used in system modeling.</span></li></ul><p class="isSelectedEnd"><span>Interfaces support automatic value propagation across connected ports and features, while binding connections ensure primitive attribute values remain consistent between linked features throughout simulation execution.</span></p><p><span>These capabilities enable SysML v2 models to be executed <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">as fully interconnected system architectures, allowing engineers to verify structural designs through simulated interactions between system elements.</span></span></p><p><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=supported-structural-and-connectivity-elements&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="water_tank_simulation.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Water tank system simulation</h6><h3><span>State Simulation</span></h3><p class="isSelectedEnd"><span>The simulation engine supports SysML v2 state execution, allowing you to analyze and visualize behaviors defined by <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8c1c0be9-b911-4a7a-a31d-60a3010ce348">states</ac:inline-comment-marker>. You can use the SysML v2 Simulation Plugin to run states directly from the model and observe how states, transitions, triggers, and actions behave dynamically. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">During execution, active states are highlighted and transitions are fired according to the precise semantic rules of the model, providing an immediate visual trace of system behavior.</span></span></p><p class="isSelectedEnd"><span>State execution enables you to:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="8c1202c6-51e6-463b-a76f-764a98aa3dd7"><span>Verify state transitions and guard logic</span></li><li data-uuid="88ad0f34-7371-4ebe-acb9-5bf78db6daf6"><span>Observe how payloads and events propagate between elements</span></li><li data-uuid="811d91e8-3977-4303-b968-f427d4371b0e"><span>Identify issues in behavioral models before implementation</span></li><li data-uuid="75b3c03b-4684-49cd-a023-fef7b528bc97"><span>Demonstrate system behavior interactively</span></li><li data-uuid="d4a9384e-128a-44ab-8e87-46a55a49d186"><span>Validate system behavior early in the design process</span></li></ul><p><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=simulating-states&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulation_annotations.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Example of annotating the state simulation in a model view.</span></h6><h3><span>Simulation Information in Model Views</span></h3><p class="isSelectedEnd"><span>During simulation, model views display runtime information associated with model elements. This information is dynamically updated during execution and reflects the current state of the simulated system, making it easier to understand how the system evolves over time.</span></p><p class="isSelectedEnd"><strong><span>Supported capabilities include:</span></strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="423a5b04-eb4f-4054-a3b6-0b3c2b378c37"><strong><span>Simulation annotations in views</span></strong><br /><span>Model elements in views are annotated during execution to reflect simulation progress. Elements are marked as Active (currently executing), Visited (already executed), or Last Visited (most recently executed).</span></li><li data-uuid="a663385d-8dce-4baa-99dd-d334fc5fea53"><strong><span>Displaying runtime values</span></strong><br /><span>Runtime values are displayed in blue and update continuously as execution progresses. These values replace default values and are displayed next to attributes, references, and satisfy requirements.</span></li><li data-uuid="314e057f-4cfb-4da3-ac57-71849955ecf7"><strong><span>Automated requirement verification</span></strong><br /><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Model simulation supports automated requirement verification. Unsatisfied requirements are highlighted in red, while satisfied requirements are highlighted in green. Tooltips display requirement details when hovering over elements associated with failing requirements.</span></span></li></ul><p><span>These capabilities enable you to observe simulation results directly in model views, providing immediate feedback on system behavior and supporting early <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cc3eb1ab-890b-4245-8b49-0803087fc9f5">verification</ac:inline-comment-marker> of both system behavior and requirements.</span></p><p><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=displaying-simulation-information&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="runtime_values_in_views.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Displaying simulation information in a model view.</span></h6><h3><span>Manipulating Simulation Information</span></h3><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can interact with the simulation data displayed in views to improve understanding and analysis of system behavior during execution. R</span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">untime information can be modified without stopping the simulation, allowing you to observe how the system responds to changing conditions. During simulation, you can use model views to:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="f9ec071f-a80e-42d3-8819-c6b5ce0dbf87"><strong><span>Change runtime values</span></strong><br /><span>You can modify initial runtime values (marked with “:=”) directly in model views during simulation. Runtime values can be edited based on their type: numerical values can be entered directly, enumeration values can be selected from a list, and Boolean values can be toggled within the view. Changes are applied automatically and reflected in the simulation results in real time.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modifying_runtime_values_based_on_type.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="106f5551-a3d9-4f4e-a45e-b568c9c20c2d"><strong><span>Send payloads from transitions</span></strong><br /><span>If supported, payloads can be triggered directly from transition symbols in a view by clicking <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Fire Transition</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">icon</span>. This allows transitions to be fired manually during simulation to observe the resulting system behavior.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="firing_transition.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul><p><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=manipulating-simulation-information&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Time Series Chart</h3><p class="isSelectedEnd"><span>The <strong>Variables</strong> panel of the <strong>SysML v2 Simulation</strong> window provides the ability to visualize numerical variable values over time during simulation using a Time Series Chart. The chart displays time-series data for selected attributes and references and updates as the simulation progresses. In an open Time Series Chart, you can:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="cdb4a0c2-430c-4bb6-a105-505be6830bea"><strong><span>Add new variables</span></strong><br /><span>Additional numerical variables can be added to an open chart during simulation, allowing combined analysis of multiple value trends in a single visualization.</span></li><li data-uuid="562470bf-a6d6-4439-92ac-d7959627f071"><strong><span>Configure chart display options</span></strong><br /><span>Chart visualization can be adjusted to improve readability. Options include linear interpolation, showing or hiding data points, and displaying or hiding numeric values on the chart.</span></li><li data-uuid="ae970ebf-bd14-4fbe-9c31-689441303a44"><strong><span>Export chart data</span></strong><br /><span>Time Series Chart data can be exported for external use in formats including PNG, JPEG, SVG, CSV, and XLS. Exported data can be used for analysis, reporting, or documentation purposes.</span></li></ul><p><span>Time Series Charts provide deeper insight into system behavior and enable easier communication of simulation results through export to various formats.</span></p><p><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=time-series-chart&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="time_series_chart_window.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Time Series Chart for the water tank system simulation.</span></h6><h3><span>Exporting Simulation Data to CSV</span></h3><p>You can export simulation data from the <span><strong>Variables</strong> panel of the <strong>SysML v2 Simulation</strong> window </span>for further analysis outside the modeling tool environment. Exported data is saved in CSV format and can be opened in standard spreadsheet or data analysis tools.</p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=exporting-simulation-data-to-csv&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="3c9382f9-18ac-4535-9e24-77e24aa681eb"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=sysml-v2-simulation-plugin-documentation&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">SysML v2 Simulation Plugin 2026x Refresh1</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180361 space=VN version=8 -->
## PAGE 00221: (2026x Refresh1) Systems Cybersecurity Designer

- page_id: `314180361`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314180361/2026x+Refresh1+Systems+Cybersecurity+Designer
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-06-26T10:48:01.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

#### Attack Path Window Enhancements

In this release, a few enhancements were implemented for the Attack Path window:

- Diagram selection. Now you can select multiple IBDs at a time.
- Shortcut menu. You can use shortcut menu to open the Specification of a selected diagram, n avigate (e.g. go to related elements), and select the chosen diagram in the Containment Tree or in Structure Tree.
- **Selecting usages.** If an element is reused by multiple Part Properties, you will be selecting its usages as Attack Patch steps, not the element itself.

#### Other Enhancements

- You can change the highlight colors for the Attack Paths in the Environment options. The default highlight representation settings are:

- 
  - **Orange** path highlight for connections and part properties
  - **Green** highlight for security measures part properties
- Security Asset and Security Measure stereotypes can be adorned with small icons. You can choose wether the icons should be displayed in the **Environment**options dialog (**Options**>**Environment**>**Cybesecurity**>**Show Icons in Containment Tree**).
- The Single Block Item crfeation is now available from the shortcut menu. A new **Add as an Item** shortcut menu command is now available under the Security when you right-clicking a «Block» in a SysML Block Definition Diagram.
- You can add a Lower Level Attak Patch for the selectedAttack Pathelement. Right-click a selected Attach Path element and from the there shortcut menu, choose**Security**>**Add Lower Level Attack Path**.
- You can add a Lower Level Threat Scenario for the selectedThreat Scenarioelement. Rught-click a selected Threat Scenario element and from the shortcut menu, choose**Security**>**Add Lower Level Threat Scenario**.
- Now, when generating the Attack Paths, the Attack Potential Attack Paths are created.
- The computation of score, rating and Level of Threat has been enhanced.
- Vulnerability related enchancements have been implemented.

##### Documentation

- Systems Cybersecurity Designer 2026x Refresh1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

