The default highlight representation settings are:</span></li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="eeb89cb2-ee17-44a7-a2c3-c44eb3e57287"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-orange-subtler-pressed,#fea362);"><strong>Orange</strong></span><span> </span>path highlight for connections and part properties</li><li data-uuid="278aa34b-a03c-4e63-b513-3e7e5496f9fb"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-warning-bold,#f5cd47);"><span style="color:var(--ds-icon-success,#22a06b);"><strong>Green</strong></span> </span>highlight for security measures part properties</li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>Security Asset and Security Measure stereotypes can be adorned with small icons. You can choose wether the icons should be displayed in the <strong>Environment </strong>options dialog <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(</span><strong style="text-align: left;">Options<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&gt;<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Environment<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&gt;<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Cybesecurity<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&gt;<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Show Icons in Containment Tree</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">)</span>. </span></li><li data-uuid="4d443c9e-31ae-4c1b-a028-87a5516ccaf6">The Single Block Item crfeation is now available from the shortcut menu. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A new <strong>Add as an Item</strong> shortcut menu command is now available under the Security when you right-clicking a «Block» in a SysML Block Definition Diagram.</span></li><li data-uuid="978bbc71-b8f2-46ea-99de-b17b558a8d1a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can add a Lower Level Attak Patch for the selected<span> </span></span>Attack Path<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> element. Right-click a selected Attach Path element and from the  there shortcut menu, choose<span> </span></span><strong>Security</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> &gt;</span><span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Add Lower Level Attack Path</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="978bbc71-b8f2-46ea-99de-b17b558a8d1a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can add a Lower Level Threat Scenario for the selected </span>Threat Scenario<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>element. Rught-click a selected Threat Scenario element and from the shortcut menu, choose </span><strong>Security</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Add Lower Level Threat Scenario</strong>.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="39cc316b-3772-4711-9f67-84eb757173a8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Now, when generating the Attack Paths, the Attack Potential Attack Paths are created.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="e25b800e-5100-4a62-9c0a-a45b234de4ae"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The computation of score, rating and Level of Threat has been enhanced.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="85960575-1b05-4ca4-87e9-d13226749ab1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Vulnerability related enchancements have been implemented.</span></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="c563de6c-f8b7-46a1-834f-7117d3f500c7"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SCD/?contextKey=systems-cybersecurity-designer&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Systems Cybersecurity Designer 2026x Refresh1</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180313 space=VN version=7 -->
## PAGE 00223: (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 Plugin

- page_id: `314180313`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314180313/2026x+Refresh1+UAF+1.3+Plugin
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2026-06-26T13:51:15.811+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x Refresh1) 2026x Refresh1 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

The**2026x Refresh1**release introduces usability enhancements in alignment with UAF v1.3 Mission Engineering Profile.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].For further information, check the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.3 modeling' space='MT'].

##### UAF 1.3 Usability Enhancements

In this version, a dedicated **Environment Properties** compartment has been implemented to group and display Environment properties separately from other properties. This enhancement brings greater clarity to diagrams and makes it easier to work with Environment properties.

Additionally, Environment properties now have dedicated **Symbol Properties**, which can be managed independently from other properties and attributes throughout the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

##### 600400

##### Documentation

- [UAF 1.3 Plugin 2026x Refresh1](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/?contextKey=uaf-13-modeling&version=2026x%20Refresh1&variant=Magic%20Systems%20of%20Systems%20Architect%20%2F%20Cameo%20Enterprise%20Architecture)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: June 26, 2026</h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The</span><strong style="text-align: left;"> 2026x Refresh1</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> <span>release introduces usability enhancements in alignment with UAF v1.3 Mission Engineering Profile.</span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To download the latest version, see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For further information, check the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="UAF 1.3 modeling" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[product documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 style="text-align: left;">UAF 1.3 Usability Enhancements</h3><p>In this version, a dedicated <strong>Environment Properties</strong> compartment has been implemented to group and display Environment properties separately from other properties. This enhancement brings greater clarity to diagrams and makes it easier to work with Environment properties.</p><p>Additionally, Environment properties now have dedicated <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>, which can be managed independently from other properties and attributes throughout the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog.</p><h3 style="text-align: center;"> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56f85e60-6e97-49d0-b0b8-4d2e81396153"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://youtu.be/XCFtSfTw-3k" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></h3><h3><span>Documentation</span></h3><ul><li><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/?contextKey=uaf-13-modeling&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1&amp;variant=Magic%20Systems%20of%20Systems%20Architect%20%2F%20Cameo%20Enterprise%20Architecture">UAF 1.3 Plugin 2026x Refresh1</a></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=278725663 space=VN version=8 -->
## PAGE 00225: (2026x) 2026x Hot Fix 1

- page_id: `278725663`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/278725663/2026x+2026x+Hot+Fix+1
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-03-17T14:48:43.132+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News > (2026x) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools

###### Released on: December 19, 2025

2026x Hot Fix 1 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when downloading its plugins. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

#### SysML v2 Features

##### Local Project Management Improvements

###### The Manage Projects Dialog Simplification

The**Manage Projects**dialog has been improved by removing redundant buttons and reducing the number of displayed columns. Project version and import details are now displayed within the**Name**column. These enhancements deliver a cleaner, more intuitive interface and a smoother project-management experience within the local repository.[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-local-projects&version=2026x#id-(2026x)Managinglocalprojects-TheManageProjectsdialog.1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project Naming Improvements in the Local Repository

Updates have been introduced to project naming in the local repository. Updates have been introduced to project naming in the local repository.

- Projects created in the local repository are now displayed only by their project name.
- Imported projects are displayed using the following structure : [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

This update provides more precise and informative details at a glance. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-local-projects&version=2026x#id-(2026x)Managinglocalprojects-Managingprojectimportsinthelocalrepository)****

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Other Improvements

- Local repository performance . First-time project opening (while building local caches) is now up to 20% faster . Subsequent openings are up to 10 times faster .
- Local repository optimization . Previously saved but unused project versions are now automatically removed on each save, preventing unnecessary local repository growth.
- Simplified project saving in the local repository . The distinction between saving a project as a draft or a new version has been removed. The unified **Save Project**command now simply saves the project as the latest version in the local repository . [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-local-projects&version=2026x#id-(2026x)Managinglocalprojects-Savingprojectsinthelocalrepository)
- Removing the original project vs. its subsequent imports from the local repository . While removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project, removing any subsequent import of the same project no longer affects the original project. Learn more >>

##### Local Used Project Management Improvements

###### The Project Usages Dialog Simplification

The **Project Usages**and **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialogs have been improved by removing any redundant buttons. The **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog now also displays fewer columns. These improvements provide a cleaner interface and a more efficient used project management experience within the local repository.Learn more [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-local-used-projects&version=2026x#id-(2026x)Managinglocalusedprojects-TheProjectUsagesdialog)and[here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-local-used-projects&version=2026x#id-(2026x)Managinglocalusedprojects-TheAdvancedProjectUsagesConfigurationdialog)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Improved Workflow for Updating Used Projects

The workflow for updating used project versions has been redesigned.In the main project, an outdated used project may refer to two of its available versions:**Older Version**or**Latest Version**. You can choose to update the used project to its latest version via the**Switch to Latest Version**command or continue using the older one. This simplified workflow makes updating used projects faster, clearer, and more efficient. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-local-used-projects&version=2026x#id-(2026x)Managinglocalusedprojects-Updatingusedprojectstotheirlatestversions)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Improved Workflow for Switching Between the Original Used Project and Its Subsequent Imports

You can now easily switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the **Switch To** command.

- The Switch To menu displays the used project originally created in the local repository by indicating the project name and version ( Latest/Oldest Version ).
- Subsequent imports of the project are displayed using the structure: <[<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] <version (Latest/Oldest Version)>> .

This new workflow separates switching between imports from updating to the latest version, offering a clearer and more intuitive experience. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=managing-local-used-projects&version=2026x#id-(2026x)Managinglocalusedprojects-Switchingbetweentheoriginalusedprojectanditssubsequentimports.1)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Performance Improvements

- The 2026x HotFix1 release delivers significantly improved model synchronization performance in the Textual Editor, providing approximately a **40% speed increase**in the local repository and Teamwork Cloud.
- Projects in the local repository now open up to **20% faster** on the first load and up to **ten times faster** after caching.
- Projects in Teamwork Cloud are updated up to **two times faster**.

#### Improvements and Bug Fixes

##### SysML v2

###### SysML v2 Plugin

- The issue where integrity errors appeared after project creation has been fixed.
- The issue of incorrect implied subsetting for the result of the Index and Select expressions has been fixed.
- The issue of incorrect implied subsetting for a feature with a *FeatureValue* has been fixed.
- The issue where importing a . sysml file with an exposed dependency caused errors has been fixed.
- The issue where connectors were not displayed for an element symbol after executing the ‘Display Connectors’ command if another symbol already displayed those connectors has been fixed.
- The issue where default style property values for views were not saved has been fixed.
- The issue where the *isMultiline* column attribute was ineffective for tabular views has been fixed.
- The issue where a *ref* keyword was added to each element created from the view palette and nested inside an element symbol has been fixed.
- The issue where navigation in the Textual Editor caused an error has been fixed.
- The issue where a single ‘Undo’ command undid two previous paste commands instead of one in the Textual Editor has been fixed.
- The issue where the modeling tool became unresponsive during operation finalization after editing in the Textual Editor has been fixed.
- The issue where the Textual Editor reported an incompatibility with the textual notation for transitions using a send action has been fixed.
- The issue where the ‘Use Server Project’ command redirected to local repository management has been fixed.
- The issue where the ‘Recent Server Projects’ command displayed local repository projects has been fixed.
- The issue where predefined library used projects were available for selection when branching used projects in Teamwork Cloud has been fixed.
- The issue where the project import to the Local Repository fails has been fixed.
- The issue where the default style is not applied to nested views has been fixed.

###### SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin

- The issue where the features with multiplicity of more than 20 are evaluated incorrectly has been resolved.
- Evaluation performance has been improved.
- The issue where an HTML table is generated with an invalid context specified has been fixed.
- The issue where featurenames could be edited in a generated HTML table has been fixed.

###### SysML v2 REST API

- Improved getElements service performance on initial invocation.
- SysML v2 API Query service now supports queries with derived properties upon indexing projects in ElasticSearch.

###### Collaboration

- Users without the required clearance level will no longer be able to open projects with data markings and remove all references.

##### Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- The issue where it was allowed to copy and paste a table cell, resulting in elements with identical names, has been fixed.
- Issues related to tagged value export to Eclipse UML2 have been fixed.

###### Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud

- The issue causing duplicated data after updating used projects has been fixed.
- The issue where removing underlying model references did not fully work has been fixed.
- The issue where projects branched together with used projects still use the original (non-branched) used projects has been resolved.

###### Collaboration powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- The issue where model merge was sometimes failing in the **3D**EXPERIENCE Cloud environment has been fixed.
- The issue that causes an error when trying to use a server project and select a specific project iteration has been fixed.
- The issue where not all related standard profiles are added to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform together with a BPMN2 projecthasbeen fixed . The issue only occurred when a modeling tool with the UPDM plugin was used.
- Several issues related to standard profile migration from version 2024x to version 2026x have been fixed.

##### Magic Systems of Systems Engineer / Cameo Enterprise Architecture / UAF 1.3

**UAF 1.3**

- Expanded the list of elements that can be created either of all or within specific viewpoints across the UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks.
- The availability of the new UAF 1.3 Mission Engineering elements has been extended to allow creating them directly from the symbolic diagram palette in symbolic diagrams across all supported frameworks (UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0).
- The creation of the**Resources Internal Connectivity**diagrams is now available in a**Resource Role**context when the type is an Organizational Resource (e.g., Organization, Post, Person, Responsibility, Project).
- Fixed the SysML validation issue related to the *ValueType* for UAF elements such as **Condition** and its specializations, including the new UAF 1.3 elements **Mission Scenario** and **Mission Vignette**.

**UPDM 2.1**

- The Measure [InstanceSpecification] classifier is changed from MeasureType [DataType] to MeasurementSet [DataType] to expand the possibility of using MeasurementSets, either created within the model or from the DoDAF Class Library.

##### Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler / SysML v1

- The issue where the symbol text covers the compartments of the *InstanceSpecification* symbol has been fixed.

##### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- The issue where SSP export failed when a system had ports without a type has been fixed.
- SSP files are now generated correctly when the URL tag of the «ExternalModel» stereotype leads to a web resource.
- SSP export now supports nested ports.
- The issue where FMU files generated with Dymola 2025x Refresh1 and later versions fail to initialize has been resolved.
- The issue causing the simulation to get stuck at a time event has been fixed.
- The issue where results from the SSP simulation fail to load on the Results Player has been fixed.

##### MagicCollaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator

- Changes in SAML Service Provider entity – this property is now read-only and generated automatically. A ll customers using SAML authentication need to update the Service Provider's registration on the Identity Provider's side.
- In Cameo Collaborator, navigation when selecting an Activity diagram has been improved.
- In MagicLab, navigation between two open views has been improved.
- The issue where Cameo Collaborator document publishing sometimes fails has been fixed.

##### Cameo DataHub

**SysML v2:**

- Fixed the need for a JAVA argument when enabling DataHub for SysML v2, making the setup cleaner and more user-friendly.
- Copy Data is now the default synchronization operation for SysML v2 projects, making synchronization flows feel more natural out of the box.

**DNG Improvements:**

- Fixed the need for a mandatory Java argument when testing the DNG Backlink scenario, simplifying the workflow.
- Fixed an issue that prevented connection to RM Global Configuration Management (GCM) streams when Test Management or Workflow Manager streams were part of the same project.

**ENOVIA Requirements:**

- A targeted set of fixes improves stability and synchronization predictability.
- Fixed display issues for attribute titles containing NLS characters.
- Fixed new-node synchronization from the modeling tool to ENOVIA Requirements when mapped attributes contained NLS.
- Fixed editing issues for NLS attributes in the Explorer panel.
- Fixed the missing synchronization icon after revising an object.
- Fixed mismatched NLS attribute names in DataHub Properties to align with ENOVIA Requirements.
- Fixed issues when synchronizing from the modeling tool to ENOVIA Requirements while using individual mapping with links.

**JAMA Connect Enhancements:**

- Enhanced overall reliability, performance, and link-handling accuracy.
- Fixed an issue where moved requirements/components in JAMA Connect appeared as new entities during synchronization to the modeling tool.
- Fixed incorrect link-deletion indicators when items were moved in JAMA Connect.
- Fixed OAuth connection failures introduced after JAMA Connect added V1/V2 API support.
- Improved connection performance when establishing a session with JAMA Connect.
- Fixed issues where links copied from the modeling tool to JAMA Connect did not transfer correctly.
- Fixed duplicated entities caused by node movement on the JAMA Connect side during sync.

**General Fixes Across All Connectors:**

- Fixed an issue where synchronization incorrectly marked nodes as excluded when using DTTFAV mapping.

##### Systems Cybersecurity Designer

**ISO 21434:**

- An ISO 21434 project can now be migrated from 2024x Refresh 2 to 2026x HF1.
- The Attack Potential–based Attack Path property names viz; Elapsed Time, Expertise, Knowledge of TOE, Window of Opportunity, and Equipment, now conform to the ISO 18045 specification.
- Elapsed Time values containing a ‘<’ symbol are now displayed correctly in the Attack Potential–based Attack Path table description.

**DO-326A:**

- Fixed an issue where an error was displayed when selecting About → Show Samples in the open project.
- Fixed an issue where renaming a running Generate Threat Condition and Security Impact command raised an error after renaming the associated package.
- In the TWC environment, a notification is now displayed when selecting Extract Loss of Security Attribute while the related SecurityImpact relationship is locked by another user.
- In the TWC environment, selecting Extract Loss of Security Attribute from a read-only Threat Condition now correctly extracts the LossOfSecurityAttribute .
- In the TWC environment, an appropriate notification is now displayed when selecting Extract Loss of Security Attribute from a Threat Condition locked by another user.
- In the TWC environment, assigning an impact rating to a LossOfSecurityAttribute now correctly fails when the corresponding SecurityImpact dependency is locked by another user.

##### Systems Safety Engineer

**FMEA Table Updates:**

The FMEA table has been updated with the following changes:

- Computed Effect is removed.
- Final Effect is removed.
- Effect is added.
- Classification is added.
- Noise Factor is added.

**Severity Criteria Behavior Updates:**

- Severity is editable only if one Computed Effect exists for the FMEA item.
- A computed effect is the result of identifying a failure mode, FM1, as a cause of a failure mode, FM2 . Once defined in this way, FM2 is labelled as a Computed Effect of FM1 in the opposite hierarchy.

**Relation between the Severity value and the identified Final Effect:**

- When only one Final Effect is identified, and a Computed Effect exists for an FMEA item, the Severity value is editable and assigned to the Final Effect.
- When multiple Final Effects are identified, Severity is not captured.
- If a Severity value was previously defined when only one Final Effect existed, that value is retained; otherwise, Severity remains unset.

**Context Definition Support:**

- A new feature has been added to the FMEA table that enables setting the Failure Modes, Cause, and Effect definitions only for a given context.
- You can use the Context field in the table toolbar to browse the available Part Properties breakdown, starting from the root context and navigating to the required Part Properties usage (Occurrence).
- Once a context is selected, all FMEA item definitions apply only to that context.
- Inputs entered for one context are not visible when the context is changed or removed, and the inputs are restored when the same context is reselected.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Improved Workflow for Switching Between the Original Used Project and Its Subsequent Imports.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3>Performance Improvements</h3><ul><li><span>The 2026x HotFix1 release delivers significantly improved model synchronization performance in the Textual Editor, providing approximately a <strong>40% speed increase </strong>in the local repository and Teamwork Cloud.</span></li><li data-uuid="71aeeab4-837a-4b2b-bf7e-6f014f5958ab"><span>Projects in the local repository now open up to <strong>20% faster</strong> on the first load and up to <strong>ten times faster</strong> after caching.</span></li><li data-uuid="b280dab8-ff27-4022-9791-47102931b00a"><span>Projects in Teamwork Cloud are updated up to <strong>two times faster</strong>.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Improvements and Bug Fixes</h2><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ae05f5e5-1f34-44a8-a395-867318447d8b">SysML v2</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><h4>SysML v2 Plugin</h4><ul><li data-uuid="0ed9d439-8913-42fe-acac-7b6c5a22033e"><span>The issue where integrity errors appeared after project creation has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="25a35b22-5908-436d-b09a-6896884b9933"><span>The issue of incorrect implied subsetting for the result of the Index and Select expressions has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="f4e1b577-6db3-4c98-8471-7caf03d38ffa"><span>The issue of incorrect implied subsetting for a feature with a <em>FeatureValue</em> has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="a316da0b-17f6-4143-9496-6b7513f2eb58"><span>The issue where importing a .</span><em><span>sysml</span></em><span> file with an exposed dependency caused errors has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="703f1389-1321-4587-8f3a-582f4f3f8c7e"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where connectors were not displayed for an element symbol after executing the ‘Display Connectors’ command if another symbol already displayed those connectors has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="da7e2b67-fc40-41e0-a429-48dc8056b969"><span>The issue where default style property values for views were not saved has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="50ae63d8-e679-417a-a327-dff7a1471b34"><span>The issue where the <em>isMultiline</em> column attribute was ineffective for tabular views has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="58ef251a-9b56-4f37-a360-d06331e2c63e"><span>The issue where a <em>ref</em> keyword was added to each element created from the view palette and nested inside an element symbol has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="a186762b-a517-4832-b1ec-170ed670f5f1"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where navigation in the Textual Editor caused an error has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="9aae0716-2864-4619-bdff-5c7f0576805e"><span>The issue where a single ‘Undo’ command undid two previous paste commands instead of one in the Textual Editor has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="c6a7d073-ef51-4338-ac3f-51be2c610474"><span>The issue where the modeling tool became unresponsive during operation finalization after editing in the Textual Editor has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="22c90f8e-a756-4946-8283-3a4430c8fb07"><span>The issue where the Textual Editor reported an incompatibility with the textual notation for transitions using a send action has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="4adc2397-82dc-4067-8479-d284da42e1f1"><span>The issue where the ‘Use Server Project’ command redirected to local repository management has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="5069aa45-6a0a-4e60-bff8-63c4ffe8aed0"><span>The issue where the ‘Recent Server Projects’ command displayed local repository projects has been fixed. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></li><li data-uuid="c3fbb594-9cf9-48e1-a170-ca0814acaa7e"><span>The issue where predefined library used projects were available for selection when branching used projects in Teamwork Cloud has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="c96a391e-1920-4276-a059-1e3689402d4b">The issue where the project import to the Local Repository fails has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="c3808f7a-c70f-40bc-ae17-f63eb1dd7b44">The issue where the default style is not applied to nested views has been fixed.</li></ul><h4>SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin</h4><ul><li>The issue where the features with multiplicity of more than 20 are evaluated incorrectly has been resolved.</li><li data-uuid="0a95b2e6-4dd7-4a70-a979-d257457e0677">Evaluation performance has been improved.</li><li data-uuid="7f10cf46-2d83-470c-9c50-5bc7eb4ee2aa">The issue where an HTML table is generated with an invalid context specified has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="f22f45ea-7180-4af0-8bdd-9378009089ed"><span>The issue where feature<span> names could be edited in a generated HTML table has been fixed.</span></span></li></ul><h4>SysML v2 <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f98326a5-38d4-4686-9902-38e09c83cfb8">REST</ac:inline-comment-marker> API</h4><ul><li data-uuid="898b281d-ac6d-4fd9-8f24-9a3c6ac1243e">Improved <em>getElements</em> service performance on initial invocation.</li><li data-uuid="29fc17b9-f06a-43d0-b9ab-62df20cdc537">SysML v2 API Query service now supports queries with derived properties upon indexing projects in ElasticSearch.</li></ul><h4>Collaboration</h4><ul><li data-uuid="46abde5c-b44d-4c6e-9968-26daeed570a4"><span>Users without the required clearance level will no longer be able to open projects with data markings and remove all references.</span></li></ul><h3>Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw</h3><ul><li data-uuid="19e75431-1e89-4cad-9433-625dde3f6157"><span>The issue where it was allowed to copy and paste a table cell, resulting in elements with identical names, has been fixed. </span></li><li data-uuid="73929683-76bd-4164-9b9d-df76a344b048"><span>Issues related to tagged value export to Eclipse UML2 have been fixed. </span></li></ul><h4>Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud</h4><ul><li data-uuid="4b79a0bf-4afe-467a-a2f7-4d93db35f8f4"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue causing duplicated data after updating used projects has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="d71e7afe-e568-41df-aad6-30fac8703565"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The issue where removing underlying model references did not fully work has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="73668ba8-bb56-434f-9069-38aa3851fcf5">The issue where projects branched together with used projects <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">still use the original (non-branched) used projects</span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d0ba6d9a-1519-4f9d-85c0-f9976654a4ca"> has been resolved.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><h4>Collaboration powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE platform</h4><ul><li data-uuid="2b91d7ca-e350-4499-b83d-7025e85ef67e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The issue where model merge was sometimes failing in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE Cloud environment has been fixed.</span></li><li data-uuid="a7733532-3bc4-444a-b0ed-82d9ca0ff63a">The issue that causes an error when trying to use a server project and select a specific project iteration has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="1f2ea5ef-abb1-4eb7-a335-a9c0a5fcaf68">The issue where not all related standard profiles are added to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform together <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="92b17f1e-8f2b-49a1-99fd-071314510096">with a BPMN2 project<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> has </span>been fixed</ac:inline-comment-marker>. The issue only occurred when a modeling tool <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">with the UPDM plugin was used.</span></li><li data-uuid="576fd86e-e188-4558-a9c7-9b8d0b838dbf">Several issues related to standard profile migration from version 2024x to version 2026x have been fixed.</li></ul><h3>Magic Systems of Systems Engineer / Cameo Enterprise Architecture / UAF 1.3</h3><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>UAF 1.3</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="f0b13434-da27-48d3-b1aa-5c7f978c6a81"><p>Expanded the list of elements that can be created either of all or within specific viewpoints across the UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks.</p></li><li data-uuid="929ef4b3-a5a8-411f-a3e8-3ad1dc7739b8"><p>The availability of the new UAF 1.3 Mission Engineering elements has been extended to allow creating them directly from the symbolic diagram palette in symbolic diagrams across all supported frameworks (UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0).</p></li><li data-uuid="e7bca756-d257-4af9-932f-2af22bb65eab"><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="137200a4-303e-47ac-9efc-76f04d6d877b">The creation of the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="137200a4-303e-47ac-9efc-76f04d6d877b">Resources Internal Connectivity</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="137200a4-303e-47ac-9efc-76f04d6d877b"> diagrams is now available in a </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="137200a4-303e-47ac-9efc-76f04d6d877b">Resource Role</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="137200a4-303e-47ac-9efc-76f04d6d877b"> context when the type is an Organizational Resource (e.g., Organization, Post, Person, Responsibility, Project).</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li data-uuid="aa9b9e92-b770-4413-947d-14b409131c13"><p>Fixed the SysML validation issue related to the <em>ValueType</em> for UAF elements such as <strong>Condition</strong> and its specializations, including the new UAF 1.3 elements <strong>Mission Scenario</strong> and <strong>Mission Vignette</strong>.</p></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>UPDM 2.1</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="5e522cd9-c7b6-41ea-b9fe-c80dbb6134d6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Measure<span> </span></span><span class="error" style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">[InstanceSpecification]</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>classifier is changed from MeasureType<span> </span></span><span class="error" style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">[DataType]</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>to MeasurementSet<span> </span></span><span class="error" style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">[DataType]</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> to expand the possibility of using MeasurementSets, either created within</span> the model or from the DoDAF Class Library.</span></li></ul><h3>Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler / SysML v1</h3><ul><li data-uuid="deba5762-ad2a-47ad-b698-f1c731ef94a0"><span>The issue where the symbol text covers the compartments of the <em>InstanceSpecification</em> symbol has been fixed.</span><span> </span></li></ul><h3>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit</h3><ul><li data-uuid="b3741977-e8e1-490a-887c-97117db197f4">The issue where SSP export failed when a system had ports without a type has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="14efd608-f251-47ed-a198-246c37e80978"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span>SSP files are now generated correctly when the URL tag of the «ExternalModel» stereotype leads to a web resource.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="4ed6fe7f-4d32-4d39-b187-68008264a699">SSP export now supports nested ports.</li><li data-uuid="b30bd466-b184-4f3e-92f5-7bc31bcae5fc">The issue where FMU files generated with Dymola 2025x Refresh1 and later versions fail to initialize has been resolved.</li><li data-uuid="f447d17d-c6be-4f04-b1f5-46f319abf346">The issue causing the simulation to get stuck at a time event has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="b5dc147b-a3fe-4106-9a73-a7148f25ea23"><p><span>The issue where results from the SSP simulation fail to load on the Results Player has been fixed.</span></p></li></ul><h3><span>Magic </span><span>Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud / Cameo Collaborator</span></h3><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="5be1e369-d055-40fa-b887-f4793f8bb88a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Changes in SAML Service Provider entity – this property is now read-only and generated automatically. A</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ll customers using SAML authentication need to update the Service Provider's registration on the Identity Provider's side.</span></li><li data-uuid="43f62419-6ea9-40f7-8271-65146e6b1bb3">In Cameo Collaborator, navigation when selecting an Activity diagram has been improved.  </li><li data-uuid="9738cf72-81b0-4bb7-b3e7-cd0b010b7816">In MagicLab, navigation between two open views has been improved.</li><li data-uuid="9c72a919-d051-4d95-8880-ca4b5c038900">The issue where Cameo Collaborator document publishing sometimes fails has been fixed.</li></ul><h3>Cameo DataHub</h3><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>SysML v2:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="09e1ce58-1436-4062-b3fc-12b78728c609">Fixed the need for a JAVA argument when enabling DataHub for SysML v2, making the setup cleaner and more user-friendly.</li><li data-uuid="d7266fc2-af05-4fb2-ba0e-58ccc8686ef8">Copy Data is now the default synchronization operation for SysML v2 projects, making synchronization flows feel more natural out of the box.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>DNG Improvements:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="6fa319f6-05f9-4e01-b4c3-ec75b22c4aeb">Fixed the need for a mandatory Java argument when testing the DNG Backlink scenario, simplifying the workflow.</li><li data-uuid="1d43f85b-66ef-4807-9ead-df41cb61ee0d">Fixed an issue that prevented connection to RM Global Configuration Management (GCM) streams when Test Management or Workflow Manager streams were part of the same project.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>ENOVIA Requirements:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="7207a410-c16f-4d61-ad9f-705f7483dc6e">A targeted set of fixes improves stability and synchronization predictability.</li><li data-uuid="9c82bfbb-1762-4fdf-9636-e3b505cdc173">Fixed display issues for attribute titles containing NLS characters.</li><li data-uuid="bdac36ee-fef1-41f4-b7da-4bae2436052d">Fixed new-node synchronization from the modeling tool to ENOVIA Requirements when mapped attributes contained NLS.</li><li data-uuid="ff8c9474-dfff-482f-88e4-09cf0673cf30">Fixed editing issues for NLS attributes in the Explorer panel.</li><li data-uuid="10b1399c-ff90-4bf6-b1f4-54cc9aa16b9f">Fixed the missing synchronization icon after revising an object.</li><li data-uuid="f5148a12-5a8a-4bbd-a5a3-0db124c02d8a">Fixed mismatched NLS attribute names in DataHub Properties to align with ENOVIA Requirements.</li><li data-uuid="c3e9051a-644f-4999-80f1-54da8233b023">Fixed issues when synchronizing from the modeling tool to ENOVIA Requirements while using individual mapping with links.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>JAMA Connect Enhancements:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="05e1005f-8c42-49ce-977e-0d3ecdc32f39">Enhanced overall reliability, performance, and link-handling accuracy.</li><li data-uuid="2062fa97-1035-4181-b87d-413a9ad245a4">Fixed an issue where moved requirements/components in JAMA Connect appeared as new entities during synchronization to the modeling tool.</li><li data-uuid="a736c83a-1a7a-4e92-9a49-3179c354343f">Fixed incorrect link-deletion indicators when items were moved in JAMA Connect.</li><li data-uuid="ea1a1beb-8727-4016-b407-3d04a700d99a">Fixed OAuth connection failures introduced after JAMA Connect added V1/V2 API support.</li><li data-uuid="a21b3836-b7be-4a27-98cb-ed6dd801c048">Improved connection performance when establishing a session with JAMA Connect.</li><li data-uuid="625f9de0-899c-42a6-a896-9a60059a1b09">Fixed issues where links copied from the modeling tool to JAMA Connect did not transfer correctly.</li><li data-uuid="2ff58abb-7626-45fc-8983-2ef1789eb399">Fixed duplicated entities caused by node movement on the JAMA Connect side during sync.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>General Fixes Across All Connectors:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="9d611fa0-a9c6-462a-92a8-2c3181a32075">Fixed an issue where synchronization incorrectly marked nodes as excluded when using <strong>DTTFAV </strong>mapping.</li></ul><h3>Systems Cybersecurity Designer</h3><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>ISO 21434:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="1a9aa45d-f22d-436d-ba20-b686a911038b">An ISO 21434 project can now be migrated from 2024x Refresh 2 to 2026x HF1.</li><li data-uuid="1e5af871-b1d1-4db5-8d1c-b2377330d096">The Attack Potential–based Attack Path property names viz; Elapsed Time, Expertise, Knowledge of TOE, Window of Opportunity, and Equipment, now conform to the ISO 18045 specification.</li><li data-uuid="f51e1933-b26c-4e87-a4a0-6a65e46d8830">Elapsed Time values containing a ‘&lt;’ symbol are now displayed correctly in the Attack Potential–based Attack Path table description.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>DO-326A:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="2301a797-a75f-4b3c-99af-192a1d159486">Fixed an issue where an error was displayed when selecting About → Show Samples in the open project.</li><li data-uuid="33986415-3cf6-44d4-ba64-e3b27098e33c">Fixed an issue where renaming a running Generate Threat Condition and Security Impact command raised an error after renaming the associated package.</li><li data-uuid="9773fbe5-2fed-4849-9b64-79cff68a975a">In the TWC environment, a notification is now displayed when selecting <strong>Extract Loss of Security Attribute</strong> while the related <strong>SecurityImpact</strong> relationship is locked by another user.</li><li data-uuid="d999e2f5-5634-495e-adec-7f80c5230203">In the TWC environment, selecting <strong>Extract Loss of Security Attribute</strong> from a read-only Threat Condition now correctly extracts the <strong>LossOfSecurityAttribute</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="bab52d5c-4641-4612-832c-cfba18af9bf5">In the TWC environment, an appropriate notification is now displayed when selecting <strong>Extract Loss of Security Attribute</strong> from a Threat Condition locked by another user.</li><li data-uuid="d3843147-2115-413d-bf5b-3d39dad38692">In the TWC environment, assigning an impact rating to a <strong>LossOfSecurityAttribute</strong> now correctly fails when the corresponding <strong>SecurityImpact </strong>dependency is locked by another user.</li></ul><h3>Systems Safety Engineer</h3><p><strong>FMEA Table Updates:</strong></p><p>The FMEA table has been updated with the following changes:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8cb98d05-e2e5-4e5c-885c-72c825672b73"><strong>Computed Effect</strong> is removed.</li><li data-uuid="c5fc3de4-172f-40e1-8126-44a316786ad0"><strong>Final Effect</strong> is removed.</li><li data-uuid="09f73060-25bc-4cf6-8c4a-132c6817044c"><strong>Effect</strong> is added.</li><li data-uuid="1ab73154-cd21-49cc-b088-5c21d574c14c"><strong>Classification</strong> is added.</li><li data-uuid="50edce5b-9ec7-4b00-86cb-9929a3ee53c9"><strong>Noise Factor </strong>is added.</li></ul><p><strong>Severity Criteria Behavior Updates:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="5beb81b8-1cf7-4fc4-9f36-9af45d21d34c">Severity is editable only if one Computed Effect exists for the FMEA item.</li><li data-uuid="4255f383-1b67-4fa9-82f0-e0cbae25fc19">A computed effect is the result of identifying a failure mode, <strong>FM1,</strong> as a cause of a failure mode, <strong>FM2</strong>. Once defined in this way, <strong>FM2</strong> is labelled as a <strong>Computed Effect </strong>of <strong>FM1</strong> in the opposite hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Relation between the Severity value and the identified Final Effect:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="f402835f-aced-434f-80e6-cd7ef7d22ec4">When only one Final Effect is identified, and a Computed Effect exists for an FMEA item, the Severity value is editable and assigned to the Final Effect.</li><li data-uuid="1377581a-1af3-4abe-a701-d96c45d591f6">When multiple Final Effects are identified, Severity is not captured.</li><li data-uuid="ff384b8f-ca52-4f24-a823-304623989159">If a Severity value was previously defined when only one Final Effect existed, that value is retained; otherwise, Severity remains unset.</li></ul><p><strong>Context Definition Support:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="f586a28b-006c-49c6-af4e-acb4ddf0a8b6">A new feature has been added to the FMEA table that enables setting the Failure Modes, Cause, and Effect definitions only for a given context.</li><li data-uuid="f554049b-7a7b-4115-95d1-f9ecedbf25c5">You can use the <strong>Context </strong>field in the table toolbar to browse the available Part Properties breakdown, starting from the root context and navigating to the required Part Properties usage (Occurrence).</li><li data-uuid="e6dee326-c542-47c7-8fba-df44f76627ce">Once a context is selected, all FMEA item definitions apply only to that context.</li><li data-uuid="19906a38-9ad7-41f0-8ed0-1a2afd1fb116">Inputs entered for one context are not visible when the context is changed or removed, and the inputs are restored when the same context is reselected.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296124571 space=VN version=29 -->
## PAGE 00226: (2026x) 2026x Hot Fix 2

- page_id: `296124571`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/296124571/2026x+2026x+Hot+Fix+2
- version_number: 29
- version_date: `2026-03-20T12:45:48.825+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News > (2026x) Hot Fixes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools

###### Released on: March 20, 2026

2026x Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Component changes and addressed vulnerabilities' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products.

#### WARNING: Hot Fix version compatibility

Hot Fix version compatibility

The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when downloading its plugins. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.

##### SysML v2

###### SysML v2 Plugin

- The issue where an invalid Subclassification to library element is created after migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2 has been resolved.
- The issue where the validateExpressionResultParameterMembership validation rule fails the OperatorExpression has been resolved.
- The issue where a patch contains unnecessary elements from Standard Libraries has been resolved.
- The issue where quickly drawing nested states caused the application to freeze has been resolved.
- The issue where an offline SysML v2 project cannot be opened anymore after a non-SysML v2 project was opened has been resolved.
- The issue where a state-owned transition started from an action is not resolved to ‘decisionTransitions’ has been resolved.
- The issue where the state-owned transition’s source ‘start’ is resolved to Actions::Action::start instead of States::StateAction::start has been resolved.
- The issue where the project save fails after migration because of an invalid symbol style has been resolved.
- The issue where you cannot change symbol styles after refactoring a relation has been resolved.
- The issue where element metadata without a declared name is shown incorrectly in the Textual Editor for comment replies made in MagicLab has been resolved.
- The issue where the Textual Editor notifies of an error after it has already been fixed has been resolved.
- The issue where the Textual Editor hangs during the initialization phase has been resolved.
- The issue where the Textual Editor notifies of an error after closing a project has been resolved.
- The issue where the Textual Editor notifies of an error if the allocation path uses feature chaining has been resolved.
- The name resolution issues after opening the Textual Editor have been resolved.
- The issue where an internal error may occur after exiting Presentation mode when the Textual Editor is open has been resolved.
- The issue where semantic highlighting could be off by one character in the Textual Editor has been resolved.
- Improved Textual Editor performance during synchronization.
- Improved name resolution in the Textual Editor in edge cases.
- The issue where the ‘Publish Diagrams as Assets’ command via the CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis results in errors has been resolved.
- The issue where saving a project after removing a project usage caused an exception in the log files has been resolved.
- The issue where diagrams in used projects sometimes did not display all symbols has been fixed.
- The issue where, under certain conditions, an error occurs when trying to remove a used project has been fixed.
- The issue where an exception sometimes occurred when opening the textual editor for a root namespace has been fixed.
- A more informative error message is now displayed when attempting to open a SysML v2 project without the SysML v2 Plugin installed.

###### Collaboration

- The issue that, in certain cases, allowed committing a historic project version to the server has been fixed.
- The issue where an offline SysML v2 project fails to open after opening a non-SysML v2 project has been resolved.
- The issue where an error was logged when opening a server project after starting the modeling tool with a fresh user home has been fixed.

##### Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- The issue where plugins from a previous application version were carried over when installing a new version in the same location has been resolved.
- The issue where an incorrect version is indicated in the Project Usage Information dialog has been resolved.

###### Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud

- IMPORTANT: The issue where the content of a used project was not updated after changing the used project version has been fixed.
- The issue where, on some occasions, an exception occurs when trying to update a used project has been fixed.
- The issue where certain data is duplicated after updating a used project has been fixed.
- The issue where exporting a Teamwork Cloud project with used projects to a server project file resulted in an error has been resolved.
- The issue where an open SysML v2 project prevented importing a server project file from the 3D EXPERIENCE platform has been fixed.
- The issue that, in some cases, prevented used projects from being successfully removed has been fixed.
- The issue where certain data is duplicated after the project merge has been fixed.
- The issue where, in some cases, diagram content became corrupted after the project merge has been fixed.

###### Collaboration Powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

- The issue where sometimes trying to open a specific project branch results in an error has been fixed.
- The issue where the history of an open project took too long to load has been resolved.
- The issue that, on some occasions, caused errors during project merge has been resolved.
- The issue where projects failed to open after the project merge has been fixed.
- The issue where certain data is duplicated after the project merge has been fixed.
- The Show Changes command of the Suspect Links feature has been disabled as it is not supported for 3D EXPERIENCE platform projects .
- The issue where an error was logged when exporting packages to a new server project has been fixed.
- The issue where importing a server project file to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform sometimes failed has been fixed.
- The issue where, on some occasions, merging project branches results in an error has been resolved.
- The issue where committing a project after the project merge sometimes results in an error has been fixed.
- The issue where the file attached to a project became corrupted after the project merge has been resolved.
- The issue where merging project branches without locking the project failed has been resolved.
- The issue where an offline project became corrupted after the project merge has been fixed.
- The issue where, in some cases, diagram content was lost after the project merge has been fixed.

##### Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler / SysML v1

- Exporting a model to SSP no longer duplicates inherited and redefined ports.
- If a model contains URL tags that reference local files in the project, a warning is displayed when those files cannot be found and are excluded from the SSP export.
- A warning is now displayed if the SSP export includes Flow Properties without types, indicating that untyped Flow Properties will be exported as “Binary,” the default unknown type in SSP.
- SSP export now supports multiple instance slot values for Value Properties with a multiplicity greater than 1.

##### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- The issue where the Simulation Configuration execution randomly stops when using the Image Switcher has been fixed.
- The issue where, in some cases, a non-existent passing verification value is exported to an instance has been resolved.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>CATIA Magic / No Magic Modeling Tools</h2><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: March 20, 2026</h6><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">2026x Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Component changes and addressed vulnerabilities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[vulnerability fixes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in our modeling tools and server products.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e23f89-7e49-4415-8c18-a6a2aff251f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hot Fix version compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The version and Hot Fix number of a modeling tool and its plugins must be the same. After installing the Hot Fix version of a modeling tool, select the same Hot Fix version when downloading its plugins. The modeling tool will not work correctly if you install plugins of a different version and/or Hot Fix number.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>SysML v2</h3><h4>SysML v2 Plugin</h4><ul><li data-uuid="8dac0656-61b3-483e-ba7e-60f50353113e"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where an invalid Subclassification to library element is created after migration from SysML v1 to SysML v2 has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="b31af173-e989-4312-9556-93b45bcae8f0"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where the validateExpressionResultParameterMembership validation rule fails the OperatorExpression has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="aca8647b-0895-4cb5-8bd4-9ea430bf56aa"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where a patch contains unnecessary elements from Standard Libraries has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="f1195c76-d216-437e-905c-3ea3acce7055"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where quickly drawing nested states caused the application to freeze has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="84470642-ddfb-496d-b65f-5ead9c7111bd"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where an offline SysML v2 project cannot be opened anymore after a non-SysML v2 project was opened has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="e2fa6da9-7d2e-45c9-bb53-676dbba07490"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where a state-owned transition started from an action is not resolved to ‘decisionTransitions’ has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="03cc130f-6d09-498b-9933-0fb5cb635815"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where the state-owned transition’s source ‘start’ is resolved to Actions::Action::start instead of States::StateAction::start has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="10a83a46-fc2d-43d8-be08-6681c68db5f7"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where the project save fails after migration because of an invalid symbol style has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="77e84440-71ad-4dff-ac0b-ce7855f57560"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where you cannot change symbol styles after refactoring a relation has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="4d7d8dde-2544-4407-9181-5b27e13f7bab"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where element metadata without a declared name is shown incorrectly in the Textual Editor for comment replies made in MagicLab has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="557cf63d-61c3-435a-956c-1573b9517672"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where the Textual Editor notifies of an error after it has already been fixed has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="8898b07c-3438-4054-88ab-5d363201ca3a"><span>The issue where the Textual Editor hangs during the initialization phase has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="bb6c41ee-0716-4a30-8856-7bef0a52524a"><span>The issue where the Textual Editor notifies of an error after closing a project has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="de73876f-21a4-4cf0-a3c0-c11116989c1c"><span>The issue where the Textual Editor notifies of an error if the allocation path uses feature chaining has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="408a1c11-93b7-4a20-b3fe-d66a2a1befb8"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The name resolution issues after opening the Textual Editor have been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="27d9ddfc-cdd5-446e-b77a-f0504070d0a9"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where an internal error may occur after exiting Presentation mode when the Textual Editor is open has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="93224c20-1459-4dc4-8422-b2168a631579">The issue where semantic highlighting could be off by one character in the Textual Editor has been resolved.</li><li data-uuid="edef11b9-552d-4e1c-9113-d84250b7f744">Improved <span>Textual Editor </span>performance during synchronization.</li><li data-uuid="4dfd524a-11ae-4fa5-bc71-004627a92a8d">Improved name resolution in the Textual Editor in edge cases.</li><li data-uuid="65b02deb-5511-4f75-a248-8b25f17a90b3"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where the ‘Publish Diagrams as Assets’ command via the CATIA Systems Synthesis Analysis results in errors has been resolved. </span></li><li data-uuid="db78c642-6c99-4bde-b95b-109663b2e690"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The issue where saving a project after removing a project usage caused an exception in the log files has been resolved.</span></li><li data-uuid="bd527df9-5d3f-4cdf-b7c3-005522bece9a">The issue where diagrams in used projects sometimes did not display all symbols has been fixed.</li><li data-uuid="d1c3f0d1-5e52-414c-9a90-c5affd77dcfe">The issue where, under certain conditions, an error occurs when trying to remove a used project has been fixed.</li><li ## PAGE 00227: (2026x) 2026x Version News
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

#### TIP: Welcome to the version news site for CATIA Magic / No Magic products!

Welcome to the version news site for CATIA Magic / No Magic products!

Get started by selecting a product below and its version in the site header.

#### NOTE: 2026x Hot Fix 3 is availabe

2026x Hot Fix 3 is availabe

Due to the Kryo serialization vulnerability issues (CVE-2026-785), the****2026x Hot Fix 3 for **Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services**was released on May 29, 2026. [Learn more >>](https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613)

##### Hot Fixes for version 2026x

- 2026x Hot Fix 1 2026x Hot Fix 1 was released on December 19, 2025, for the CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) 2026x Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>
- 2026x Hot Fix 2 2026x Hot Fix 2 was released on March 20, 2026, for the CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements, bug fixes, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Addressed vulnerabilities and component changes' space=''] in our modeling tools and server products. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) 2026x Hot Fix 2' space='']>]]>
- **2026x Hot Fix 3** 
Due to the Kryo serialization vulnerability issues (CVE-2026-785), the****2026x Hot Fix 3 for **Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services**was released on May 29, 2026. [Learn more >>](https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747597 space=VN version=4 -->
## PAGE 00230: (2026x) Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `272747597`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747597/2026x+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-01-09T10:52:33.764+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

#### SysML v2 Support

You can now use the **Copy Data**operation in Cameo DataHub with **DOORS, DOORS Next (DNG), JAMA Connect, and ENOVIA Requirements** connectors without support for links. Standard properties, such as **Declared Name******and **Documentation Body**, are available for mapping. The SysML v2 support is available for evaluation purposes, and the DataHub team will continue adding new SysML v2 capabilities in upcoming feature-plugin releases.

- You must restart the modeling tool to switch between SysML v1 and v2.
- To learn more about enabling the SysMLv2 support, click [here](https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=troubleshooting&version=2026x).

#### ENOVIA Requirements

##### New Environment Options

The **Show All Elements, Show Ownership**,****and **Show Revisions** options are now added in the **ENOVIA Requirements**section of the Environment Options dialog. To learn more about the newly added environment options, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='ENOVIA Requirements' space='CDHTWRT'] page. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The newly added **Show All Elements, Show Ownership**,****and**Show Revisions**options present in theEnvironment Options dialog.

##### Other Improvements

- All requirement domain-related objects are now available on the CDH Explorer panel for you.
- Support for customized **Chapters**objects is enabled. All the ENOVIA Requirements custom data types are now available.
- Synchronization links rerouting to a new revision are enabled. When therevised object is replacedon the platform side,synchronization linksare rerouted to thereplaced objectautomatically whensynchronizationis initiated for therevised objectfor the first time.When therevised object is not replaced,link reroutingis initiated manually by selecting the command on thecontextual menu.
- Significant data synchronization performance improvements delivered.

#### Performance Improvements

Operations related to below below-mentioned activities have been improved. The following table presents a performance comparison of the time taken to perform various activities on a set of 1000 requirements organized in a flat hierarchy:

| Sr. No. | Action | 2024xR2 | 2026x |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Opening a local MSOSA project | 17 Seconds | 11 Seconds |
| 2 | Logging into ENOVIA Requirements | 10 Seconds | 9 Seconds |
| 3 | Launching ENOVIA Requirements | 349 Seconds | 39 Seconds |
| 4 | Using the Show all elements command | 220 Seconds | 45 Seconds |
| 5 | Expanding the ENOVIA node | 200 Seconds | 52 Seconds |

#### Roadmap

The upcoming Cameo DataHub releases will add support for relationships, custom types, and additional operations for SysMLv2.

#### Documentation

- [Cameo DataHub 2026x](https://docs.nomagic.com/CDH/?contextKey=cameo-datahub-documentation&version=2026x)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747834 space=VN version=21 -->
## PAGE 00232: (2026x) CATIA SysML v2 Solution

- page_id: `272747834`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747834/2026x+CATIA+SysML+v2+Solution
- version_number: 21
- version_date: `2026-01-13T11:43:49.530+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: 2026x Hot Fix 1 is out!

2026x Hot Fix 1 is out!

2026x Hot Fix 1 has been released on December 19, 2025, for the CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. Itoffers several important improvements and optimizations, **focused primarily on two key areas of the SysML v2 implementation**:

- Performance enhancements, particularly in the Textual Editor .
- Significant usability improvements to the Local Repository .

2026x Hot Fix 1 alsoincludes a number of bug fixes, as well as vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) 2026x Hot Fix 1' space='']>]]>

#### THE MOST POWERFUL, INTEGRATED, AND 100% STANDARD-COMPLIANT SYSML V2 SOLUTION

We invite you to watch the video from our September 2025 webinar showcasing our SysML v2 solution overview.

700400

##### SysML v2 Quick Sheet

For your convenience, the Dassault Systèmes and OOSE co-authored SysML v2 quick sheet is available for download below as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language using our SysML v2 solution.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Click the image, then click Download in the top-right corner to download the full file.

##### Standard Conformance

Full compliance with the SysML v2 standard is at the core of our implementation.

- Built on principle – our team makes standard compliance a core value; every engineer is committed to it.
- Interoperability – models exchange seamlessly with other tools following the standard.
- Ecosystem-ready – full conformance simplifies integration across the partner ecosystem.
- Future-proofing – no risk of vendor lock-in or compatibility gaps as the standard evolves.
- Trust & Transparency – engineers know their models are pure SysML v2, not tool-specific variants.

By delivering 100% standard conformance, Dassault Systèmes ensures that customers benefit from the**full power of SysML v2.**

##### Text and Diagrams in Perfect Sync

CATIA Magic/Cameo products deliver**seamless two-way synchronization**between**textual**and**graphical**SysML v2 model representations.

- Instant consistency – changes in text or diagrams are immediately reflected in both views.
- Dual approach – textual syntax appeals to software developers, while diagrams provide clarity for complex system architectures.
- Unique capability – currently the only solution supporting 100% of the SysML v2 standard with true two-way synchronization.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Dynamic Views

SysML v2 supports**three model visualization options**:

- Graphical
- Tabular
- Textual

In CATIA Magic/Cameo, users can**dynamically generate graphical and tabular views**by specifying**expressions that query the model**.

- Automatic updates – views can be rebuilt automatically when the model changes, saving systems engineers significant time and effort.
- Reusable definitions – view definitions can be stored in libraries and reused across projects and teams.
- Efficient modeling – eliminates manual adjustments.

Dynamic views are one of the most powerful capabilities in CATIA Magic/Cameo, enabling**efficient, consistent, and reusable visualizations**throughout the organization.

700400

50%

700400

50%

700400

##### Model Evaluation

SysML v2 introduces a powerful expression language for concise navigation and analysis of models. Using dot‑notation for feature chaining and collection operators such as sum(), complex queries can be written in a compact, intuitive form. Expressions are evaluated in full model context, honoring inheritance, redefinitions, subsets, and library functions.

CATIA Magic/Cameo enhances this with features that make analysis seamless:

- Requirements verification – any requirement violations are immediately flagged, providing instant traceability to violated constraints.
- Roll-ups & trade studies – apply roll-up patterns, perform “what-if” analyses, and conduct trade-off studies directly on the model.
- Evaluation in Model or Console – run expressions directly within the model or use the Evaluation Console to execute custom expressions without modifying the model.
- Advanced evaluation – supports analysis at both model and meta-model levels.
- Interactive diagrams – adjust initial values to test system behavior without modifying the model.

This combination of language and product capabilities**bridges modeling and analysis**, enabling**data-driven decision-making**and**full traceability from design exploration to validation**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

700400

##### Adopt SysML v2 at Your Own Pace

Organizations can adopt SysML v2 at their own pace thanks tothe**full co-existence of SysML v2 with SysML v1**. Both versions can be authored in the same CATIA Magic/Cameo modeling environment, with the ability to select SysML v1 or v2 per project. Migration is supported by the SysML v2 transformation specification, ensuring legacy models remain usable and protecting existing investments.

Key features:

- Authoring – co-existence of SysML v1 and v2 in the same environment
  - Choose v1 or v2 on a per-project basis
  - Support for SysML v2 transformation specifications for smooth migration, including diagrams
- API – server REST API continuity
  - Full SysML v2 standard API support
  - Continue using existing server REST API for both v1 and v2 projects
- Simulation – use case continuity from v1
  - Core SysML v1 simulation use cases supported in SysML v2
- User experience – focus on usability
  - Best features from SysML v1 implementation reused to maximize user efficiency

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**700400**

##### Standard SysML v2 REST API: CI/CD workflows, automation, querying, model evaluation

Teamwork Cloud fully conforms to the**SysML v2 API and Services**specification, ensuring interoperability with external tools and deployment of automated MBSE workflows.

- Programmatic access – create, edit, query, retrieve, and evaluate models directly from scripts or applications.
- Seamless integration – connect with data analytics platforms, DevOps CI/CD pipelines, and custom engineering workflows.
- Custom API extensions – query and analyze SysML v2 model more efficiently with CATIA Magic's home-grown Model Evaluation services. Stay tuned for more exciting API extensions!

This combination enables engineers to effectively**navigate, query, and analyze SysML v2 models**with full standard compliance and interoperability in mind.

##### Collaborative Modeling Through Teamwork Cloud

**Teamwork Cloud (TWC)** enables secure, lock-free collaborative modeling for SysML v2.

- Simultaneous collaboration – multiple users can work on the same model at the same time.
- Automatic merging – non-conflicting changes are merged automatically; intuitive resolution for overlaps.
- Versioned & modular models – decomposition into manageable units, supporting efficient collaboration across teams, organizations, and geographies. Supports collaborative development in branches for experimentation, with reintegration through a model patch mechanism.
- Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) – fine-grained permissions at the project or branch level to protect data access, ensuring that users and user groups have appropriate access rights.
- Support for U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) requirements
  - Provides support for Data Markings/Classification based on the DoD Manual 5200.01 Volume 2 specification, allowing to mark security-sensitive data such as users and projects with custom-defined data markings
  - Data spillage handling via dedicated tools for physical removal of model data affected by data spillage incidents
  - On-premise deployment for isolated classified programs
  - Prime-sub model exchange workflows built with scalability and IP protection in mind
- Standards-based SAML authentication integrations – supports single sign-on including built-in, LDAP, PKI/CAC, and SAML-based authentication.
- Auditing – provides access to audit reports to track user access and permissions granted for server projects, ensuring compliance and traceability.
- Cross-cluster synchronization – enables model updates to be shared securely across multiple server clusters, facilitating distributed collaboration.

Integration with 3DEXPERIENCE enables large-scale projects and supports multi-discipline integration (electrical, fluidics, thermal, etc.).

700400

##### MagicLab (Web-based SysML v2 Model Reader)

MagicLab is a new browser-based environment for viewing SysML v2 models. It provides read-only access to projects stored in Teamwork Cloud, eliminating the need to publish or export models for review. Users can open diagrams, tables, and model elements directly in the browser, with changes appearing instantly when the model is updated in the modeling tool or via the SysML v2 REST API. MagicLab also includes a textual representation of the SysML v2 syntax and supports lightweight model sharing across teams without requiring local installation.

33%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

33%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

33%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Integration and Data Synchronization

CATIA DataHub now supports the **Copy Data** operation for **SysML v2 projects**, available for **DOORS**, **DOORS Next (DNG)**, **JAMA Connect**, and **ENOVIA Requirements**connectors, with standard properties such as **Declared Name**and **Documentation Body**included in mapping support.

700400
