Sheet</h3><p>For your convenience, the Dassault Systèmes and OOSE co-authored SysML v2 quick sheet is available for download below as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language using our SysML v2 solution. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SysML v2 Quick Sheet.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Click the image, then click Download in the top-right corner to download the full file.</h6><h3>Standard Conformance</h3><p>Full compliance with the SysML v2 standard is at the core of our implementation.</p><ul><li data-uuid="4644ff3f-2489-41d7-9da7-f89b3b15faed"><strong>Built on principle</strong><span> </span>– our team makes standard compliance a core value; every engineer is committed to it.</li><li data-uuid="80815bfd-785e-4f6b-a2eb-5bfb71159a32"><strong>Interoperability</strong><span> </span>– models exchange seamlessly with other tools following the standard.</li><li data-uuid="c97685f5-6c44-469e-a829-06cdc21e143f"><strong>Ecosystem-ready</strong><span> </span>– full conformance simplifies integration across the partner ecosystem.</li><li data-uuid="c61e57cc-638c-4ef4-a873-e13d8170af14"><strong>Future-proofing</strong><span> </span>– no risk of vendor lock-in or compatibility gaps as the standard evolves.</li><li data-uuid="513f6d95-516b-43b5-b1cb-8f2d5379191b"><strong>Trust &amp; Transparency</strong><span> </span>– engineers know their models are pure SysML v2, not tool-specific variants.</li></ul><p>By delivering 100% standard conformance, Dassault Systèmes ensures that customers benefit from the<span> </span><strong>full power of SysML v2.</strong></p><h3>Text and Diagrams in Perfect Sync</h3><p>CATIA Magic/Cameo products deliver<span> </span><strong>seamless two-way synchronization</strong><span> </span>between<span> </span><strong>textual</strong><span> </span>and<span> </span><strong>graphical<span> </span></strong>SysML v2 model representations.</p><ul><li data-uuid="3762e455-2217-4d06-a8ba-72dd79564a4d"><strong>Instant consistency</strong><span> </span>– changes in text or diagrams are immediately reflected in both views.</li><li data-uuid="faf9100e-2822-4e24-909b-38ed45837395"><strong>Dual approach</strong><span> </span>– textual syntax appeals to software developers, while diagrams provide clarity for complex system architectures.</li><li data-uuid="99ed2649-777d-4761-9797-509ab4bdfa34"><strong>Unique capability</strong><span> </span>– currently the only solution supporting<span> </span><strong>100% of the SysML v2 standard</strong><span> </span>with true two-way synchronization.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image 1.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interfacedocs.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="StatesPicture.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><h3>Dynamic Views</h3><p>SysML v2 supports<span> </span><strong>three model visualization options</strong>:</p><ul><li data-uuid="74631d28-1875-47ab-9773-7b9c1a4c1459"><strong>Graphical</strong></li><li data-uuid="83b305e0-d185-4632-9b84-a77602b65d50"><strong>Tabular</strong></li><li data-uuid="1f4d335e-4081-4b57-b54d-eb21d214f005"><strong>Textual</strong></li></ul><p>In CATIA Magic/Cameo, users can<span> </span><strong>dynamically generate graphical and tabular views</strong><span> </span>by specifying<span> </span><strong>expressions that query the model</strong>.</p><ul><li data-uuid="1ec7b541-d153-4049-9950-f25c829b64a4"><strong>Automatic updates</strong><span> </span>– views can be rebuilt automatically when the model changes, saving systems engineers significant time and effort.</li><li data-uuid="c9b93767-6bc4-432d-8d91-d1df024428e2"><strong>Reusable definitions</strong><span> </span>– view definitions can be stored in libraries and reused across projects and teams.</li><li data-uuid="0f6e383e-00d9-4895-8181-1c2f7dd89492"><strong>Efficient modeling</strong><span> </span>– eliminates manual adjustments.</li></ul><p>Dynamic views are one of the most powerful capabilities in CATIA Magic/Cameo, enabling<span> </span><strong>efficient, consistent, and reusable visualizations</strong><span> </span>throughout the organization.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3624b352-c119-4ccc-8476-3955ef13a6ba"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jPqD5CJUyBo" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e03f446e-3d51-4878-8d08-9c3325090ac8"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27260bd2-a1de-472a-8bac-aab5d279eb4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d741134d-91c8-4a52-a681-57f435c44f9c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qqCzpN4d264" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e46a6ab8-c3bb-4e9a-b99f-1270691d80bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6dc2596a-533b-4a66-8059-8f4c4ff628c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3dSCLmsfKv4" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Model Evaluation</h3><p>SysML v2 introduces a powerful expression language for concise navigation and analysis of models. Using dot‑notation for feature chaining and collection operators such as sum(), complex queries can be written in a compact, intuitive form. Expressions are evaluated in full model context, honoring inheritance, redefinitions, subsets, and library functions.</p><p>CATIA Magic/Cameo enhances this with features that make analysis seamless:</p><ul><li data-uuid="db52b756-7897-4601-b231-cc0bc206bca3"><strong>Requirements verification<span> </span></strong>– any requirement violations are immediately flagged, providing instant traceability to violated constraints.</li><li data-uuid="259ae354-c755-4e8a-92d2-3bd9bc6b917a"><strong>Roll-ups &amp; trade studies</strong><span> </span>– apply roll-up patterns, perform “what-if” analyses, and conduct trade-off studies directly on the model.</li><li data-uuid="dfd3cbcd-c8cd-4e1b-8fb7-2da570fec6dd"><strong>Evaluation in Model or Console</strong><span> </span>– run expressions directly within the model or use the Evaluation Console to execute custom expressions without modifying the model.</li><li data-uuid="c6d71e6e-6a2a-4cae-8f7d-f0aee97583c4"><strong>Advanced evaluation</strong><span> </span>– supports analysis at both model and meta-model levels.</li><li data-uuid="69001929-7a75-47b6-ba96-58853a7cad5c"><strong>Interactive diagrams</strong><span> </span>– adjust initial values to test system behavior without modifying the model.</li></ul><p>This combination of language and product capabilities<span> </span><strong>bridges modeling and analysis</strong>, enabling<span> </span><strong>data-driven decision-making</strong><span> </span>and<span> </span><strong>full traceability from design exploration to validation</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Model Evaluation 1.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Model Evaluation 2.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Model Evaluation 3.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52b78337-9849-471e-862f-ab634e190422"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zX0UR_ibLbw" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Adopt SysML v2 at Your Own Pace</h3><p>Organizations can adopt SysML v2 at their own pace thanks to<span> the </span><strong>full co-existence of SysML v2 with SysML v1</strong>. Both versions can be authored in the same CATIA Magic/Cameo modeling environment, with the ability to select SysML v1 or v2 per project. Migration is supported by the SysML v2 transformation specification, ensuring legacy models remain usable and protecting existing investments.</p><p>Key features:</p><ul><li data-uuid="125949f1-ddbb-4857-b0b3-eccbc7827ef5"><strong>Authoring</strong><span> </span>– co-existence of SysML v1 and v2 in the same environment<ul><li>Choose v1 or v2 on a per-project basis</li><li>Support for SysML v2 transformation specifications for smooth migration, including diagrams</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="ce64baf9-7515-4d36-8bff-ca03e8279bde"><strong>API</strong><span> </span>– server REST API continuity<ul><li>Full SysML v2 standard API support</li><li>Continue using existing server REST API for both v1 and v2 projects</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="0a35b6eb-7c33-4356-97ff-3f2c976d17c1"><strong>Simulation</strong><span> </span>– use case continuity from v1<ul><li>Core SysML v1 simulation use cases supported in SysML v2</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="4db31cf9-49b3-4b10-a4d9-8afd0515bc98"><strong>User experience</strong><span> </span>– focus on usability<ul><li>Best features from SysML v1 implementation reused to maximize user efficiency</li></ul></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration Progress.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-icon-accent-blue,#1d7afc);"><strong> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95e4c1fa-7298-4e25-be73-761ae5690911"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P7B2nzaT-F4" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></span></p><h3><span>Standard SysML v2 REST API: CI/CD workflows, automation, querying, model evaluation</span></h3><p>Teamwork Cloud fully conforms to the<span> </span><strong>SysML v2 API and Services</strong><span> </span>specification, ensuring interoperability with external tools and deployment of automated MBSE workflows.</p><ul><li data-uuid="b94365ab-4cb7-481a-920c-59ce61b647f2"><strong>Programmatic access</strong><span> </span>– create, edit, query, retrieve, and evaluate models directly from scripts or applications.</li><li data-uuid="ff8d4046-74da-4db4-bd29-e4b0843962e8"><strong>Seamless integration</strong><span> </span>– connect with data analytics platforms, DevOps CI/CD pipelines, and custom engineering workflows.</li><li data-uuid="9a1cd27d-f080-48df-a15d-75a5cf2e5ecb"><strong>Custom API extensions</strong><span> </span>– query and analyze SysML v2 model more efficiently with CATIA Magic's home-grown Model Evaluation services.<span> </span>Stay tuned for more exciting API extensions!</li></ul><p>This combination enables engineers to effectively<span> </span><strong>navigate, query, and analyze SysML v2 models</strong><span> </span>with full standard compliance and interoperability in mind.</p><h3>Collaborative Modeling Through Teamwork Cloud</h3><p><strong>Teamwork Cloud (TWC)</strong> enables secure, lock-free collaborative modeling for SysML v2.</p><ul><li data-uuid="6ad8b3b3-91f6-4814-aaa8-a2d562349404"><strong>Simultaneous collaboration</strong> – multiple users can work on the same model at the same time.</li><li data-uuid="a11f79a8-0ac6-4370-9d08-271d6d8a6390"><strong>Automatic merging</strong> – non-conflicting changes are merged automatically; intuitive resolution for overlaps.</li><li data-uuid="75b7f8bd-7526-48f4-96e8-cb4245474daa"><strong>Versioned &amp; modular models</strong> – decomposition into manageable units, supporting efficient collaboration across teams, organizations, and geographies. Supports collaborative development in branches for experimentation, with reintegration through a model patch mechanism.</li><li data-uuid="57458b45-06d3-4f87-a848-94907fe99361"><strong>Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)</strong> – fine-grained permissions at the project or branch level to protect data access, ensuring that users and user groups have appropriate access rights.</li><li data-uuid="529ca520-b0c3-4b30-81b6-05f89e9020da"><strong>Support for U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) requirements</strong> <ul><li>Provides support for Data Markings/Classification based on the DoD Manual 5200.01 Volume 2 specification, allowing to mark security-sensitive data such as users and projects with custom-defined data markings</li><li>Data spillage handling via dedicated tools for physical removal of model data affected by data spillage incidents</li><li>On-premise deployment for isolated classified programs</li><li>Prime-sub model exchange workflows built with scalability and IP protection in mind</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="2c711d9a-29d8-4403-b3e2-501e49f303e3"><strong>Standards-based SAML authentication integrations</strong> – supports single sign-on including built-in, LDAP, PKI/CAC, and SAML-based authentication.</li><li data-uuid="2630b12f-d5e2-4334-b057-ee81d1689aa3"><strong>Auditing</strong> – provides access to audit reports to track user access and permissions granted for server projects, ensuring compliance and traceability.</li><li data-uuid="c1167162-20c4-4c47-8f73-b503ce3cc075"><strong>Cross-cluster synchronization</strong> – enables model updates to be shared securely across multiple server clusters, facilitating distributed collaboration.</li></ul><p>Integration with 3DEXPERIENCE enables large-scale projects and supports multi-discipline integration (electrical, fluidics, thermal, etc.).</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b0a6841-02ae-4249-abc9-06c186ab8cfd"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x1lLHDEvusk" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>MagicLab (Web-based SysML v2 Model Reader)</h3><p>MagicLab is a new browser-based environment for viewing SysML v2 models. It provides read-only access to projects stored in Teamwork Cloud, eliminating the need to publish or export models for review. Users can open diagrams, tables, and model elements directly in the browser, with changes appearing instantly when the model is updated in the modeling tool or via the SysML v2 REST API. MagicLab also includes a textual representation of the SysML v2 syntax and supports lightweight model sharing across teams without requiring local installation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="294b9818-a3f5-4088-9dc8-d83ac184ef8b"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0069aa0-b4f1-4797-b7f6-43c75a470738"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">33%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 1.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f162b0ea-b3f8-4ecb-8a84-506da3ed2f0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">33%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 3.png" /></ac:image> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb185a1-38ff-44b1-9527-f62769a92710"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">33%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 2.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><h3>Integration and Data Synchronization</h3><p>CATIA DataHub now supports the <strong>Copy Data</strong> operation for <strong>SysML v2 projects</strong>, available for <strong>DOORS</strong>, <strong>DOORS Next (DNG)</strong>, <strong>JAMA Connect</strong>, and <strong>ENOVIA Requirements </strong>connectors, with standard properties such as <strong>Declared Name </strong>and <strong>Documentation Body </strong>included in mapping support.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35134cc1-db38-4559-9e34-c552bb84b65e"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jcnk9Xp7CPo" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p>
````

## PAGE 00234: (2026x) Data Markings and Classification Plugin

- page_id: `272747805`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747805/2026x+Data+Markings+and+Classification+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-22T09:47:12.917+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

The Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2026xrelease introduces new project options for enhanced Data Markings control and visibility. TheShow Colors of Data Markingsoption lets you manage the color display of Data Markings, while theShow Data Markings in Referencesoption allows you to control the Data Markings visibility on element types, Element Properties compartments, and realizing connectors with Item Flows containing sensitive Conveyed Items. TheInvisible Data MarkingsandInvalid Diagram Markingsvalidation rules have also been enhanced with a new solver,Show Conveyed Information, to help detect and display hidden sensitive Conveyed Item labels. Additionally, when exporting tables to Excel, relevant portion markings are now automatically included in the file name.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&version=2026x).

##### Controlling the Display of Data Markings on References

A new project option,**Show Data Markings in References**, has been introduced. This option allows you to enable or disable the visibility of Data Markings on element types, Element Properties compartments, and realizing connectors with Item Flows that contain sensitive Conveyed Items.[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=project-options&version=2026x)

Additionally, the **Invisible Data Markings** and **Invalid Diagram Markings** validation rules have been enhanced to consider realizing connectors with Item Flows that include sensitive Conveyed Items. A new solver, **Show Conveyed Information**, has been added to help detect and display any hidden sensitive Conveyed Item labels. Learn more [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=invisible-data-markings&version=2026x) and [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=invalid-diagram-markings&version=2026x)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data Markings are displayed for element types in element properties compartment when the project option **Show Data Markings in References**is enabled.

##### Controlling the Data Marking Color Display

A new project option,**Show Colors of Data Markings**, has been introduced. This option enables or disables the display of colors for Data Markings within the project, enhancing the visual differentiation of elements.[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/DMP/?contextKey=project-options&version=2026x)

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Data Marking colors are displayed in the default black when the **Show Colors of Data Markings**project option is disabled.

##### Data Markings in Diagram Name Upon Table Export

When exporting a table to an Excel file, the appropriate portion markings are now automatically added to the exported file's name. These portion markings reflect the Data Markings displayed in the diagram banner of the exported table.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The table *Sensitive Information* is being exported to an Excel file. The file is given the same name as the exported table. The exported file's name also contains portion markings that correspond to the Data Markings of the exported table's diagram banner.

##### Documentation

- Data Markings and Classification Plugin 2026x

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747431 space=VN version=6 -->
## PAGE 00237: (2026x) Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `272747431`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747431/2026x+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-06-01T10:19:53.808+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on November 28, 2025

Version 2026x introduces customizable login screens via the Admin UI, allowing users to upload logos, set background colors, and add messages with live previews. The release fully supports the OMG SysML v2 API standard, including services like project navigation, versioning, and querying, with improved indexing management through a new tabbed interface. A major addition is **MagicLab**, a web-based SysML v2 model reader that enables real-time, read-only model viewing and sharing directly from Teamwork Cloud.

#### NOTE: 2026x Hot Fix 3 available

2026x Hot Fix 3 available

Due to the Kryo serialization vulnerability issues (CVE-2026-785), the 2026x HF3 has been released on May 29, 2026.

For more information, see the Knowledge Base article at[https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613](https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613).

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### aLogin Screen Customization via UI

You can now customize the login screen directly via the Teamwork Cloud Admin UI. Located in the Settings app, this capability includes uploading a custom logo (PNG, JPEG, SVG), setting a background color, and adding a styled message. For an even greater convenience, a live preview displays changes in real time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing the login screen via the Teamwork Cloud Admin user interface.

##### bStandard SysML v2 API Support

Teamwork Cloud 2026x fully conforms to the OMG SysML v2 API and Services standard (REST/HTTP PSM mapping). Specifically, all of the standard sevices in the following service groups are supported:

- Project Service
- Element Navigation Service
- Project and Data Versioning Service
- Query Service
- External Relationship Service
- Project Usage Service

In addition to exposing the above-mentioned services, Teamwork Cloud adheres to derived property conformance rules. Please note, though, in order to fully utilize the Query Services, it‘s recommended to index SysML v2 models first.

###### b1New SysML v2 API Querying Management Tab

The Indexing Management page now supports configuration for both Global Element Usage Search and SysML v2 API Querying via a tabbed interface. Each tab displays its respective indexing configurations, with contextual descriptions shown when no entries are present. This update organizes indexing management into clearly separated views for easier maintenance.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SysML v2 API Querying tab in the Indexing management page.

##### cOther

- The installation files have been completely refactored to improve installation speed, enhance configuration flexibility, and reduce the number of installation packages. Focused efforts to improve various deployment strategies resulted in the elimination of standalone Web Application Platform installation files. Moving forward, Web Application Platform can be deployed using the unified Teamwork Cloud and Magic Collaboration Studio installer. Additionally, the ”no-install“ concept has been removed from server-side deployments. It was replaced by ZIP installation files that no longer contain *.war files; instead, the *.war files are now provided in a separate archive.
- Added support for a separate Teamwork Cloud Admin custom truststore , using it alongside cacerts to construct the SSL context for remote resource configurations with TLS.

### MagicLab

One of the key updates in 2026x is **MagicLab**, a new web-based SysML v2 model reader. MagicLab provides read-only access to SysML v2 projects, allowing users to open and view models directly from Teamwork Cloud without the need for publishing. Integrated under the same Teamwork Cloud license, it supports instant diagram updates from modeling tools or REST API changes, displays tables, includes a SysML v2 textual syntax viewer, and offers model-sharing capabilities.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Documentation

- Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud 2026x

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The release fully supports the OMG SysML v2 API standard, including services like project navigation, versioning, and querying, with improved indexing management through a new tabbed interface. A major addition is <strong>MagicLab</strong>, a web-based SysML v2 model reader that enables real-time, read-only model viewing and sharing directly from Teamwork Cloud.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0c0b321-14d3-4507-9d7e-47b71b1eb931"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2026x Hot Fix 3 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Due to the <span>Kryo serialization vulnerability issues (CVE-2026-785)</span>, the 2026x HF3 has been released on May 29, 2026.</p><p><span data-teams="true"><span>For more information, see the Knowledge Base article at </span><a href="https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613" class="fui-Link ___1q1shib f2hkw1w f3rmtva f1ewtqcl fyind8e f1k6fduh f1w7gpdv fk6fouc fjoy568 figsok6 f1s184ao f1mk8lai fnbmjn9 f1o700av f13mvf36 f1cmlufx f9n3di6 f1ids18y f1tx3yz7 f1deo86v f1eh06m1 f1iescvh fhgqx19 f1olyrje f1p93eir f1nev41a f1h8hb77 f1lqvz6u f10aw75t fsle3fq f17ae5zn"><span>https://support.3ds.com/knowledge-base/?q=docid:QA00000455613</span></a><span>.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Teamwork Cloud</h2><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3826677e-1c54-4a25-a962-a33eeeaa6ea4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">a</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Login Screen Customization via UI </h3><p>You can now customize the login screen directly via the Teamwork Cloud Admin UI. For an even greater convenience, a live preview displays changes in real time.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="login_screen_customization.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Customizing the login screen via the Teamwork Cloud Admin user interface.</h6><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43838695-4c7e-455c-9d20-8ec18793c86d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">b</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Standard SysML v2 API Support</h3><p>Teamwork Cloud 2026x fully conforms to the OMG SysML v2 API and Services standard (REST/HTTP PSM mapping). Specifically, all of the standard sevices in the following service groups are supported:</p><ul><li data-uuid="05027a51-ca7c-4729-91b4-8494203f2178">Project Service</li><li data-uuid="79ae45d4-61c0-4c47-8e0f-8b8b994b97d0">Element Navigation Service</li><li data-uuid="c2bf1fad-3488-453d-aa4f-e18727bf91db">Project and Data Versioning Service</li><li data-uuid="59ed617d-6d34-43ff-9cc6-511277a8b929">Query Service</li><li data-uuid="440d3f49-c246-4687-b9de-5d1d163a15e8">External Relationship Service</li><li data-uuid="64cf1e57-db35-4798-acd3-6614421f9447">Project Usage Service</li></ul><p>In addition to exposing the above-mentioned services, Teamwork Cloud adheres to derived property conformance rules. Please note, though, in order to fully utilize the Query Services, it‘s recommended to index SysML v2 models first.</p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a82d563-c7f5-461d-bedd-3a9834ebad37"><ac:parameter ac:name="">b1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>New SysML v2 API Querying Management Tab</h4><p>The Indexing Management page now supports configuration for both Global Element Usage Search and SysML v2 API Querying via a tabbed interface. Each tab displays its respective indexing configurations, with contextual descriptions shown when no entries are present. This update organizes indexing management into clearly separated views for easier maintenance.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sysmlv2_querying_twc_admin.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">SysML v2 API Querying tab in the Indexing management page.</h6><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b9946f5-ab05-48c3-8c2d-1d40c176cb5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">c</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other</h3><ul><li data-uuid="0b278214-7667-419e-a817-093a44d2fcfc">The <strong>installation files</strong> have been completely refactored to improve installation speed, enhance configuration flexibility, and reduce the number of installation packages. Focused efforts to improve various deployment strategies resulted in the elimination of standalone Web Application Platform installation files. <span>Moving forward, Web Application Platform can be deployed using the unified Teamwork Cloud and Magic Collaboration Studio installer. Additionally, the ”no-install“ concept has been removed from server-side deployments. It was replaced by ZIP installation files that no longer contain *.war files; instead, the *.war files are now provided in a separate archive.</span></li><li data-uuid="aa45fe5a-ae8b-4ab6-80fa-14ffb3944529">Added support for a separate Teamwork Cloud Admin custom <strong>truststore</strong>, using it alongside <em>cacerts </em>to construct the SSL context for remote resource configurations with TLS.</li></ul><h1>MagicLab</h1><p>One of the key updates in 2026x is <strong>MagicLab</strong>, a new web-based SysML v2 model reader. MagicLab provides read-only access to SysML v2 projects, allowing users to open and view models directly from Teamwork Cloud without the need for publishing. Integrated under the same Teamwork Cloud license, it supports instant diagram updates from modeling tools or REST API changes, displays tables, includes a SysML v2 textual syntax viewer, and offers model-sharing capabilities.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab_overview.png" /></ac:image></p><h2 style="text-align: left;">Documentation</h2><ul><li data-uuid="8d9ef490-d199-491f-84e6-ead6b947f471"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/?contextKey=magic-collaboration-studio-teamwork-cloud-and-services&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud 2026x</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747234 space=VN version=3 -->
## PAGE 00239: (2026x) Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `272747234`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747234/2026x+Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-03-17T07:47:34.921+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on November 28. 2025

In this release, we bring key enhancements and updates across simulation and modeling workflows. The HTML UI has been improved for a smoother and more interactive simulation experience. Result Player now offers enhanced usability and output navigation. Server-side simulation setup is simplified with a new license configuration process. Modelica export now supports Expandable Connectors, enabling more flexible integration of components. Additionally, certain outdated integrations have been discontinued to streamline the toolset and improve overall performance.

General modeling capabilities introduce the Project History panel for Teamwork Cloud projects, providing quick access to commit messages and the project’s branch structure. For **3D**EXPERIENCE platform projects, you can now create Collaborative Spaces specifically for standard profiles and libraries, ensuring they are always stored together in an accessible, shared location.

General modeling improvements include path-variable support in Excel/CSV Sync Options and Import dialogs, the deprecation of several legacy engineering sets, the introduction of a new Non-Profit Research license category, and expanded academic license options.

#### Simulation

##### HTML UI Generation Integrated into Simulation Plugin

In this release, the Simulation plugin introduces integrated HTML UI generation, streamlining the simulation workflow. The Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit plugin has been removed, and its core functionality is now integrated into the Simulation plugin. The **Start Web Server** property is renamed to **Show UI as HTML**. Before running the **SimulationConfig**, if the property is set to *true*, the Simulation plugin automatically generates HTML UI files, opens them in the embedded browser, and lists their hyperlinks in the Simulation Console tab. If the property is set to *false*, a Java-based UI is launched in an embedded browser. You can customize these HTML files at any time and regenerate them using the Generate HTML button on the toolbar of the UI modeling diagram.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of a Simulation Configuration with the Show UI As HTML property set to true to auto-generate HTML files.

##### Server-side Simulation - License setup update

In this release, you can now set up licenses directly through the GUI using the Settings application. If the license setup is already defined in the properties file, the GUI option will be disabled. However, if no license configuration is specified in the properties file, you can use the GUI for setup. To learn more, refer to the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud licensing' space='TWCloudTWRT']page.

##### Ability to Send/Receive Signal via redefined port

The Simulation toolkit can now detect if a port was redefined in your specific system and send/receive Signals via redefined ports without altering the original Behavioral design.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The p1 port being redefined by p11 port in the Variables pane.

##### Modelica Export – Expandable connectors support

In this release, a new stereotype**ModelicaBus**has been introduced to support the export of expandable connectors to Modelica.Expandable connectors, commonly used to define busses, can now be exported when this stereotype is applied to an Interface Block that types the Proxy Ports. In such cases, the generated Modelica file will include an**Expandable Connector**instead of a**Simple Connector**, enabling more flexible component integration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### ModelicaBus present in the Applied Stereotype property cell

##### Math Engine Improvements

- Arrays in simulation are now set as 1*x matrix to the MathEngine instead of x*1. This allows you to use array-based functions without transposing the matrix.
- Arrays within arrays are now automatically unwrapped and can be used for math operations. For example a=[1,2,3]; b=[4,5,6]; c=[a,b]. This would give c result of [1,2,3,4,5,6]
- “MATLAB only” – String properties are now set to the MATLAB workspace as a String variable instead of a Char Set.

##### Result Player – New shortcuts

In this release, the Results Player is improved to support easier navigation through failures. When requirements are verified and some steps are failing, the **Step Forward** and **Step Backward** buttons now jump directly to the failure points. If you hold the **CTRL** key, these buttons let you move between individual failed steps. New keyboard shortcuts have also been added: press **Space** to play or pause, **Home** to go to the beginning, and **End** to jump to the end. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CSTTWRT'] page.

##### Discontinued Integrations

Starting from version 2026x, the Cameo Simulation Toolkit will no longer support the old SIMULIA API. Previously, projects had the option to choose between the old and new APIs. With this change, only the new SIMULIA API will be supported, ensuring improved performance and future compatibility. The new API was introduced in Platform version 2024x FD01; hence, the older platforms will be incompatible for Simulia integration.

#### Modeling and Infrastructure

- Path variables can now be used in the Excel/CSV Sync Options and Import dialogs. This feature provides greater sharing flexibility by allowing you to store file paths as named variables in your project, which can be easily adjusted on different machines, making it easier to collaborate across teams and environments. Learn more [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Excel-and-CSV-Import-dialog&version=2026x&variant=default) and [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Sync-with-Excel-or-CSV-files&version=2026x&variant=default)
- Deprecated XML Schema, CORBA IDL, C++ and Java CE engineering sets. You can use the [CATIA Software Producer](https://help.3ds.com/2026/English/DSCATIAMagicDoc/CatEspMagicUserMap/catesp-c-ov.htm) plugin for code generation from Java and C++.
- Oracle DB and PosgreSQL drivers are no longer distributed with the modeling tool. Download and add the needed drivers according to the database type and version. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Code-Engineering-Sets&version=2026x&variant=default)
- A new modeling tool license category, Non-Profit Research, has been introduced for Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Magic Cyber Systems Engineer modeling tools, as well as Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer, Magic Model Analyst, Magic Systems EE Architect, and Magic Systems Safety Engineer plugins. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=Modeling-tools-and-plugins-licensing&version=2026x)
- Academic licenses are now available for Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer, Magic Systems EE Architect, and Magic Systems Safety Engineer plugins.

#### Collaboration

##### Teamwork Cloud

###### The Project History Panel

In version 2026x, Teamwork Cloud introduces the **Project History** panel, which can be opened with just a few clicks. It saves time when reviewing commit messages or investigating the branch structure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Project History panel.

##### **3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

###### Collaborative Spaces for Standard Profiles

You can now create Collaborative Spaces specifically for standard profiles and libraries. This ensures profiles and libraries are always stored together in an accessible, shared space. It also prevents users from accidentally adding profiles to the spaces that others cannot access—for example, when creating a project, adding a project to the server, or migrating profiles.

#### Documentation

- [Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler 2026x](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=modeling-tools-documentation&version=2026x&variant=Magic%20Cyber%20Systems%20Engineer%20%2F%20Cameo%20Systems%20Modeler)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747700 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00240: (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

- page_id: `272747700`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747700/2026x+Magic+Model+Analyst+Cameo+Simulation+Toolkit
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-28T10:41:46.720+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on November 28. 2025

In this release, we bring key enhancements and updates across simulation and modeling workflows. The HTML UI has been improved for a smoother and more interactive simulation experience. Result Player now offers enhanced usability and output navigation. Server-side simulation setup is simplified with a new license configuration process. Modelica export now supports Expandable Connectors, enabling more flexible integration of components. Additionally, certain outdated integrations have been discontinued to streamline the toolset and improve overall performance.

##### HTML UI Generation Integrated into Simulation Plugin

In this release, the Simulation plugin introduces integrated HTML UI generation, streamlining the simulation workflow. The Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit plugin has been removed, and its core functionality is now integrated into the Simulation plugin. The **Start Web Server** property is renamed to **Show UI as HTML**. Before running the **SimulationConfig**, if the property is set to *true*, the Simulation plugin automatically generates HTML UI files, opens them in the embedded browser, and lists their hyperlinks in the Simulation Console tab. If the property is set to *false*, a Java-based UI is launched in an embedded browser. You can customize these HTML files at any time and regenerate them using the Generate HTML button on the toolbar of the UI modeling diagram.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of a Simulation Configuration with the Show UI As HTML property set to true to auto-generate HTML files.

##### Server-side Simulation - License setup update

In this release, you can now set up licenses directly through the GUI using the Settings application. If the license setup is already defined in the properties file, the GUI option will be disabled. However, if no license configuration is specified in the properties file, you can use the GUI for setup. To learn more, refer to the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud licensing' space='TWCloudTWRT']page.

##### Ability to Send/Receive Signal via redefined port

The Simulation toolkit can now detect if a port was redefined in your specific system and send/receive Signals via redefined ports without altering the original Behavioral design.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The p1 port being redefined by p11 port in the Variables pane.

##### Modelica Export – Expandable connectors support

In this release, a new stereotype**ModelicaBus**has been introduced to support the export of expandable connectors to Modelica.Expandable connectors, commonly used to define busses, can now be exported when this stereotype is applied to an Interface Block that types the Proxy Ports. In such cases, the generated Modelica file will include an**Expandable Connector**instead of a**Simple Connector**, enabling more flexible component integration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### ModelicaBus present in the Applied Stereotype property cell

##### Math Engine Improvements

- Arrays in simulation are now set as 1*x matrix to the MathEngine instead of x*1. This allows you to use array-based functions without transposing the matrix.
- Arrays within arrays are now automatically unwrapped and can be used for math operations. For example a=[1,2,3]; b=[4,5,6]; c=[a,b]. This would give c result of [1,2,3,4,5,6]
- “MATLAB only” – String properties are now set to the MATLAB workspace as a String variable instead of a Char Set.

##### Result Player – New shortcuts

In this release, the Results Player is improved to support easier navigation through failures. When requirements are verified and some steps are failing, the **Step Forward** and **Step Backward** buttons now jump directly to the failure points. If you hold the **CTRL** key, these buttons let you move between individual failed steps. New keyboard shortcuts have also been added: press **Space** to play or pause, **Home** to go to the beginning, and **End** to jump to the end. To learn more, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Results Player' space='CSTTWRT'] page.

##### Discontinued Integrations

Starting from version 2026x, the Cameo Simulation Toolkit will no longer support the old SIMULIA API. Previously, projects had the option to choose between the old and new APIs. With this change, only the new SIMULIA API will be supported, ensuring improved performance and future compatibility. The new API was introduced in Platform version 2024x FD01; hence, the older platforms will be incompatible for Simulia integration.

##### Documentation

- Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2026x

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In such cases, the generated Modelica file will include an </span><strong>Expandable Connector</strong><span> instead of a </span><strong>Simple Connector</strong><span>, enabling more flexible component integration.<br /><br /></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Expandable Connectors support for Modelica export.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>ModelicaBus present in the Applied Stereotype property cell</span></h6><h3><span>Math Engine Improvements</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="38d5a060-348c-47bb-bb5a-1f6f87c98710">Arrays in simulation are now set as 1*x matrix to the MathEngine instead of x*1. This allows you to use array-based functions without transposing the matrix.</li><li data-uuid="1a5b3444-a370-4e3f-8a2b-31b906501677">Arrays within arrays are now automatically unwrapped and can be used for math operations. For example a=[1,2,3]; b=[4,5,6]; c=[a,b]. This would give c result of [1,2,3,4,5,6]</li><li data-uuid="004f8cb7-d59b-42bb-8a13-128a6e99f199">“MATLAB only” – String properties are now set to the MATLAB workspace as a String variable instead of a Char Set.</li></ul><h3>Result Player – New shortcuts</h3><p>In this release, the Results Player is improved to support easier navigation through failures. When requirements are verified and some steps are failing, the <strong>Step Forward</strong> and <strong>Step Backward</strong> buttons now jump directly to the failure points. If you hold the <strong>CTRL</strong> key, these buttons let you move between individual failed steps. New keyboard shortcuts have also been added: press <strong>Space</strong> to play or pause, <strong>Home</strong> to go to the beginning, and <strong>End</strong> to jump to the end. To learn more, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CSTTWRT" ri:content-title="Results Player" /></ac:link> page.</p><h3>Discontinued Integrations</h3><p>Starting from version 2026x, the Cameo Simulation Toolkit will no longer support the old SIMULIA API. Previously, projects had the option to choose between the old and new APIs. With this change, only the new SIMULIA API will be supported, ensuring improved performance and future compatibility. T<span>he new API was introduced in Platform version 2024x FD01; hence, the older platforms will be incompatible for Simulia integration.</span></p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="08593a07-b1de-48d4-92e2-a045ee6314a0"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MMA/?contextKey=magic-model-analyst-cameo-simulation-toolkit-documentation&amp;version=2026x">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit 2026x</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747014 space=VN version=5 -->
## PAGE 00241: (2026x) Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw

- page_id: `272747014`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747014/2026x+Magic+Software+Architect+MagicDraw
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-03-17T07:45:43.874+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

###### Released on: November 28. 2025

The 2026x version release introduces the Project History panel for Teamwork Cloud projects, providing quick access to commit messages and the project’s branch structure. For **3D**EXPERIENCE platform projects, you can now create Collaborative Spaces specifically for standard profiles and libraries, ensuring they are always stored together in an accessible, shared location.

General modeling improvements include path-variable support in Excel/CSV Sync Options and Import dialogs, the deprecation of several legacy engineering sets, the introduction of a new Non-Profit Research license category, and expanded academic license options.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&version=2026x). For further information, check the[product documentation](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=modeling-tools-documentation&version=2026x&variant=default).

#### Modeling and Infrastructure

- Path variables can now be used in the Excel/CSV Sync Options and Import dialogs. This feature provides greater sharing flexibility by allowing you to store file paths as named variables in your project, which can be easily adjusted on different machines, making it easier to collaborate across teams and environments. Learn more [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Excel-and-CSV-Import-dialog&version=2026x&variant=default) and [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Sync-with-Excel-or-CSV-files&version=2026x&variant=default)
- Deprecated XML Schema, CORBA IDL, C++ and Java CE engineering sets. You can use the [CATIA Software Producer](https://help.3ds.com/2026/English/DSCATIAMagicDoc/CatEspMagicUserMap/catesp-c-ov.htm) plugin for code generation from Java and C++.
- Oracle DB and PosgreSQL drivers are no longer distributed with the modeling tool. Download and add the needed drivers according to the database type and version. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Code-Engineering-Sets&version=2026x&variant=default)
- A new modeling tool license category, Non-Profit Research, has been introduced for Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Magic Cyber Systems Engineer modeling tools, as well as Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer, Magic Model Analyst, Magic Systems EE Architect, and Magic Systems Safety Engineer plugins. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=Modeling-tools-and-plugins-licensing&version=2026x)
- Academic licenses are now available for Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer, Magic Systems EE Architect, and Magic Systems Safety Engineer plugins.

#### Collaboration

##### Teamwork Cloud

###### The Project History Panel

In version 2026x, Teamwork Cloud introduces the **Project History** panel, which can be opened with just a few clicks. It saves time when reviewing commit messages or investigating the branch structure.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/CM/?contextKey=reviewing-historical-versions-of-the-project&version=2026x)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Project History panel.

###### Saving Projects to Earlier Versions

Now you can save local projects to a previous major version—for example, from version 2026x to 2024x—making it easier to collaborate with teams using older modeling tool versions and maintain backward compatibility.

[Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=saving-projects-to-earlier-versions&version=2026x&variant=default)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Saving a project to an older version.

##### **3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

###### Collaborative Spaces for Standard Profiles

You can now create Collaborative Spaces specifically for standard profiles and libraries. This ensures profiles and libraries are always stored together in an accessible, shared space. It also prevents users from accidentally adding profiles to the spaces that others cannot access—for example, when creating a project, adding a project to the server, or migrating profiles.

#### Documentation

- [Magic Software Architect / MagicDraw 2026x](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=modeling-tools-documentation&version=2026x&variant=Magic%20Software%20Architect%20%2F%20MagicDraw)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747107 space=VN version=18 -->
## PAGE 00242: (2026x) Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `272747107`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747107/2026x+Magic+Systems+of+Systems+Architect+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture
- version_number: 18
- version_date: `2026-03-17T07:47:48.926+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

In the**2026x** release, we implemented the **UAF 1.3 standard**, which includes key features for Mission Engineering Modeling. The **UAF 1.3 Profile** is fully supported in UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks. Therefore, UAF 1.2 is no longer supported in this version. Moreover, the New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available.

General modeling capabilities introduce the Project History panel for Teamwork Cloud projects, providing quick access to commit messages and the project’s branch structure. For **3D**EXPERIENCE platform projects, you can now create Collaborative Spaces specifically for standard profiles and libraries, ensuring they are always stored together in an accessible, shared location.

General modeling improvements include path-variable support in Excel/CSV Sync Options and Import dialogs, the deprecation of several legacy engineering sets, the introduction of a new Non-Profit Research license category, and expanded academic license options.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&version=2026x). For further information, check the[product documentation](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=modeling-tools-documentation&version=2026x&variant=default).

#### UAF Features

##### Methods and Standards

Beginning with this version, the **UAF 1.3 Plugin** is available for production use. The UAF 1.3 Profile is fully supported in UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**IMPORTANT!**The UAF 1.2 Plugin is no longer supported starting with the 2026x version.

Projects created in earlier versions **require**the following migration before you can continue working with them:

- UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### UAF 1.3 Profile Support

600400

Together with the**UAF 1.3 Profile**, the following changes areimplementedto enable**Mission Engineering modeling in the 2026x version**:

- New elements, properties, and relationships were added. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. New elements' space='MT']>>]]>
- The measurements library was updated. New Force Designation enumeration added.
- New diagram in Strategic Processes:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Mission Phases diagram' space='MT']
- New diagrams in Operational Processes:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Mission Threads diagram' space='MT']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Mission Threads Flow diagram' space='MT']
- New diagram in Resources Processes:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Mission Engineering Threads diagram' space='MT']
- New diagram in Parameters:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram' space='MT']
- The measurement tables were updated to align with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes. Now you can add Activities and their specializations directly to the measurement tables, because Activities are treated as Property Sets and can own measurements. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Measurements' space='MT']>>]]>
- The Implementation Matrix was updated by adding two new pairs: Function to Actual Strategic Phase and Service Function to Operational Activity.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Implementation Matrix' space='MT']>>]]>
- The UAF template for the Cameo Collaborator was updated in accordance with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Library Support

New**DISR and UJTL library versions**are now available:

- UJTL (20250715)
- DISR (20240606)

You can choose the library version when creating a new project.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using libraries' space='MT']>>]]>

##### UAF/UPDM Compliance Mode for BPMN2 projects

The**“UPDM Profile for BPMN”**is no longer available in this version. All of its stereotypes have been moved to the existing**BPMN Customization Profile**. In addition, a new**UAF/UPDM compliance mode**has been introduced for BPMN projects, allowing you to choose whether the compliant UAF/UPDM stereotypes (depending on the plugin) should be applied to BPMN elements.

##### Validation

- The new correctness validation rules dedicated to checking if the client and supplier are correct have been introduced for the following relationships:
  - Opposes, Defines . New relationships, which were implemented with the UAF 1.3 Profile support.
  - Existing relationships: Phases, Compares To, Governed By, Maps To Goal, Owns Value, Creates, Enables, Evoked By, Presented By, Sequence
- The new correctness validation rules were implemented to verify whether the classifiers are correct for the following elements:
  - **Actual Mission Scenario**. The classifier must be Mission Scenario or its specialization.
  - Actual Mission Vignette . The classifier must be Mission Vignette or its specialization.
- The new correctness validation rule was implemented to verify whether the behavior is correct for the Mission Task Action . The behavior must be a Mission Task or its specialization.

##### Discontinued Products

The**AutoStyler plugin**is deprecated, with the intention of removing it with the next major version release, the 2028x version.

##### Other Enhancements

- A new backwards compatibility feature allows UPDM 2.1 projects created in the 2026x version to be *Saved As Older Version*in 2024x compatible file format. This makes them directly compatible with the UPDM 2.1 plugin in 2024x, allowing you to open and work with these projects immediately in the 2024x version. This approach does not apply to the UAF 1.3 projects. Learn more >>>
- The UAF Enterprise Architecture Framework is now renamed to**EA-BPMN**. The perspective and project creation template was already renamed in the 2024x version.
- The «ActualDODAFProperties» stereotype will no longer be applied automatically after creating instances for «DODAFProperties». Moreover, the « DODAFProperties» stereotype is renamed to **«UAFProperties»**.
- In this version, the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table was renamed to the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table' space='MT']>>]]>
- A new UAF BoK Sample is now available. This sample is based on the UAF WOW! book .
- This version includes new functionality that lets you select whether the Definition or Comment will serve as the Documentation for UAF elements.

#### Modeling and Infrastructure

- Path variables can now be used in the Excel/CSV Sync Options and Import dialogs. This feature provides greater sharing flexibility by allowing you to store file paths as named variables in your project, which can be easily adjusted on different machines, making it easier to collaborate across teams and environments. Learn more [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Excel-and-CSV-Import-dialog&version=2026x&variant=default) and [here >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Sync-with-Excel-or-CSV-files&version=2026x&variant=default)
- Deprecated XML Schema, CORBA IDL, C++ and Java CE engineering sets. You can use the [CATIA Software Producer](https://help.3ds.com/2026/English/DSCATIAMagicDoc/CatEspMagicUserMap/catesp-c-ov.htm) plugin for code generation from Java and C++.
- Oracle DB and PosgreSQL drivers are no longer distributed with the modeling tool. Download and add the needed drivers according to the database type and version. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Code-Engineering-Sets&version=2026x&variant=default)
- A new modeling tool license category, Non-Profit Research, has been introduced for Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Magic Cyber Systems Engineer modeling tools, as well as Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer, Magic Model Analyst, Magic Systems EE Architect, and Magic Systems Safety Engineer plugins. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=Modeling-tools-and-plugins-licensing&version=2026x)
- Academic licenses are now available for Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer, Magic Systems EE Architect, and Magic Systems Safety Engineer plugins.

#### Collaboration

##### Teamwork Cloud

###### The Project History Panel

In version 2026x, Teamwork Cloud introduces the **Project History** panel, which can be opened with just a few clicks. It saves time when reviewing commit messages or investigating the branch structure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Project History panel.

##### **3D**EXPERIENCE Platform

###### Collaborative Spaces for Standard Profiles

You can now create Collaborative Spaces specifically for standard profiles and libraries. This ensures profiles and libraries are always stored together in an accessible, shared space. It also prevents users from accidentally adding profiles to the spaces that others cannot access—for example, when creating a project, adding a project to the server, or migrating profiles.

#### Documentation

- [Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2026x](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=modeling-tools-documentation&version=2026x&variant=default)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="(2026x) Measurements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Measurements >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="a7b460de-2cde-4721-bf26-a64528f353c1"><span>The Implementation Matrix was updated by adding two new pairs: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Function to Actual Strategic Phase and<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Service Function to Operational Activity.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Implementation Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Implementation Matrix >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="c793ac3b-3518-4abb-b273-14bd2f2deed4"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The UAF template for the Cameo Collaborator was updated in accordance with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="UAF 1.3 grid" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Library Support</h3><p style="text-align: left;">New<span> </span><strong>DISR and UJTL library versions</strong><span> </span>are now available:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="cca70269-5997-4e77-b4cd-9cfc1e2b86da"><strong>UJTL (20250715)</strong></li><li data-uuid="191b57f8-3963-44c7-9517-78f9d5cf63c6"><strong>DISR (20240606)</strong></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">You can choose the library version when creating a new project.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="(2026x) Using libraries" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about using libraries >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">UAF/UPDM Compliance Mode for BPMN2 projects</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">“UPDM Profile for BPMN”</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>is no longer available in this version. All of its stereotypes have been moved to the existing </span><strong style="text-align: left;">BPMN Customization Profile</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. In addition, a new<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">UAF/UPDM compliance mode</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>has been introduced for BPMN projects, allowing you to choose whether the compliant UAF/UPDM stereotypes (depending on the plugin) should be applied to BPMN elements.</span></p><h3>Validation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="6008a243-322c-497a-a8eb-cf6a010d4e47"><span>The new correctness validation rules <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">dedicated to checking if the client and supplier are correct </span></span><span>have been introduced for the following relationships:</span><ul><li><strong>Opposes, Defines</strong>. New relationships, which were implemented with the UAF 1.3 Profile support.</li><li>Existing relationships: Phases, Compares To, Governed By, Maps To Goal, Owns Value, Creates, Enables, Evoked By, Presented By, Sequence</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="49b7b4f6-0458-4d29-bf38-c8d89d02c74a">The new correctness validation rules were implemented to verify whether the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">classifiers are correct for the following elements:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Actual Mission Scenario</strong>. The classifier must be Mission Scenario or its specialization.</span></li><li><strong>Actual Mission Vignette</strong>. The classifier must be Mission Vignette or its specialization.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="396d649c-2f0f-4ecd-bec6-dddadd29e35e">The new correctness validation rule was implemented to verify whether the behavior is correct for the<span> </span><strong>Mission Task Action</strong>. The behavior must be a Mission Task or its specialization.</li></ul><h3>Discontinued Products</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span><strong>AutoStyler plugin</strong><span> </span>is deprecated, with the intention of removing it with the next major version release, the 2028x version.</span></p><h3>Other Enhancements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="0d03fc7c-0658-457c-a7aa-cf76c6b50b73">A new backwards compatibility feature allows<span> </span><strong>UPDM 2.1</strong><span> </span>projects created in the<span> </span><strong>2026x</strong><span> </span>version to be <strong><em>Saved As Older Version</em><span> </span>in 2024x</strong><span> </span>compatible file format. This makes them directly compatible with the UPDM 2.1 plugin in 2024x, allowing you to open and work with these projects immediately in the 2024x version. This approach does not apply to the UAF 1.3 projects. <a style="text-align: left;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=saving-projects-to-earlier-versions&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default" rel="nofollow">Learn more &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="b84131a0-8ddc-4143-9e41-74038fde32b9">The<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UAF Enterprise Architecture Framework is now renamed to<span> </span><strong>EA-BPMN</strong>. The perspective and project creation template was already renamed in the 2024x version.</span></li><li data-uuid="90362e55-d693-48ea-8e0f-543e28d5560b"><span class="ghx-inner">The «ActualDODAFProperties» stereotype </span><span>will no longer be applied automatically after creating instances for «DODAFProperties». Moreover, the «</span><span>DODAFProperties» stereotype is renamed to <strong>«UAFProperties»</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="7cdb8a22-28f7-44f7-8119-9063b13f2823">In this version, the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table was renamed to the<span> </span><strong>Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table</strong>.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="5908b21c-854b-4227-9f89-c636631737fd">A new<span> </span><strong>UAF BoK Sample</strong><span> </span>is now available. This sample is based on the<span> </span><strong>UAF WOW! book</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="0bd74457-ae67-4938-bfc6-eaf7f743a9b0">This version includes new functionality that lets you select whether the Definition or Comment will<span> </span><strong>serve as the Documentation</strong><span> </span>for UAF elements.</li></ul><h2>Modeling and Infrastructure<span> </span></h2><ul><li data-uuid="ff92e60e-f346-49b2-a994-4740698946d3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Path variables can now be used in the Excel/CSV Sync Options and Import dialogs. This feature provides greater sharing flexibility by allowing you to store file paths as named variables in your project, which can be easily adjusted on different machines, making it easier to collaborate across teams and environments. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Learn more <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Excel-and-CSV-Import-dialog&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">here &gt;&gt;</a> and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Sync-with-Excel-or-CSV-files&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">here &gt;&gt;</a> </span></li><li data-uuid="9adbc59f-3d2e-4c20-813f-36fc56f01654"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>Deprecated XML Schema, CORBA IDL, C++ and Java CE engineering sets. You can use the <a href="https://help.3ds.com/2026/English/DSCATIAMagicDoc/CatEspMagicUserMap/catesp-c-ov.htm">CATIA Software Producer</a> plugin for code generation from Java and C++. </span></span></li><li data-uuid="7fb374e8-2e9d-47b4-bc91-5c7c6dda9acb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>Oracle DB and PosgreSQL drivers are no longer distributed with the modeling tool. Download and add the needed drivers according to the database type and version. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Code-Engineering-Sets&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li><li data-uuid="10aa5ae6-ca47-47d1-b9ed-76f015a910f2"><span>A new modeling tool license category, Non-Profit Research, has been introduced for Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Magic Cyber Systems Engineer modeling tools, as well as <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer, Magic Model Analyst, Magic Systems EE Architect, and Magic Systems Safety Engineer plugins. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=Modeling-tools-and-plugins-licensing&amp;version=2026x">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></span></li><li data-uuid="f06aec68-77d7-4172-9ad9-850d9ff703fb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Academic licenses are now available for Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer, Magic Systems EE Architect, and Magic Systems Safety Engineer plugins.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h2><span>Collaboration</span></h2><h3><span>Teamwork Cloud</span></h3><h4><span>The Project History Panel</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;">In version 2026x, Teamwork Cloud introduces the <strong>Project History</strong> panel, which can be opened with just a few clicks. It saves time when reviewing commit messages or investigating the branch structure.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_history.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Project History panel.</h6><h3><strong>3D</strong><span>EXPERIENCE Platform</span></h3><h4><span>Collaborative Spaces for Standard Profiles</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;">You can now create Collaborative Spaces specifically for standard profiles and libraries. This ensures profiles and libraries are always stored together in an accessible, shared space. It also prevents users from accidentally adding profiles to the spaces that others cannot access—for example, when creating a project, adding a project to the server, or migrating profiles.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><h2 style="text-align: left;"><span>Documentation</span></h2><ul><li data-uuid="e8a41602-e0d7-48a2-b2c7-e15b534dea12"><span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=modeling-tools-documentation&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture 2026x</a></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747832 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00243: (2026x) News for Developers

- page_id: `272747832`
- space_key: `VN`
