- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-28T10:42:05.071+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Modeling tools

##### GeneralchangesGeneral OpenAPI Changes

**Removed constants:**

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyID#SHOW_SMART_MANIPULATORS
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml2.datatypes.OSDecimalFormatSymbolsGetter#INFINITY
- com.nomagic.uml2.UML2Constants#STANDARD_PROFILE_L2
- com.nomagic.uml2.UML2Constants#STANDARD_PROFILE_L3

**Removed constructors:**

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService.UsedViaReadOnlyAutomaticUsageException#UsedViaReadOnlyAutomaticUsageException( com.nomagic.ci .persistence.spi.decomposition.AttachmentSet)

**Removed methods:**

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.modules.ModulesService.UsedViaReadOnlyAutomaticUsageException#getOffendingUsages
- com.nomagic.uml2.finder.AbstractByQualifiedNameFinder#isMatchingElement
- com.nomagic.uml2.finder.AbstractByQualifiedNameFinder#isFinalPartOfQualifiedName
- com.nomagic.uml2.finder.AbstractByQualifiedNameFinder#splitQualifiedName
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.AbstractRepository#isRawMode - always returned false
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.AbstractRepository#setRawMode - empty implementation
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.MDElement#accept

**Changes:**

The changes consist of classes being moved to other packages and renamed.

| Old API | New API |
| --- | --- |
| com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.AbstractRepository#nextVersion | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.reflect.AbstractRepository#nextBasicVersion |
| com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartEventSupport#registerConfig | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartEventSupport#register |
| com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartEventSupport#unregisterConfig | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.smartlistener.SmartEventSupport#unregister |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.utils.PriorityProvider | com.nomagic.utils.PriorityProvider |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.OptionsGroup#getIcon | com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.OptionsGroup#getGroupIcon |
| com.nomagic.ui.ResizableIconAdapter | com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.common.ui.ResizableIconAdapter |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.MDElement#getProject | com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project#getProject(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement) |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.MDElement#getProjectImpl | com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project#getProject(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement) |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.MDElement | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.core.MDElement |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ModelElementProvider | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.core.ModelElementProvider |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.ModelElementsProvider | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.core.ModelElementsProvider |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.AbstractDiagramType | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.AbstractDiagramType |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.AbstractDiagramSurfacePainter | com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.diagram.AbstractDiagramSurfacePainter |

**Class**

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.CompartmentElement

was removed from OpenAPI and moved to another package

- com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.common.compartments.CompartmentElement

##### PresentationElementchangesPresentationElement OpenAPI Changes

The changes consist of Property related calls being moved to PresentationElementsManager, and some method name changes.

| Old API | New API |
| --- | --- |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPreferredSize | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPreferredDimension |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getProperty | com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager#getProperty |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#addProperty | com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager#addProperty |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPropertyManager | com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager#getPropertyManager |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPreferredSize | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPreferredDimension |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getDiagramPresentationElement | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement#get |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#useParentProperties | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#useParentStyle |

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Visitor was refactored by moving symbols specific methods to

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.PresentationElementVisitor and
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.BasePresentationElementVisitor

com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.InheritanceVisitor was refactored by moving symbols specific methods to

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.BasePresentationElementInheritanceVisitor and
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.PresentationElementInheritanceVisitor

##### ProjectMergeComparechangesProject Merge/Compare OpenAPI Changes

com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.CompareUtil#getDifference API:

- CompareUtil#getDifference(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor, com.nomagic.utils.ErrorHandler<java.lang.Exception>, com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Optimization, java.util.Set<com.nomagic.magicdraw.diff.ModuleInfo>)

was changed with:

- CompareUtil#getDifference(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor, com.nomagic.utils.ErrorHandler<java.lang.Exception>, com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Optimization)

**Removed:**

com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.ProjectDifference.getRejectedModules() method removed that incorrectly returned empty collection.

com.nomagic.magicdraw.diff.RwMergeModeConstraint class removed.

##### RemoteProjectchangesRemote project-related OpenAPI Changes

**Changes:**

Class com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils changed to Interface. Most methods are no longer static and work with a specific TWC server connection. To get an instance, use com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils#get(), com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils#get([com.nomagic.ci](http://com.nomagic.ci).persistence.IPrimaryProject)

| Old API | New API |
| --- | --- |
| EsiUtils.getCommandLinePasswordManager(...) | EsiUtils.getPasswordManager(...) |
| EsiUtils.getCategoryID(...) | EsiUtils.getCategory(...) |
| EsiUtils#getCategory(String) | EsiUtils#getCategoryInfoByResourceID(String) |
| EsiUtils.getNewBranch(...) | EsiUtils.getNewEsiBranch(...) |
| EsiUtils#setNotifyOnNewTags(com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.project.project.EsiPrimaryProject, com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject, java.util.Collection<String>) | EsiUtils#setNotifyOnNewTags(com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IPrimaryProject, com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject, java.util.Collection<String>) |
| EsiUtils#cloneProject(org.eclipse.emf.common.util.URI, String,String,String) | EsiUtils#cloneProject(org.eclipse.emf.common.util.URI, String, String, java.util.UUID) |
| ITeamworkService.isLiveConnection() | ITeamworkService.isConnected() |
| TeamworkService.getInstance(java.lang.String) | TeamworkService.getInstance() |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.teamwork2.esi.protect.CommandLinePasswordManager | com.nomagic.magicdraw.teamwork2.esi.protect.PasswordManager |

**Removed:**

ITeamworkService.setAsLastUsedService() method removed due to empty implementation

ITeamworkService setActiveInstance(ITeamworkService) method removed. The method was dangerous.

##### DependencyMatrixchangesDependency Matrix OpenAPI Changes

DependencyEditor(com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.editing.DependencyEditor) methods createAddActions, createEditActions, canCreate, canEdit now require ElementNode(com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.ElementNode) type parameters instead of Element (com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element).

| Old API | New API |
| --- | --- |
| DependencyEditor#createAddActions(PersistenceManager, Element, Element, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory,ActionsCategory) | DependencyEditor#createAddActions(PersistenceManager, ElementNode, ElementNode, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory) |
| DependencyEditor#createEditActions(PersistenceManager, Element, Element, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory) | DependencyEditor#createEditActions(PersistenceManager, ElementNode,ElementNode, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory) |
| DependencyEditor#canCreate(PersistenceManager, Element, Element, AbstractMatrixCell) | DependencyEditor#canCreate(PersistenceManager, ElementNode, ElementNode, AbstractMatrixCell) |
| DependencyEditor#canEdit(PersistenceManager, Element, Element, AbstractMatrixCell) | DependencyEditor#canEdit(PersistenceManager, ElementNode, ElementNode, AbstractMatrixCell) |

##### DiagramLayoutchangesDiagram Layout OpenAPI Changes

Following the deprecation of layout related code in 2024xRefresh3, the OpenAPI is removed in this version.

- The coupling between a diagram and layouters has been removed.
- All of the following changes have been replaced with a separate class for layouting - com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout.Layouting (see Layouting symbols in the diagram ).
- Layout related methods removed from AbstractDiagramPresentationElement instead use Layouting class

All concrete layouters are no longer OpenAPI, constructors related to Environment Options removed.

This includes layouters:

- DiagramLayouter
- AbstractDiagramLayouter
- AbstractHierarchicDiagramLayouter
- AbstractLinkDiagramLayouter
- CircularDiagramLayouter
- GridDiagramLayouter
- OrganicDiagramLayouter
- OrthogonalDiagramLayouter
- TreeDiagramLayouter
- HierarchicDiagramLayouter
- ActivityDiagramLayouter
- BusinessProcessDiagramLayouter
- CompositeStructureDiagramLayouter
- ClassDiagramLayouter
- OrderedHierarchicDiagramLayouter
- StateDiagramLayouter
- OrganicLinkDiagramLayouter
- OrthogonalLinkDiagramLayouter
- LayoutManager

They are also moved from com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout package to com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.diagram.ylayout

Graph classes no longer OpenAPI:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout.BaseGraph
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout.UMLGraph

#### Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit

##### API Changes

**Renamed packages:**

| Old Package | New Package |
| --- | --- |
| fUML.Semantics.Activities.IntermediateActivities | com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.activities.intermediate |
| fUML.Semantics.Classes.Kernel | com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.classes |
| fUML.Semantics.CommonBehaviors.BasicBehaviors | com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.behaviors |
| fUML.Semantics.CommonBehaviors.Communications | com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.behaviors.communications |
| fUML.Semantics.Loci.LociL1 | com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.loci |

Renamed tag due to removal of Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit Plugin from:

com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.SimulationProfile.SimulationConfigStereotype#isStartWebServer → com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.SimulationProfile.SimulationConfigStereotype#isShowUIAsHTML

**Simulation options API changes:**

| Old API | New API |
| --- | --- |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationExecution#getSimulationOptions | com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationExecution#getOptions |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#getOption | com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#get |
| com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#addOption | com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#set |

**Renamed** option due to removal of Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit Plugin

com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#START_WEB_SERVER → com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#SHOW_UI_AS_HTML

**Removed:**

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#ADD_CONTROL_PANEL
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.SimulationProfile.SimulationConfigStereotype#isAddControlPanel

In addition to the method renaming, the package of the Object_ class has changed from:

- fUML.Semantics.Classes.Kernel.Object_#getExecution

to

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.classes.Object_#getExecutions

Class com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper methods removed:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper#getFeatureValueByName(StructuredValue, String, Locus) - Use shorter overloaded version without the Locus parameter
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper#getFeatureValue(StructuredValue, StructuralFeature, Locus) - Use shorter overloaded version without the Locus parameter
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper#getFeatureValue - Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.classes.StructuredValue#getFeatureValue directly
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper#getObjectFromValueList(ValueList, Type, int) - Use shorter overloaded version without the Type parameter

Downgraded com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.engine.AbstractExecutionEngineDescriptor to InternalAPI.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h2>Modeling tools</h2><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3524d8e6-239f-41c4-81a4-d337faa0076d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Generalchanges</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>General OpenAPI Changes</h3><p><strong>Removed constants:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="269f9269-3ffd-4abf-8c27-ef0bb090b046">com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyID#SHOW_SMART_MANIPULATORS</li><li data-uuid="9735c0ca-a2b8-4b94-9598-b87317b835dc">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml2.datatypes.OSDecimalFormatSymbolsGetter#INFINITY</li><li data-uuid="09c9b8e3-400d-4fff-8632-fbbd52382a40">com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.common.compartments.CompartmentElement</li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" Changes</h3><p>The changes consist of Property related calls being moved to PresentationElementsManager, and some method name changes.</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Old API</th><th>New API</th></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPreferredSize</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPreferredDimension</td></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getProperty</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager#getProperty</td></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPreferredSize</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getPreferredDimension</td></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#getDiagramPresentationElement</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement#get</td></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#useParentProperties</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement#useParentStyle</td></tr></tbody></table><p>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.Visitor was refactored by moving symbols specific methods  to</p><ul><li data-uuid="a012cb07-27c9-450b-b5fc-bb4c0e6776d1">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.PresentationElementVisitor and</li><li data-uuid="e4d605f4-4602-4d9b-ab96-f10c3b70ba62">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.BasePresentationElementVisitor</li></ul><p>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.InheritanceVisitor was refactored by moving symbols specific methods  to</p><ul><li data-uuid="aec3d6fa-9be9-4222-b63b-1b6b0acf0b12">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.BasePresentationElementInheritanceVisitor and</li><li data-uuid="378d6d00-3c8b-45a9-a57c-6e244596fa58">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.PresentationElementInheritanceVisitor</li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f718ce42-eb85-4de0-9293-a42a19bf1758"><ac:parameter ac:name="">ProjectMergeComparechanges</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Project Merge/Compare OpenAPI Changes</h3><p>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.CompareUtil#getDifference API:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e7b5b49c-dc21-4c9a-b90f-75d950658591">CompareUtil#getDifference(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor, com.nomagic.utils.ErrorHandler&lt;java.lang.Exception&gt;, com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Optimization, java.util.Set&lt;com.nomagic.magicdraw.diff.ModuleInfo&gt;)</li></ul><p>was changed with:</p><ul><li data-uuid="049c1a4d-2b70-421f-91f6-3b288eaa1f5e">CompareUtil#getDifference(com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor, com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.project.ProjectDescriptor, com.nomagic.utils.ErrorHandler&lt;java.lang.Exception&gt;, com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.Optimization)</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong>Removed:</strong></p><p>com.nomagic.magicdraw.merge.ProjectDifference.getRejectedModules() method removed that incorrectly returned empty collection.</p><p>com.nomagic.magicdraw.diff.RwMergeModeConstraint class removed.</p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0e43027-23c4-4ef4-b953-63368bd46c69"><ac:parameter ac:name="">RemoteProjectchanges</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Remote project-related OpenAPI Changes</h3><p><strong>Changes:</strong></p><p>Class com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils changed to Interface. Most methods are no longer static and work with a specific TWC server connection. To get an instance, use com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils#get(), com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.EsiUtils#get(<a href="http://com.nomagic.ci">com.nomagic.ci</a>.persistence.IPrimaryProject)</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 60.9825%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 40.8394%;" /><col class="" style="width: 59.1606%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Old API</th><th>New API</th></tr><tr class=""><td>EsiUtils.getCommandLinePasswordManager(...)</td><td>EsiUtils.getPasswordManager(...)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>EsiUtils.getCategoryID(...)</td><td>EsiUtils.getCategory(...)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>EsiUtils#getCategory(String)</td><td>EsiUtils#getCategoryInfoByResourceID(String)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>EsiUtils.getNewBranch(...)</td><td>EsiUtils.getNewEsiBranch(...)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>EsiUtils#setNotifyOnNewTags(com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.project.project.EsiPrimaryProject, <a href="http://com.nomagic.ci">com.nomagic.ci</a>.persistence.IAttachedProject, java.util.Collection&lt;String&gt;)</td><td>EsiUtils#setNotifyOnNewTags(<a href="http://com.nomagic.ci">com.nomagic.ci</a>.persistence.IPrimaryProject, <a href="http://com.nomagic.ci">com.nomagic.ci</a>.persistence.IAttachedProject, java.util.Collection&lt;String&gt;)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>EsiUtils#cloneProject(org.eclipse.emf.common.util.URI, String,String,String)</td><td>EsiUtils#cloneProject(org.eclipse.emf.common.util.URI, String, String, java.util.UUID)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>ITeamworkService.isLiveConnection()</td><td>ITeamworkService.isConnected()</td></tr><tr class=""><td>TeamworkService.getInstance(java.lang.String) </td><td>TeamworkService.getInstance()</td></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.teamwork2.esi.protect.CommandLinePasswordManager</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.teamwork2.esi.protect.PasswordManager</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Removed:</strong></p><p>ITeamworkService.setAsLastUsedService() method removed due to empty implementation</p><p>ITeamworkService setActiveInstance(ITeamworkService) method removed. The method was dangerous.</p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ad2026b-ff52-4de8-bde6-7694872778d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">DependencyMatrixchanges</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Dependency Matrix OpenAPI Changes</h3><p>DependencyEditor(com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.editing.DependencyEditor) methods createAddActions, createEditActions, canCreate, canEdit now require ElementNode(com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.ElementNode) type parameters instead of Element (com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element).</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Old API</th><th>New API</th></tr><tr class=""><td>DependencyEditor#createAddActions(PersistenceManager, Element, Element, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory,ActionsCategory)</td><td>DependencyEditor#createAddActions(PersistenceManager, ElementNode, ElementNode, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>DependencyEditor#createEditActions(PersistenceManager, Element, Element, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory)</td><td>DependencyEditor#createEditActions(PersistenceManager, ElementNode,ElementNode, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>DependencyEditor#canCreate(PersistenceManager, Element, Element, AbstractMatrixCell)</td><td>DependencyEditor#canCreate(PersistenceManager, ElementNode, ElementNode, AbstractMatrixCell)</td></tr><tr class=""><td>DependencyEditor#canEdit(PersistenceManager, Element, Element, AbstractMatrixCell)</td><td>DependencyEditor#canEdit(PersistenceManager, ElementNode, ElementNode, AbstractMatrixCell)</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df78fee1-14c2-4672-8f20-9bd52eadc63c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">DiagramLayoutchanges</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Diagram Layout OpenAPI Changes</h3><p>Following the deprecation of layout related code in 2024xRefresh3, the OpenAPI is removed in this version.</p><ul><li data-uuid="19790629-0cdc-4d5d-8873-17995ce5f05f">The coupling between a diagram and layouters has been removed.</li><li data-uuid="4cb95438-32a8-42cd-ae12-fea1881c50a5">All of the following changes have been replaced with a separate class for layouting - com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout.Layouting (see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/DEVG2024xR3/pages/225347324/Layouting+symbols+in+the+diagram">Layouting symbols in the diagram</a>).</li><li data-uuid="c3612561-a873-45b9-b6b6-9f03f03d72c4">Layout related methods removed from AbstractDiagramPresentationElement instead use Layouting class</li></ul><p>All concrete layouters are no longer OpenAPI, constructors related to Environment Options removed.</p><p>This includes layouters:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8cc6e612-e858-4b5d-bed3-50945ce6335d">DiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="e40ccd64-5a8b-4a19-a57b-2936b322afe3">AbstractDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="e98417e5-ce06-4a58-9d7c-5db5be076998">AbstractHierarchicDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="b6b2559a-c6c2-4898-9210-11e7561dd855">AbstractLinkDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="dade7576-f4dc-4b89-a68a-3b473e408aa8">CircularDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="53649bbd-7c77-48f2-b5bd-218fb1365ddb">GridDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="1c86f97b-51c9-4282-bd7d-9d85ae4bf7f2">OrganicDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="7304828e-ce8a-431b-bb08-fb509a60660a">OrthogonalDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="8b0c2bf0-da76-4368-97aa-856307bc5a4b">TreeDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="13a8bdc6-c659-47b2-b079-b5650b3e277a">HierarchicDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="863d7fed-180f-48fe-b634-8642c33e6f9a">ActivityDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="ab79733a-5868-43a5-8e34-9ea9a6f0e15c">BusinessProcessDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="c7436bed-90d7-4f8f-a03b-cf2fc20581a2">CompositeStructureDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="7c0f253a-f4e7-4cca-b269-b15d8887aca4">ClassDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="e2855b9d-e5b5-48d9-bdc1-5ccbec8a51c1">OrderedHierarchicDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="4fe60f4b-6a8a-4069-9305-59f7bd7aa16d">StateDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="7c390ae7-9ef0-4758-ac03-788160d63e4b">OrganicLinkDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="b4f2dff6-e219-4209-b146-d6b0ee186afb">OrthogonalLinkDiagramLayouter</li><li data-uuid="83f5b7c6-598a-4abc-bcae-2b113477f9fd">LayoutManager</li></ul><p>They are also moved from com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout package to com.dassault_systemes.modeler.foundation.diagram.ylayout</p><p>Graph classes no longer OpenAPI:</p><ul><li data-uuid="1025a9ba-f183-4120-86bd-76e09755f940">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout.BaseGraph</li><li data-uuid="8dbbcb06-b28d-4a83-815c-aad2902e4481">com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.layout.UMLGraph</li></ul><h2>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit</h2><h3>API Changes</h3><p><strong>Renamed packages:</strong></p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Old Package</th><th>New Package</th></tr><tr class=""><td>fUML.Semantics.Activities.IntermediateActivities</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.activities.intermediate</td></tr><tr class=""><td>fUML.Semantics.Classes.Kernel</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.classes</td></tr><tr class=""><td>fUML.Semantics.CommonBehaviors.BasicBehaviors</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.behaviors</td></tr><tr class=""><td>fUML.Semantics.CommonBehaviors.Communications</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.behaviors.communications</td></tr><tr class=""><td>fUML.Semantics.Loci.LociL1</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.loci</td></tr></tbody></table><p>Renamed tag due to removal of Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit Plugin from:</p><p>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.SimulationProfile.SimulationConfigStereotype#isStartWebServer → com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.SimulationProfile.SimulationConfigStereotype#isShowUIAsHTML</p><p><strong>Simulation options API changes:</strong></p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Old API</th><th>New API</th></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationExecution#getSimulationOptions</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationExecution#getOptions</td></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#getOption</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#get</td></tr><tr class=""><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#addOption</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#set</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Renamed</strong> option due to removal of Web Server for Cameo Simulation Toolkit Plugin</p><p>com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#START_WEB_SERVER → com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#SHOW_UI_AS_HTML</p><p><strong>Removed:</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="0700318d-c9b6-4a02-b979-cd3933a89ab6">com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.execution.SimulationOptions#ADD_CONTROL_PANEL</li><li data-uuid="e01121a7-b8e5-4d1f-ad19-e583e147b4b2">com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.SimulationProfile.SimulationConfigStereotype#isAddControlPanel</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>In addition to the method renaming, the package of the Object_ class has changed from:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d46a4f18-7767-45ba-852d-c78d67fa281d">fUML.Semantics.Classes.Kernel.Object_#getExecution</li></ul><p>to</p><ul><li data-uuid="8c4e4c79-69f6-462b-9b71-d30fecbe9b4f">com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.classes.Object_#getExecutions</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>Class com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper methods removed:</p><ul><li data-uuid="fba2b04c-9762-4770-82d3-a8e4e1cce8b0">com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper#getFeatureValueByName(StructuredValue, String, Locus) - Use shorter overloaded version without the Locus parameter</li><li data-uuid="75d34fa3-98e8-4334-826f-e7525df21007">com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper#getFeatureValue(StructuredValue, StructuralFeature, Locus) - Use shorter overloaded version without the Locus parameter</li><li data-uuid="a8c4e0fa-a057-4aaf-8183-19fc3ee6ba5f">com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper#getFeatureValue - Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.classes.StructuredValue#getFeatureValue directly</li><li data-uuid="7dfc4dda-ac45-4979-bbf8-0334c614e782">com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.fuml.fUMLHelper#getObjectFromValueList(ValueList, Type, int) - Use shorter overloaded version without the Type parameter</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>Downgraded com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation.engine.AbstractExecutionEngineDescriptor to InternalAPI.</p>
````

It does not contain new capabilities.</p><p style="text-align: left;">Check the product compatibility <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="PC" ri:content-title="Product Compatibility" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="8e3d9b27-8aab-4140-b4f9-0a5144872abf"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=sysml-v1-modeling&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">SysML v1 Plugin 2026x</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747821 space=VN version=1 -->
## PAGE 00246: (2026x) Systems Cybersecurity Designer

- page_id: `272747821`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747821/2026x+Systems+Cybersecurity+Designer
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-28T10:42:04.097+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

In this release, a new Cybersecurity Template: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DO-326A Preliminary Security Risk Assessment' space='SCDTWRT']has been introduced, which is based on the [DO-326A](https://products.rtca.org/21dja1f/)standard. TheDO-326Astandard, titled*Airworthiness Security Process Specification*, provides guidance for the implementation of an airworthiness cybersecurity process.

##### DO-326A Preliminary Security Risk Assessment

Cybersecurity engineering is a critical discipline that ensures the safe and secure operation of connected and complex airborne systems. The increasing integration of digital and networked technologies in aviation demands a robust, lifecycle-oriented approach to manage cybersecurity risks. This update implements the[DO-326A](https://products.rtca.org/21dja1f/)methodology within the Systems Cybersecurity Designer to enable compliance assessments during the development of avionics systems.

DO-326A, published by RTCA and EUROCAE, defines the process framework for airworthiness security — that is, ensuring that cybersecurity threats do not compromise the safety and continued airworthiness of an aircraft. Its main principles include:

- A risk-based approach to managing cybersecurity threats to aircraft systems.
- Lifecycle integration, requiring threat and mitigation analysis from system concept through decommissioning.

The Systems Cybersecurity Designer now enables users to:

- Model avionics systems for cybersecurity risk assessment , incorporating assets, vulnerabilities, threat scenarios, attack paths, and mitigations in line with DO-326A guidance.
- Perform a Security Risk Assessment (SRA) and Aircraft System Security Assessment (ASSA) using structured, iterative methodologies.
- Link safety and cybersecurity analyses , helping teams ensure consistency between security measures and system safety objectives.
- Produce certification-ready artifacts that demonstrate compliance with DO-326A, and support alignment with DO-355 (Information Security Guidance for Continuing Airworthiness) and DO-356A (Security Methods and Considerations).

By embedding DO-326A methodology into the design workflow, this update empowers aerospace engineers to reduce certification risk, identify security issues early in development, and streamline the path toward regulatory approval.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### DO-326A Index.

##### Documentation

- Systems Cybersecurity Designer 2026x

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181954 space=VN version=2 -->
## PAGE 00247: (2026x) Systems Safety Engineer

- page_id: `314181954`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314181954/2026x+Systems+Safety+Engineer
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-06-26T13:11:15.854+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

This release introduces a new plugin:Systems Safety Engineer. The plugin enables safety and quality assessments on systems architecture, supporting SAE J1739:2021 and AIAG VDA concepts.

##### Systems Safety Engineer

Safety and quality engineering are critical disciplines that ensure the development of safe, reliable, and resilient systems. Failure Mode Avoidance aimed to identify failure modes at an early stage of systems development to implement robust countermeasures

Based on the system architecture and following a Failure Mode Avoidance method, the Systems Safety Engineer plugin enables:

- The creation of the Boundary Diagram and Item to frame the scope: the relevant parts of the system to be analyzed.
- The creation of an item-based Interface Analysis table to study connections between system elements
- The creation of a Parameter Diagram to identify the measurable parameters that could affect the intended output of a given function.
- The creation of a Robustness Checklist to identify causality links between elements for a given function.
- The creation of a Failure Mode and Effects Analysis table

Systems Safety Engineer is **RAAML-based** and partially supports **SAE J1739.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Failure Mode Avoidance Index.

##### Documentation

- Systems Safety Engineer 2026x

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272747780 space=VN version=36 -->
## PAGE 00248: (2026x) UAF 1.3 Plugin

- page_id: `272747780`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272747780/2026x+UAF+1.3+Plugin
- version_number: 36
- version_date: `2026-01-23T14:37:20.260+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Version News > (2026x) 2026x Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

In the**2026x** release, we implemented the **UAF 1.3 standard**, which includes key features for Mission Engineering Modeling. The **UAF 1.3 Profile** is fully supported in UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks. Therefore, UAF 1.2 is no longer supported in this version. Moreover, the New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space='IL'].For further information, check the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling' space='MT'].

##### Methods and Standards

Beginning with this version, the **UAF 1.3 Plugin** is available for production use. The UAF 1.3 Profile is fully supported in UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**IMPORTANT!**The UAF 1.2 Plugin is no longer supported starting with the 2026x version.

Projects created in earlier versions **require**the following migration before you can continue working with them:

- UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### UAF 1.3 Profile Support

600400

Together with the**UAF 1.3 Profile**, the following changes areimplementedto enable**Mission Engineering modeling in the 2026x version**:

- New elements, properties, and relationships were added. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. New elements' space='MT']>>]]>
- The measurements library was updated. New Force Designation enumeration added.
- New diagram in Strategic Processes:
  - Mission Phases diagram
- New diagrams in Operational Processes:
  - [Mission Threads diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=working-with-mission-threads-diagram&version=2026x&variant=default)
  - [Mission Threads Flow diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=working-with-mission-threads-flow-diagram&version=2026x&variant=default)
- New diagram in Resources Processes:
  - [Mission Engineering Threads diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=working-with-mission-engineering-threads-diagram&version=2026x&variant=default)
- New diagram in Parameters:
  - Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram
- The measurement tables were updated to align with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes. Now you can add Activities and their specializations directly to the measurement tables, because Activities are treated as Property Sets and can own measurements. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Measurements' space='MT']>>]]>
- The Implementation Matrix was updated by adding two new pairs: Function to Actual Strategic Phase and Service Function to Operational Activity.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Implementation Matrix' space='MT']>>]]>
- The UAF template for the Cameo Collaborator was updated in accordance with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Library Support

New**DISR and UJTL library versions**are now available:

- UJTL (20250715)
- DISR (20240606)

You can choose the library version when creating a new project.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using libraries' space='MT']>>]]>

##### UAF/UPDM Compliance Mode for BPMN2 projects

The**“UPDM Profile for BPMN”**is no longer available in this version. All of its stereotypes have been moved to the existing**BPMN Customization Profile**. In addition, a new**UAF/UPDM compliance mode**has been introduced for BPMN projects, allowing you to choose whether the compliant UAF/UPDM stereotypes (depending on the plugin) should be applied to BPMN elements.

##### Validation

- The new correctness validation rules dedicated to checking if the client and supplier are correct have been introduced for the following relationships:
  - Opposes, Defines . New relationships, which were implemented with the UAF 1.3 Profile support.
  - Existing relationships: Phases, Compares To, Governed By, Maps To Goal, Owns Value, Creates, Enables, Evoked By, Presented By, Sequence
- The new correctness validation rules were implemented to verify whether the classifiers are correct for the following elements:
  - **Actual Mission Scenario**. The classifier must be Mission Scenario or its specialization.
  - Actual Mission Vignette . The classifier must be Mission Vignette or its specialization.
- The new correctness validation rule was implemented to verify whether the behavior is correct for the Mission Task Action . The behavior must be a Mission Task or its specialization.

##### Discontinued Products

The**AutoStyler plugin**is deprecated, with the intention of removing it with the next major version release, the 2028x version.

##### Other Enhancements

- A new backwards compatibility feature allows UPDM 2.1 projects created in the 2026x version to be *Saved As Older Version*in 2024x compatible file format. This makes them directly compatible with the UPDM 2.1 plugin in 2024x, allowing you to open and work with these projects immediately in the 2024x version. This approach does not apply to the UAF 1.3 projects. Learn more >>>
- The UAF Enterprise Architecture Framework is now renamed to**EA-BPMN**. The perspective and project creation template was already renamed in the 2024x version.
- The «ActualDODAFProperties» stereotype will no longer be applied automatically after creating instances for «DODAFProperties». Moreover, the « DODAFProperties» stereotype is renamed to **«UAFProperties»**.
- In this version, the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table was renamed to the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table' space='MT']>>]]>
- A new UAF BoK Sample is now available. This sample is based on the UAF WOW! book .
- This version includes new functionality that lets you select whether the Definition or Comment will serve as the Documentation for UAF elements.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.3 modeling' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

New<strong><span> </span></strong>Force Designation enumeration added.</li><li data-uuid="c294f40b-f71f-4b1a-bfa7-7d4f01527972"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New diagram in Strategic Processes:</span><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=working-with-mission-phases-diagram&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Mission Phases diagram</a></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="29353c5c-188d-40d8-ab95-1f4b84726f9b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New diagrams in Operational Processes:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=working-with-mission-threads-diagram&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Mission Threads diagram</a></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=working-with-mission-threads-flow-diagram&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Mission Threads Flow diagram</a></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="d7084c48-f3b9-4d49-9108-a38f714d697e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New diagram in Resources Processes:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=working-with-mission-engineering-threads-diagram&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Mission Engineering Threads diagram</a></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="7917a40b-8146-4d32-aa8f-b9a6e04a9b67"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New diagram in Parameters:</span><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=working-with-mission-vignette-and-scenario-diagram&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram</a></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="565a50a5-6524-4a1f-8a20-81576d5bab11">The measurement tables were updated to align with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes. Now you can add Activities and their specializations directly to the measurement tables, because Activities are treated as Property Sets and can own measurements.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="(2026x) Measurements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Measurements >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2c4ce17d-cbfb-4855-800c-f7cd1822689c"><span>The Implementation Matrix was updated by adding two new pairs: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Function to Actual Strategic Phase and<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Service Function to Operational Activity.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Implementation Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Implementation Matrix >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="2b694e77-5548-4023-a6b1-7d0286e00653"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The UAF template for the Cameo Collaborator was updated in accordance with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="UAF 1.3 grid" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Library Support</h3><p style="text-align: left;">New<span> </span><strong>DISR and UJTL library versions</strong><span> </span>are now available:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="7776cdc9-7c83-45d0-b39d-ca209a2f7c71"><strong>UJTL (20250715)</strong></li><li data-uuid="1a6d5535-12d1-42c9-bf68-b7c5be0bad52"><strong>DISR (20240606)</strong></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">You can choose the library version when creating a new project.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="(2026x) Using libraries" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about using libraries >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">UAF/UPDM Compliance Mode for BPMN2 projects</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">“UPDM Profile for BPMN”</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>is no longer available in this version. All of its stereotypes have been moved to the existing </span><strong style="text-align: left;">BPMN Customization Profile</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. In addition, a new<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">UAF/UPDM compliance mode</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>has been introduced for BPMN projects, allowing you to choose whether the compliant UAF/UPDM stereotypes (depending on the plugin) should be applied to BPMN elements.</span></p><h3>Validation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="819fc7e6-4db9-4348-8a57-97ee9b5b9774"><span>The new correctness validation rules <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">dedicated to checking if the client and supplier are correct </span></span><span>have been introduced for the following relationships:</span><ul><li><strong>Opposes, Defines</strong>. New relationships, which were implemented with the UAF 1.3 Profile support.</li><li>Existing relationships: Phases, Compares To, Governed By, Maps To Goal, Owns Value, Creates, Enables, Evoked By, Presented By, Sequence</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="931c9f84-b136-48e5-ad3b-7893b2de0360">The new correctness validation rules were implemented to verify whether the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">classifiers are correct for the following elements:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Actual Mission Scenario</strong>. The classifier must be Mission Scenario or its specialization.</span></li><li><strong>Actual Mission Vignette</strong>. The classifier must be Mission Vignette or its specialization.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="20f38d51-f673-443d-b461-7b041d449259">The new correctness validation rule was implemented to verify whether the behavior is correct for the<span> </span><strong>Mission Task Action</strong>. The behavior must be a Mission Task or its specialization.</li></ul><h3>Discontinued Products</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span><strong>AutoStyler plugin</strong><span> </span>is deprecated, with the intention of removing it with the next major version release, the 2028x version.</span></p><h3>Other Enhancements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="38797f07-ce6f-4d70-a66b-cf4719b2b4c4">A new backwards compatibility feature allows<span> </span><strong>UPDM 2.1</strong><span> </span>projects created in the<span> </span><strong>2026x</strong><span> </span>version to be <strong><em>Saved As Older Version</em><span> </span>in 2024x</strong><span> </span>compatible file format. This makes them directly compatible with the UPDM 2.1 plugin in 2024x, allowing you to open and work with these projects immediately in the 2024x version. This approach does not apply to the UAF 1.3 projects. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=saving-projects-to-earlier-versions&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default">Learn more &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="834b48a2-8d11-40a3-8353-758bf5a24e41">The<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UAF Enterprise Architecture Framework is now renamed to<span> </span><strong>EA-BPMN</strong>. The perspective and project creation template was already renamed in the 2024x version.</span></li><li data-uuid="4bdfea5e-045a-4c15-a921-2614690337c2"><span class="ghx-inner">The «ActualDODAFProperties» stereotype </span><span>will no longer be applied automatically after creating instances for «DODAFProperties». Moreover, the «</span><span>DODAFProperties» stereotype is renamed to <strong>«UAFProperties»</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="e4ce0754-cdd3-4167-8c09-56b2be3d52b4">In this version, the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table was renamed to the<span> </span><strong>Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table</strong>.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d6093bf2-61ab-44a7-8851-dd21a76ff1e9">A new<span> </span><strong>UAF BoK Sample</strong><span> </span>is now available. This sample is based on the<span> </span><strong>UAF WOW! book</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="19d4a521-82de-4181-adab-6e0e5a3b86e7">This version includes new functionality that lets you select whether the Definition or Comment will<span> </span><strong>serve as the Documentation</strong><span> </span>for UAF elements.</li></ul><h3><span>Documentation</span></h3><ul><li data-uuid="9888ccbb-c590-4f96-bd68-f12c6662f3d8"><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="UAF 1.3 modeling" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF 1.3 Plugin 2026x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261621744 space=VN version=23 -->
## PAGE 00252: Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `261621744`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261621744/Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 23
- version_date: `2026-05-11T13:56:29.708+02:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: ['news-feature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

##### Expanded SysML v2 Support in DataHub

**Dependency relationships now supported in “Copy data”**

DataHub’s **Copy data** operation for SysML v2 projects now supports **Dependency** relationship types, improving preservation of model traceability and relationship structure during data transfer.

**SysML v2 graduates from Tech Preview**

The **Tech Preview** designation for SysML v2 projects has been removed from the DataHub plugin. SysML v2 support is now promoted to standard product functionality, reflecting improved maturity and broader production readiness.

##### New Connector Capabilities

**Jama Connect “Copy data” support for SysML v2**

The **Copy data** operation is now available for **Jama Connect** integrations in SysML v2 projects, enabling more complete requirement and artifact transfer workflows.

##### Improved TRM Connector Configuration

**Contextual commands relocated to DataHub Environment Options**

The following TRM connector contextual commands have been moved under the **DataHub** section of **Environment Options** for better discoverability and centralized configuration:

- Show All / Show Owned
- Show Revisions
- Show IP classification attribute

This update streamlines connector settings management and improves consistency across DataHub integrations.

##### Improved DOORS Next Connector Data Source Switching

The **DOORS Next (DNG) connector** has been enhanced to improve synchronization accuracy when using the **Copy DHLink from other data source** command.

When this command is applied, DataHub now correctly updates synchronized items to reference the appropriate **stream or baseline data source** after switching to another data source. OSLC links on synchronized items are automatically redirected to the correct target data source, ensuring that links remain aligned with the selected stream or baseline context.

**Benefits**

- More accurate stream and baseline synchronization
- Correct OSLC link targeting after data source switching
- Reduced risk of broken or misdirected synchronization references
- Improved consistency across multiple DNG configurations

This enhancement significantly improves reliability for configuration-specific workflows and change management processes involving multiple DOORS Next data sources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: June 26, 2026</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Expanded SysML v2 Support in DataHub</h3><p><strong>Dependency relationships now supported in “Copy data”</strong></p><p>DataHub’s <strong>Copy data</strong> operation for SysML v2 projects now supports <strong>Dependency</strong> relationship types, improving preservation of model traceability and relationship structure during data transfer.</p><p><strong>SysML v2 graduates from Tech Preview</strong></p><p>The <strong>Tech Preview</strong> designation for SysML v2 projects has been removed from the DataHub plugin. SysML v2 support is now promoted to standard product functionality, reflecting improved maturity and broader production readiness.</p><h3>New Connector Capabilities</h3><p><strong>Jama Connect “Copy data” support for SysML v2</strong></p><p>The <strong>Copy data</strong> operation is now available for <strong>Jama Connect</strong> integrations in SysML v2 projects, enabling more complete requirement and artifact transfer workflows.</p><h3>Improved TRM Connector Configuration</h3><p><strong>Contextual commands relocated to DataHub Environment Options</strong></p><p>The following TRM connector contextual commands have been moved under the <strong>DataHub</strong> section of <strong>Environment Options</strong> for better discoverability and centralized configuration:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3d647b98-c5f1-4e32-ac94-1053d43c505b">Show All / Show Owned</li><li data-uuid="c4028182-3be7-4e4e-b8b7-e78a2fec36ae">Show Revisions</li><li data-uuid="32f097b0-87b4-4d3b-b807-b1b812826153">Show IP classification attribute</li></ul><p>This update streamlines connector settings management and improves consistency across DataHub integrations.</p><h3>Improved DOORS Next Connector Data Source Switching</h3><p>The <strong>DOORS Next (DNG) connector</strong> has been enhanced to improve synchronization accuracy when using the <strong>Copy DHLink from other data source</strong> command.</p><p>When this command is applied, DataHub now correctly updates synchronized items to reference the appropriate <strong>stream or baseline data source</strong> after switching to another data source. OSLC links on synchronized items are automatically redirected to the correct target data source, ensuring that links remain aligned with the selected stream or baseline context.</p><p><strong>Benefits</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="9888a7c8-848b-410c-86e8-207dec3b40df">More accurate stream and baseline synchronization</li><li data-uuid="6c5f8e48-7be1-4149-9626-aeafa1d3a494">Correct OSLC link targeting after data source switching</li><li data-uuid="e7b1da08-1983-4193-ac2b-a044651ea394">Reduced risk of broken or misdirected synchronization references</li><li data-uuid="778c4112-3c6a-442e-b7eb-d70fb0c98674">Improved consistency across multiple DNG configurations</li></ul><p>This enhancement significantly improves reliability for configuration-specific workflows and change management processes involving multiple DOORS Next data sources.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261621685 space=VN version=71 -->
## PAGE 00257: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services

- page_id: `261621685`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261621685/Magic+Collaboration+Studio+Teamwork+Cloud+and+Services
- version_number: 71
- version_date: `2026-07-14T13:16:05.499+02:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### To be released on: November 6, 2026

The 2026x Refresh2 release introduces enhancements across MagicLab Collaborator, Teamwork Cloud, and Cameo Collaborator, improving collaboration, resource management, usability, and automation.

Key updates include in-context commenting in Cameo modeling tools, bringing the MagicLab Collaborator commenting capabilities directly within the desktop client.

For Teamwork Cloud, we introduce model lifecycle management for UML-based resources (UML, SysML v1, UAF, and so on), improved resource history analysis, element-level webhooks for SysML v2, and enhanced control over synchronized resource editing. The release also delivers usability improvements such as upgraded UI components and streamlined administration workflows.

Cameo Collaborator offers improved document selection, OAuth 2.0 mail authentication support, and persistent user state (technology preview). Additionally, the new SysML v2 Validation Services API enables automated model validation and integration of validation workflows into CI/CD pipelines.

#### MagicLab Collaborator

##### Comment Panel in Cameo Modeling Tools

From the 2026x Refresh2 release, the Cameo modeling tools include a dedicatedCommentstab within theSpecificationpanel. It brings the MagicLab Collaborator commenting experience directly into the desktop client, serving as a bridge between web-based collaboration and the modeling environment. For engineers, this enables them to address comments directly in their modeling environment without leaving it.

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### Model Lifecycle Management - Technology Preview

Starting with the 2026x Refresh2 release, Teamwork Cloud provides initial support for model lifecycle management at the element level. For added convenience, users can manage lifecycle configuration through the Teamwork Cloud Admin Settings application, including enabling or disabling the feature and assigning resources or resource categories to the default schema.

This feature is available as a technology preview.

##### Resource History Improvements

To improve the usability of the Resource History view, the 2026x Refresh2 release introduces user-controlled visibility filters and layout customization options. Users can now filter branches by status, view the lifecycle of a selected branch, customize the commit table columns, and more. These enhancements make branch review and merge analysis faster and more convenient, reduce complexity when working with resources that have extensive histories, and simplify the management of complex workflows.

##### Element-Level Webhooks For SysML v2 Resources

Introduced support for element-level webhooks for SysML v2 resources. Element-level webhook functionality now works consistently with UML resources, enabling users to receive notifications about changes to individual model elements and improve integration with external systems.

##### Enhanced Synchronized Resource Editability

Added the ability to make synchronized resources editable directly from the Resources application in the Teamwork Cloud Admin Console. A new**Make editable**button is available in the resource details side panel, allowing users to remove the read-only restriction without requiring a Teamwork Cloud server restart. This simplifies resource management and provides a faster way to enable editing for synchronized resources. ([TWCSRV-12120](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/TWCSRV-12120))

##### Other

- Upgraded the supported services UI library to Angular Material 3 (M3), providing long-term support, improved accessibility, and alignment with the latest Material Design standards. ([TWCSRV-12130](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/TWCSRV-12130))
- Implemented bulk deletion of announcement configurations in the Teamwork Cloud Admin Settings application by adding support for multi-selection. This streamlines administration and reduces the time required to manage multiple announcement configurations. ( TWCSRV-11503 )
- The Teamwork Cloud Admin Settings application now supports automatic re-authentication for multiple synchronization configurations that use the same source server. This simplifies synchronization management by reducing the need to manually re-authenticate each configuration individually. ( TWCSRV-12271 )

#### Cameo Collaborator

##### SysML v2 Validation Service

Introduced a new Validation Services API group alongside the existing Evaluation Services group in the SysML v2 API. This enables model validation through the API, allowing users to automate validation workflows, identify model issuesprogrammatically in an automated way, and integrate validation seamlessly into development pipelines. ([WAP-18013](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/WAP-18013))

##### Other

- Improved the Cameo Collaborator Publish dialog for cases involving a large number of documents. Users can now click the three-dot menu to open a dialog and select documents from a nested tree view, making document selection faster and more convenient. ([WAP-17402](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/WAP-17402))
- As SMTP AUTH Basic Authentication is gradually being deprecated, support for OAuth 2.0 authentication has been added to mail configuration. This provides a more secure and future-ready authentication method for email communication. ([WAP-17669](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/WAP-17669))
- Technology preview: Persistent user state — Introduced the ability to preserve user state to improve convenience and continuity. Users no longer need to reopen diagrams, tables, or text editors after refreshing the page, as their working context is restored automatically. ([WAP-16335](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/WAP-16335))

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261621623 space=VN version=54 -->
## PAGE 00259: Magic Cyber Systems Engineer / Cameo Systems Modeler

- page_id: `261621623`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261621623/Magic+Cyber+Systems+Engineer+Cameo+Systems+Modeler
- version_number: 54
- version_date: `2026-03-13T14:10:31.926+01:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### top

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

#### SysML v1 Features

##### Nested Connector Import

Import from Excel files has been enhanced to support nested connectors. You can now define connectors between elements located at different levels of the model structure, and the tool will automatically construct the corresponding nested connector paths during import. This improvement enables accurate reconstruction of complex internal structures directly from external data sources, reducing manual adjustments and improving modeling efficiency. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Importing-data-from-Excel-and-CSV-files&version=latest&variant=default)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Simulation Features

##### Instance Selection in Behavioral Diagrams

The 2026x Refresh1 version of the plugin allows you to filter animation for one specific object in State Machine, Activity, and Sequence diagrams.If several instances of the same behavior exist, the diagram automatically shows animation only for the first instance annotated in the diagram. This prevents the diagram from merging events from multiple concurrent instances and improves clarity during simulation analysis. You can switch the displayed instance from the contextual menu.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 Selecting the Instance to be animated in a State Machine Diagram.

##### UI Mockup Improvements

This release introduces the ability to display the unit symbol in UI mockups. In addition, a new Simulation Configuration property — **Show Units** — allows you to control whether units are displayed in diagrams and UI mockups.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying units in UI mockups

##### Other Improvements

- Time Series and Timeline Charts now support negative time values.
- UI mockups, graphs, and other elements generated during model execution are displayed with an automatic offset, making them easier to rearrange. In addition, the adjusted layout is now preserved between execution runs.

#### Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud

##### Partial Project Usages

We are excited to announce that Partial Project Usages is now out of the technology preview phase, featuring a significantly improved overall user experience. As in previous releases, the feature allows you to define the usage scope by selecting a specific part of a project, and enables seamless switching between full and partial usages as well as easy adjustment of the usage scope at any time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Partial project usage

##### Saving Projects to an Earlier Version

The previous release introduced the ability to save projects to an earlier major version—for example, from version 2026x to 2024x—making it easier to collaborate with teams using older versions of the modeling tool.

In version 2026x Refresh1, this functionality brings the following improvements:

- If your project uses other projects, all used projects are saved together with the open project.
- You can now save Teamwork Cloud projects to an older version as .mdzip files without first saving them locally.

##### SysML v2 Project Migration to Version 2026x Refresh1

The new release supports several options for migrating **SysML v2 projects** to version 2026x Refresh1. You can migrate all or selected projects on the Teamwork Cloud server in bulk via the **Collaborate** menu, or migrate standalone Teamwork Cloud or local repository projects by opening them and agreeing to update standard libraries.

This prevents the diagram from merging events from multiple concurrent instances and improves clarity during simulation analysis. You can switch the displayed instance from the contextual menu.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_selection.png" /></ac:image><br /> Selecting the Instance to be animated in a State Machine Diagram.</h6><h3>UI Mockup Improvements</h3><p>This release introduces the ability to display the unit symbol in UI mockups. In addition, a new Simulation Configuration property — <strong>Show Units</strong> — allows you to control whether units are displayed in diagrams and UI mockups.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="units_in_ui_mockups.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Displaying units in UI mockups</span></h6><h3>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="12e552fc-e21b-4680-a85b-6b5f3602738d">Time Series and Timeline Charts now support negative time values.</li><li data-uuid="be2513d2-a2fc-4a88-b26f-4bcc2360118f">UI mockups, graphs, and other elements generated during model execution are displayed with an automatic offset, making them easier to rearrange. In addition, the adjusted layout is now preserved between execution runs.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261621563 space=VN version=75 -->
## PAGE 00262: Magic Systems of Systems Architect / Cameo Enterprise Architecture

- page_id: `261621563`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261621563/Magic+Systems+of+Systems+Architect+Cameo+Enterprise+Architecture
- version_number: 75
- version_date: `2026-03-13T14:10:36.022+01:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### top

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

#### SysML v1 Features

##### Nested Connector Import

Import from Excel files has been enhanced to support nested connectors. You can now define connectors between elements located at different levels of the model structure, and the tool will automatically construct the corresponding nested connector paths during import. This improvement enables accurate reconstruction of complex internal structures directly from external data sources, reducing manual adjustments and improving modeling efficiency. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=Importing-data-from-Excel-and-CSV-files&version=latest&variant=default)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Collaboration in Teamwork Cloud

##### Partial Project Usages

We are excited to announce that Partial Project Usages is now out of the technology preview phase, featuring a significantly improved overall user experience. As in previous releases, the feature allows you to define the usage scope by selecting a specific part of a project, and enables seamless switching between full and partial usages as well as easy adjustment of the usage scope at any time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Partial project usage

##### Saving Projects to an Earlier Version

The previous release introduced the ability to save projects to an earlier major version—for example, from version 2026x to 2024x—making it easier to collaborate with teams using older versions of the modeling tool.

In version 2026x Refresh1, this functionality brings the following improvements:

- If your project uses other projects, all used projects are saved together with the open project.
- You can now save Teamwork Cloud projects to an older version as .mdzip files without first saving them locally.

##### SysML v2 Project Migration to Version 2026x Refresh1

The new release supports several options for migrating **SysML v2 projects** to version 2026x Refresh1. You can migrate all or selected projects on the Teamwork Cloud server in bulk via the **Collaborate** menu, or migrate standalone Teamwork Cloud or local repository projects by opening them and agreeing to update standard libraries.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=291833582 space=VN version=17 -->
## PAGE 00266: SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin

- page_id: `291833582`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/291833582/SysML+v2+Evaluation+Plugin
- version_number: 17
- version_date: `2026-03-12T14:20:07.281+01:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: June 26, 2026

##### Textual Representation Support for Constraints

The new version of the SysML v2 Evaluation plugin enables the use of textual representation elements to evaluate constraints written in other scripting languages. As a result, scripting languages can now be used not only for standard calculations, but also to evaluate both requirements and standalone constraints.

partvehicle{attributespeed:Real=100;assertconstraint{language"groovy"/*speed==100 */} 
}

Support for Binding Connectors

The 2026x Refresh1 release introduces support for binding connectors used with feature chains in expressions. This enhancement enables you to evaluate calculations and execute performActions in model views. During evaluation, output values are calculated using eitherdefault valuesorvalues obtained through binding connectors.

##### Defining Initial Values During Evaluation

You can now enter initial values during evaluation using model views, even if no value is defined in the model. Such features and attributes are displayed with “:=” and an empty field in both shapes and compartments.

##### Displaying Units for Evaluated Values

Views now display units next to calculated values when units are defined in the model. This improves clarity and makes results easier to interpret.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying units next to evaluated values in model views.

##### Evaluating Part Definitions

In addition to part usages, the new plugin version now allows you to evaluate part definitions as well. When a part definition is evaluated directly, all of its features are evaluated recursively. When evaluating an entire model view, only the first-level part definitions are processed. This enhancement helps you quickly analyze the structure of a part definition without having to create part usages.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Initiating the evaluation of a part definition in a model view.

##### Evaluation Results for Individual Requirement Constraints

When a requirement has multiple constraints, model views now display the evaluation result for each constraint individually. This improvement provides clearer insight into how the overall requirement result is determined and makes it easier to understand evaluation results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample requirement displaying evaluation results for individual constraints.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
