<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=291833414 space=VN version=82 -->
## PAGE 00267: SysML v2 Plugin

- page_id: `291833414`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/291833414/SysML+v2+Plugin
- version_number: 82
- version_date: `2026-07-14T08:58:50.331+02:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Planned release on: November 6, 2026

The SysML v2 Plugin 2026xRefresh2 release introduces...

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&version=2026x%20Refresh1).

### topSysML v2 Features

##### Diagram Interchange Plugin

TBD

##### Sequence View Technology Preview

Sequence View has been introduced as a dedicated view for modeling messages and time-ordered event occurrences on lifelines of exposed features. In the view, you can create or display features such as parts with their lifelines, as well as create messages with or without payloads.This enables event ordering and message exchanges to be modeled and reviewed directly in a dedicated view, providing a clearer representation of system behavior.The view is available in the Create View dialog and the Create View submenu for easy access.Sequence View is currently a work in progress and is provided as a technology preview.

Learn more: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='SYSML2P'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbolic views' space='SYSML2P']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Model Validation Improvements

###### Validation Suites and Rules Creation Improvements

You can now create validation suites directly from the Project Validation Suites dialog and create validation rules for a suite through the Specification panel. This streamlines the validation authoring workflow by allowing validation suites and rules to be created without using the Textual Editor, making customization more accessible. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites and rules' space='SYSML2P']>]]> [SYSML2-10883](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-10883)[SYSML2-10884](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-10884)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### New Validation Suite and Rules

- Multiple new validation rules have been added to the Dassault Systèmes Validation Suite , providing broader validation coverage and helping identify additional modeling issues. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dassault Systemes Validation Suite' space='SYSML2P']>]]>
- A new validation suite, Dassault Systèmes Composition Integrity Validation Suite , has been introduced to complement the existing validation suites with additional composition integrity checks, helping ensure model consistency and correctness. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dassault Systemes Composition Integrity Validation Suite' space='SYSML2P']>]]>

##### Type Specification Improvements

Type specification is now more accessible throughout the modeling workflow, reducing navigation and making it faster to assign and manage feature types. The improvements include:

- Specify feature types for elements using the Select Type command available in the Smart Manipulator. Learn more >> SYSML2-10365

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can now easily specify feature types for elements in the Specification panel’s Type field using the Select Type button directly in the Type field. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-ModifyingthefeaturetypeviatheSpecificationpanel) SYSML2-10370

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Add additional feature types directly from the Type field in the Specification panel using the Add Type button. Learn more >> SYSML2-11834

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The Type field in the Specification panel now also displays types inherited through redefinition, subsetting, reference subsetting, and feature typing. Learn more >> SYSML2-8712

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Style Sheet Specification Improvements

- You can now specify condition values for style sheet rules in the Specification panel using the **Add**button directly in the value field. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying a condition for a rule' space='SYSML2P']>]]> [SYSML2-11835](https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-11835)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- When specifying condition values for style sheet rules in the Specification panel, drag-and-drop operations now append values instead of replacing existing ones. Learn more >>

##### Tabular Views Improvements

TBD

##### Specification Panel Improvements

###### Relations tab

TheSpecificationpanel has been enhanced with aRelationstab that provides access to the relations of the selected element. Relations are organized into categories such asInterface,Feature Typing,Connection,Exhibit, and others.This makes it easier to review an element's relations, navigate to them in the Containment tree, or open them for editing in the Specification panel or Textual Editor. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-Relationstab)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Find Functionality

The Find functionality is now available in the Specification panel. Press Ctrl+F (Windows/Linux) or Cmd+F (macOS) to quickly locate properties and elements by entering text in the Find field, making navigation within large specifications faster and more efficient. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-UsingtheFindintheSpecificationpanel)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element-Typed Field Display Limit

Element-typed fields now display up to 10 elements by default to optimize visual space. When more elements are available, you can click the appropriate label to expand the field and view all elements. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-ModifyingthefeaturetypeviatheSpecificationpanel)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Display Semantic Library Elements

The **Display Library Elements** toggle button in the Specification window has been renamed to **Display Semantic Library Elements**to reflect its updated behavior. Library elements are now always visible in the window, while semantic library elements remain hidden by default and can be displayed using the toggle button. This change provides a more consistent property display experience across the Specification panel and Specification window. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577248/Specification+window#Specificationwindow-Togglingsemanticlibraryelements%27visibilityintheSpecificationwindow)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Other Improvements

- You can now easily navigate from a connection usage to its owned or inherited flow payload using the **Go To** command in the element shortcut menu. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element navigation' space='SYSML2P']>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- The **Drag and Drop**handler is now available when multiple element symbols are selected, enabling more efficient modeling by allowing you to work with multiple elements at once. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space='SYSML2P']>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- New symbol style attributes have been introduced:
  - **Show Name In Tab**- allows you to specify whether the package name and keywords should be displayed in the tab.
  - **Label Orientation**- allows you to specify the label orientation.
  - **Label Position**- allows you to specify the position of labels relative to the shape.
  - **Show Direction Icon**- allows you to specify whether the direction icon should be displayed on the element symbol.
  - **Show Alias Short Name**- allows you to specify whether the element's alias short name should be displayed on the element symbol.
  - **Show Alias Name**- allows you to specify whether the element's alias name should be displayed on the element symbol. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='SYSML2P']>]]>
- Support for the following compartments has been introduced:
  - **Result**- allowing you to display result expressions for calculation usages and definitions.
  - **Filters**- allowing you to display filters for packages, view usages, and definitions. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='SYSML2P']>]]>
- Boolean-type value editor in the Specification panel now displays value selection as a checkbox. Learn more >> [ATTACHMENT filename='Boolean-typed element value specification.png']

##### Documentation

- SysML v2 Plugin 2026x Refresh2

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Planned release on: November 6, 2026</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The SysML v2 Plugin 2026x</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>Refresh2 release introduces<span> ...</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh1">Downloading installation files</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span> </span></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc86ee14-c8f3-436e-b8a4-d5de71309a65"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>SysML v2 Features</h1><h3>Diagram Interchange Plugin</h3><p>TBD</p><h3>Sequence View Technology Preview</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Sequence View has been introduced as a dedicated view for modeling messages and time-ordered event occurrences on lifelines of exposed features. In the view, you can create or display features such as parts with their lifelines, as well as create messages with or without payloads. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This enables event ordering and message exchanges to be modeled and reviewed directly in a dedicated view, providing a clearer representation of system behavior. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The view is available in the Create View dialog and the Create View submenu for easy access. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Sequence View is currently a work in progress and is provided as a technology preview.</span></p><p>Learn more: <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Message" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Symbolic views" /></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sequence view.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Model Validation Improvements</h3><h4><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Validation Suites and Rules Creation Improvements</span></h4><p>You can now create validation suites directly from the Project Validation Suites dialog and create validation rules for a suite through the Specification panel. This streamlines the validation authoring workflow by allowing validation suites and rules to be created without using the Textual Editor, making customization more accessible. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Creating validation suites and rules" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>  <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-10883">SYSML2-10883</a><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-10884">SYSML2-10884</a></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Validation Suites and Rules Creation Improvements.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>New Validation Suite and Rules</h4><ul><li data-uuid="bdbbe38f-9dd7-4a8a-a90e-839f9859ac8f">Multiple new validation rules have been added to the <em>Dassault Systèmes Validation Suite</em>, providing broader validation coverage and helping identify additional modeling issues. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Dassault Systemes Validation Suite" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="69293c07-f7a1-48fb-ad18-62f5068990fa">A new validation suite, <em>Dassault Systèmes Composition Integrity Validation Suite</em>, has been introduced to complement the existing validation suites with additional composition integrity checks, helping ensure model consistency and correctness. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Dassault Systemes Composition Integrity Validation Suite" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Type Specification Improvements</h3><p class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchorContainer">Type specification is now more accessible throughout the modeling workflow, reducing navigation and making it faster to assign and manage feature types. The improvements include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a8ca6ee0-d11d-418b-82d2-1992e705038b">Specify feature types for elements using the <strong>Select Type</strong> command available in the Smart Manipulator. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577342/Feature+typing#Featuretyping-Defininganelementviathesmartmanipulatorinaview">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-10365">SYSML2-10365</a></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Type command in the Smart Manipulator.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="d027290a-23bc-458a-b383-324cd351c3a6">You can now easily specify feature types for elements in the Specification panel’s <strong>Type </strong>field using the <strong>Select Type </strong>button directly in the <strong>Type </strong>field. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-ModifyingthefeaturetypeviatheSpecificationpanel">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span> <a href="https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-10370" style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">SYSML2-10370</a></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Type in Specification Panel.png" /></ac:image></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="2d37421f-5c3a-4491-8ab8-147d4b2526a0">Add additional feature types directly from the <strong>Type</strong> field in the Specification panel using the <strong>Add Type</strong> button. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-ModifyingthefeaturetypeviatheSpecificationpanel">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a><span> </span><a href="https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-11834">SYSML2-11834</a><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add Type in Specification Panel.png" /></ac:image></span></p><ul><li>The <strong>Type </strong>field in the Specification panel now also displays types inherited through redefinition, subsetting, reference subsetting, and feature typing. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-ModifyingthefeaturetypeviatheSpecificationpanel">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a> <a href="https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-8712" class="">SYSML2-8712</a></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inherited types.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Style Sheet Specification Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="1f102ec7-c753-435a-ae19-a768e3d48f90"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">You can now specify condition values for style sheet rules in the Specification panel using the <strong>Add </strong>button directly in the value field. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Specifying a condition for a rule" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <a href="https://kns-jira.dsone.3ds.com/browse/SYSML2-11835" class="">SYSML2-11835</a></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition value selection.png" /></ac:image></span></p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">When specifying condition values for style sheet rules in the Specification panel, drag-and-drop operations now append values instead of replacing existing ones. </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125633/Specifying+a+condition+for+a+rule" style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></li></ul><h3>Tabular Views Improvements</h3><p>TBD</p><h3>Specification Panel Improvements</h3><h4>Relations tab</h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Specification</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> panel has been enhanced with a </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Relations</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> tab that provides access to the relations of the selected element. Relations are organized into categories such as </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Interface</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Feature Typing</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Connection</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Exhibit</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, and others. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">This makes it easier to review an element's relations, navigate to them in the Containment tree, or open them for editing in the Specification panel or Textual Editor. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-Relationstab">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Relations tab.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Find Functionality</h4><p>The Find functionality is now available in the Specification panel. Press Ctrl+F (Windows/Linux) or Cmd+F (macOS) to quickly locate properties and elements by entering text in the Find field, making navigation within large specifications faster and more efficient. <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-UsingtheFindintheSpecificationpanel">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Find Functionality.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Element-Typed Field Display Limit</h4><p>Element-typed fields now display up to 10 elements by default to optimize visual space. When more elements are available, you can click the appropriate label to expand the field and view all elements. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-ModifyingthefeaturetypeviatheSpecificationpanel">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element-Typed Field Display Limit.png" /></ac:image></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">Display Semantic Library Elements</h4><p style="text-align: left;">The <strong>Display Library Elements</strong> toggle button in the Specification window has been renamed to <strong>Display Semantic Library Elements </strong>to reflect its updated behavior. Library elements are now always visible in the window, while semantic library elements remain hidden by default and can be displayed using the toggle button. This change provides a more consistent property display experience across the Specification panel and Specification window. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577248/Specification+window#Specificationwindow-Togglingsemanticlibraryelements%27visibilityintheSpecificationwindow">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display Semantic Library Elements.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><h3>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="d7936e26-384f-495a-991e-f59428e4789f"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can now easily navigate from a connection usage to its owned or inherited flow payload using the <strong>Go To</strong> command in the element shortcut menu. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Element navigation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Navigating to flow payload.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="8f10cb4a-b854-4aeb-8392-2e6dc3ba8ff3"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The <strong>Drag and Drop </strong>handler is now available when multiple element symbols are selected, enabling more efficient modeling by allowing you to work with multiple elements at once. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Drag and Drop handler For Multiple Symbols.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="42967d77-8024-4189-b817-444ad00150ea"><span>New symbol style attributes have been introduced:</span><ul><li data-uuid="4bf6802a-4f26-4859-a3df-0858865f5eda"><span><strong>Show Name In Tab </strong>- allows you to specify whether the package name and keywords should be displayed in the tab.</span></li><li data-uuid="d1e938f7-6a83-4f45-a6e0-5cc9afd2b279"><span><strong>Label Orientation </strong>- allows you to specify the label orientation. </span></li><li data-uuid="90da375f-ee4f-4d8b-888d-c632daf99dbd"><span><strong>Label Position </strong>- allows you to specify the position of labels relative to the shape.</span></li><li data-uuid="09832dd4-fc22-4440-8ada-8c26e9478f8c"><span><strong>Show Direction Icon </strong>- allows you to specify whether the direction icon should be displayed on the element symbol.</span></li><li data-uuid="105947e5-8c2c-4ea8-bce8-ef18f435f9fa"><span><strong>Show Alias Short Name </strong>- allows you to specify whether the element's alias short name should be displayed on the element symbol.</span></li><li data-uuid="a25374dc-73e9-41eb-a05c-db158a987f8d"><span><strong>Show Alias Name </strong>- allows you to specify whether the element's alias name should be displayed on the element symbol.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="af26efae-fcd4-4c3d-bb16-2e3402fcb492"><span>Support for the following compartments has been introduced:</span><ul><li data-uuid="10bdd875-ae16-4755-9907-ca85a5adf84e"><span><strong>Result </strong>- allowing you to display result expressions for calculation usages and definitions. </span></li><li data-uuid="7200a79a-2cf3-47fe-8e9f-f1382062ee70"><span><strong>Filters </strong>- allowing you to display filters for packages, view usages, and definitions. <br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="54522b9b-57e5-4a04-b797-2d29658dfec1">Boolean-type value editor in the <strong>Specification panel</strong> now displays value selection as a checkbox. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577226/Specification+panel#Specificationpanel-ModifyingthefeaturevalueviatheSpecificationpanel">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Boolean-typed element value specification.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p style="text-align: right;"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Documentation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="2c9505f3-ff17-4f89-a68b-ae07ea10516c"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2P/?contextKey=sysml-v2-plugin-documentation&amp;version=2026x%20Refresh2">SysML v2 Plugin 2026x Refresh2</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180751 space=VN version=41 -->
## PAGE 00268: SysML v2 Simulation Plugin

- page_id: `314180751`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/314180751/SysML+v2+Simulation+Plugin
- version_number: 41
- version_date: `2026-07-14T14:12:51.247+02:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 6, 2026

##### Simulation Configuration

With the new version of the SysML v2 Simulation plugin, you can define simulation configurations, allowing you to reuse and execute simulation scenarios without repeatedly configuring simulation settings.A simulation configuration defines the execution target, startup behavior, simulation speed, simulation start and end times, startup view, and other simulation options. You can execute simulation configurations from the simulation configurationdrop-down list in the main toolbar or by right-clicking a simulation configuration in the Containment tree and selecting**Run**.

###### Time Series Chart Configuration Support

Simulation configurations supportTime Series Chart configurations, which define the simulation data to display and chart presentation options. One or more charts can be associated with a simulation configuration and are opened automatically when the simulation starts. During execution, chart options such as interpolation, data point visibility, and value visibility can be adjusted interactively.

###### Data Export Configuration Support

Simulation configurations also support data export configurations, which define the simulation data to export when the simulation completes. One or more data export configurations can be associated with a simulation configuration, allowing selected simulation values to be automatically exported to a CSV file.

The example below demonstrate how to define simulation, chart, and data export configurations using SysML v2 textual notation.

#### PANEL: Example

Example

packageSimConfigs{ 
itemWaterTankChartConfig{ 
@DS_Simulation::SimulationMetadata::'time series chart'{ 
:>>values= ( 
WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystem.waterTank.level, 
WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystem.controller.valvePort.valve 
); 
:>>title="Water Tank System"; 
:>>subtitle="Water Level and Valve State"; 
:>>isInterpolated=true; 
:>>showValues=false; 
:>>showDataPoints=false; 
} 
}

itemWaterTankDataConfig{ 
@DS_Simulation::SimulationMetadata::'data export'{ 
:>>values= ( 
WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystem.waterTank.level, 
WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystem.controller.valvePort.valve 
); 
:>>fileName="WaterTankSystemResults"; 
} 
} 
 
itemWaterTankConfig{ 
@DS_Simulation::SimulationMetadata::'sim config'{ 
:>>executionTarget=WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystem; 
:>>autoStart:=false; 
:>>speed:=10; 
:>>startupView=WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystemView; 
:>>decimalPlaces=5; 
:>>UI=WaterTankChartConfig; 
:>>dataExport=WaterTankDataConfig; 
} 
} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Executing the simulation configuration defined in the example above.

##### Action Simulation

The new action simulation capability allows you to execute SysML v2 behavioral models and analyze how system actions evolve at runtime. Simulation follows the behavioral flows defined in the model, including action successions, composite actions, decision logic, guards, forks, joins, and merge behavior, helping you validate complex sequential, conditional, and parallel behaviors.

Action simulation also supports time-aware and data-aware behavior modeling. You can define action duration constraints, simulate interruptible behaviors, and transfer data between actions using succession flows and binding support. These capabilities make it possible to analyze how system behavior evolves over time and how information moves through the model.

In addition, simulation annotations show active, visited, and last visited elements directly in model views.

Together, these capabilities help you validate behavioral models more effectively and identify modeling issues earlier.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">title</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Water Tank System&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">subtitle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Water Level and Valve State&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isInterpolated</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showValues</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showDataPoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span></p><p><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">WaterTankDataConfig</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        @</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Simulation::SimulationMetadata::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'data export'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">values</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = (</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">waterTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">level</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">,</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">controller</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">valvePort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">valve</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            );</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fileName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;WaterTankSystemResults&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">WaterTankConfig</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        @</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Simulation::SimulationMetadata::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'sim config'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">executionTarget</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">autoStart</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> := </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">speed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> := </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">10</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">startupView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">WaterTankSystem::WaterTankSystemView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decimalPlaces</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">UI</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">WaterTankChartConfig</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">dataExport</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">WaterTankDataConfig</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="isSelectedEnd" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="running_simulation_configuration.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 class="isSelectedEnd" style="text-align: center;">Executing the simulation configuration defined in the example above.</h6><h3 class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchorContainer" data-end="34" data-section-id="1se30kg" data-start="0"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="34ea2b88-0c28-4d2f-b274-b5f5a9dfb170">Action Simulation</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchorContainer" data-end="1250" data-start="868">The new action simulation capability allows you to execute SysML v2 behavioral models and analyze how system actions evolve at runtime. Simulation follows the behavioral flows defined in the model, including action successions, composite actions, decision logic, guards, forks, joins, and merge behavior, helping you validate complex sequential, conditional, and parallel behaviors.</p><p data-end="1614" data-start="1252">Action simulation also supports time-aware and data-aware behavior modeling. You can define action duration constraints, simulate interruptible behaviors, and transfer data between actions using succession flows and binding support. These capabilities make it possible to analyze how system behavior evolves over time and how information moves through the model.</p><p data-end="1724" data-start="1616">In addition, simulation annotations show active, visited, and last visited elements directly in model views.</p><p data-end="1845" data-start="1726">Together, these capabilities help you validate behavioral models more effectively and identify modeling issues earlier.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272744023 space=VN version=5 -->
## PAGE 00269: Systems Cybersecurity Designer

- page_id: `272744023`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/272744023/Systems+Cybersecurity+Designer
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-01T16:59:20.831+01:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

In this release, a new Cybersecurity Template: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DO-326A Preliminary Security Risk Assessment' space='SCD']has been introduced, which is based on the [DO-326A](https://products.rtca.org/21dja1f/)standard. TheDO-326Astandard, titled*Airworthiness Security Process Specification*, provides guidance for the implementation of an airworthiness cybersecurity process.

##### DO-326A Preliminary Security Risk Assessment

Cybersecurity engineering is a critical discipline that ensures the safe and secure operation of connected and complex airborne systems. The increasing integration of digital and networked technologies in aviation demands a robust, lifecycle-oriented approach to manage cybersecurity risks. This update implements the[DO-326A](https://products.rtca.org/21dja1f/)methodology within the Systems Cybersecurity Designer to enable compliance assessments during the development of avionics systems.

DO-326A, published by RTCA and EUROCAE, defines the process framework for airworthiness security — that is, ensuring that cybersecurity threats do not compromise the safety and continued airworthiness of an aircraft. Its main principles include:

- A risk-based approach to managing cybersecurity threats to aircraft systems.
- Lifecycle integration, requiring threat and mitigation analysis from system concept through decommissioning.

The Systems Cybersecurity Designer now enables users to:

- Model avionics systems for cybersecurity risk assessment , incorporating assets, vulnerabilities, threat scenarios, attack paths, and mitigations in line with DO-326A guidance.
- Perform a Security Risk Assessment (SRA) and Aircraft System Security Assessment (ASSA) using structured, iterative methodologies.
- Link safety and cybersecurity analyses , helping teams ensure consistency between security measures and system safety objectives.
- Produce certification-ready artifacts that demonstrate compliance with DO-326A, and support alignment with DO-355 (Information Security Guidance for Continuing Airworthiness) and DO-356A (Security Methods and Considerations).

By embedding DO-326A methodology into the design workflow, this update empowers aerospace engineers to reduce certification risk, identify security issues early in development, and streamline the path toward regulatory approval.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### DO-326A Index.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Systems Cybersecurity Designer' space='SCD']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622281 space=VN version=51 -->
## PAGE 00271: UAF 1.3 Plugin

- page_id: `261622281`
- space_key: `VN`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VN/pages/261622281/UAF+1.3+Plugin
- version_number: 51
- version_date: `2026-01-23T14:38:01.921+01:00`
- ancestors: 2026x Refresh2 Version News
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Released on: November 28, 2025

In the**2026x**release, we implemented the**UAF 1.3 standard**, which includes key features for Mission Engineering Modeling. The**UAF 1.3 Profile**is fully supported in UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks. Therefore, UAF 1.2 is no longer supported in this version. Moreover, the New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available.

To download the latest version, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].For further information, check the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.3 modeling' space='MT'].

##### Methods and Standards

Beginning with this version, the **UAF 1.3 Plugin** is available for production use. The UAF 1.3 Profile is fully supported in UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**IMPORTANT!**The UAF 1.2 Plugin is no longer supported starting with the 2026x version.

Projects created in earlier versions **require**the following migration before you can continue working with them:

- UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>
- UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.3' space='MT']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### UAF 1.3 Profile Support

600400

Together with the**UAF 1.3 Profile**, the following changes areimplementedto enable**Mission Engineering modeling in the 2026x version**:

- New elements, properties, and relationships were added. Learn more >>>
- The measurements library was updated. New Force Designation enumeration added.
- New diagram in Strategic Processes:
  - Mission Phases diagram
- New diagrams in Operational Processes:
  - [Mission Threads diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/working-with-mission-threads-diagram-264929457.html)
  - [Mission Threads Flow diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/working-with-mission-threads-flow-diagram-264929460.html)
- New diagram in Resources Processes:
  - [Mission Engineering Threads diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/working-with-mission-engineering-threads-diagram-258048820.html)
- New diagram in Parameters:
  - Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram
- The measurement tables were updated to align with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes. Now you can add Activities and their specializations directly to the measurement tables, because Activities are treated as Property Sets and can own measurements. Learn more about Measurements >>>
- The Implementation Matrix was updated by adding two new pairs: Function to Actual Strategic Phase and Service Function to Operational Activity.[Learn more about Implementation Matrix >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/creating-implementation-matrix-249565176.html)
- The UAF template for the Cameo Collaborator was updated in accordance with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Library Support

New**DISR and UJTL library versions**are now available:

- UJTL (20250715)
- DISR (20240606)

You can choose the library version when creating a new project.[Learn more about using libraries >>>](https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/using-libraries-249565388.html)

##### UAF/UPDM Compliance Mode for BPMN2 projects

The**“UPDM Profile for BPMN”**is no longer available in this version. All of its stereotypes have been moved to the existing**BPMN Customization Profile**. In addition, a new**UAF/UPDM compliance mode**has been introduced for BPMN projects, allowing you to choose whether the compliant UAF/UPDM stereotypes (depending on the plugin) should be applied to BPMN elements.

##### Validation

- The new correctness validation rulesdedicated to checking if the client and supplier are correct have been introduced for the following relationships:
  - Opposes, Defines . New relationships, which were implemented with the UAF 1.3 Profile support.
  - Existing relationships: Phases, Compares To, Governed By, Maps To Goal, Owns Value, Creates, Enables, Evoked By, Presented By, Sequence
- The new correctness validation rules were implemented to verify whether the classifiers are correct for the following elements:
  - **Actual Mission Scenario**. The classifier must be Mission Scenario or its specialization.
  - Actual Mission Vignette . The classifier must be Mission Vignette or its specialization.
- The new correctness validation rule was implemented to verify whether the behavior is correct for the Mission Task Action . The behavior must be a Mission Task or its specialization.

##### Discontinued Products

The**AutoStyler plugin**is deprecated, with the intention of removing it with the next major version release, the 2028x version.

##### Other Enhancements

- A new backwards compatibility feature allows UPDM 2.1 projects created in the 2026x version to be *Saved As Older Version*in 2024x compatible file format. This makes them directly compatible with the UPDM 2.1 plugin in 2024x, allowing you to open and work with these projects immediately in the 2024x version. This approach does not apply to the UAF 1.3 projects. Learn more >>>
- The UAF Enterprise Architecture Framework is now renamed to**EA-BPMN**. The perspective and project creation template was already renamed in the 2024x version.
- The «ActualDODAFProperties» stereotype will no longer be applied automatically after creating instances for «DODAFProperties». Moreover, the « DODAFProperties» stereotype is renamed to**«UAFProperties»**.
- In this version, the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table was renamed to the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table . Learn more >>>
- A new UAF BoK Sample is now available. This sample is based on the UAF WOW! book .
- This version includes new functionality that lets you select whether the Definition or Comment will serve as the Documentation for UAF elements.

##### Documentation

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.3 modeling' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">Released on: November 28, 2025</h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the</span><strong style="text-align: left;"> 2026x</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>release</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, we implemented the<span> </span><strong>UAF 1.3 standard</strong>, which includes key features for Mission Engineering Modeling. The<span> </span><strong>UAF 1.3 Profile</strong><span> </span>is fully supported in UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks. Therefore, UAF 1.2 is no longer supported in this version. Moreover, the New DISR and UJTL library versions are now available.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To download the latest version, see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For further information, check the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="UAF 1.3 modeling" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[product documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Methods and Standards</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Beginning with this version, the <strong>UAF 1.3 Plugin</strong> is available for production use. The UAF 1.3 Profile is fully supported in UAF, EA-BPMN, DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, and NAF 4.0 frameworks.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Evolution of standards" ac:width="864"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF_evolution.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong>IMPORTANT!</strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> The UAF 1.2 Plugin is no longer supported starting with the 2026x version.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span>   </span></p><p style="text-align: left;">Projects created in earlier versions <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong>require </strong></span>the following migration before you can continue working with them:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="92078979-9221-4bd9-8219-5c4dbbd83d09"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-magenta-bolder,#50253f);">UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-magenta-bolder,#50253f);"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the changes >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e696ab10-eff3-4a5f-a680-e0b7155315da"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-magenta-bolder,#50253f);">UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.3.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-magenta-bolder,#50253f);"> <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the changes >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li data-uuid="48fc411c-dd13-4ade-aead-3e62383b2b37">UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3. <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the changes >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="93ff5180-e92b-4b49-a657-4e844a7ba78e">UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.3.<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about the changes >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Possible migration paths"><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_paths.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3>UAF 1.3 Profile Support</h3><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dbf812f3-7994-4685-9597-1ea0374457a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://youtu.be/C_BgT9gL5gI" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Together with the<span> </span></span><strong>UAF 1.3 Profile</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, the following changes are<span> </span></span>implemented<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">to enable<span> </span><strong>Mission Engineering modeling in the 2026x version</strong></span>:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="63bb83da-12ea-45db-b5c7-9e5878d4e89c">New elements, properties, and relationships were added. <a class="conf-macro output-inline" style="text-decoration: none;" data-macro-name="sp-plaintextbody-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/uaf-1-2-to-uaf-1-3-new-elements-253046122.html">Learn more &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="b4866ff6-2e11-4bf4-97b0-96b16c4c0843">The measurements library was updated. New<strong><span> </span></strong>Force Designation enumeration added.</li><li data-uuid="15acf61a-9c12-42f3-ab96-4c7b6dbe81bb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New diagram in Strategic Processes:</span><ul><li><a style="text-decoration: none;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/working-with-mission-phases-diagram-249580036.html">Mission Phases diagram</a></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="384dff44-23e4-491a-a48e-1285d0bd1dba"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New diagrams in Operational Processes:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/working-with-mission-threads-diagram-264929457.html" style="text-decoration: none;">Mission Threads diagram</a></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/working-with-mission-threads-flow-diagram-264929460.html" style="text-decoration: none;">Mission Threads Flow diagram</a></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="ff1ecf02-25c0-43ab-a2c9-dce2e3c92010"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New diagram in Resources Processes:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/working-with-mission-engineering-threads-diagram-258048820.html" style="text-decoration: none;">Mission Engineering Threads diagram</a></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="9744a0b4-8598-48fc-aec5-4358691104d1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">New diagram in Parameters:</span><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/working-with-mission-vignette-and-scenario-diagram-249580042.html" style="text-decoration: none;">Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram</a></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="25560875-1e50-4dd9-8fa8-cf053f84d873">The measurement tables were updated to align with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes. Now you can add Activities and their specializations directly to the measurement tables, because Activities are treated as Property Sets and can own measurements.<span> </span><a class="conf-macro output-inline" style="text-decoration: none;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/measurements-249565281.html" data-macro-name="sp-plaintextbody-link">Learn more about Measurements &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="9b6123d5-5bae-4dc4-88bf-21066ead5533"><span>The Implementation Matrix was updated by adding two new pairs: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Function to Actual Strategic Phase and<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Service Function to Operational Activity.<span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/creating-implementation-matrix-249565176.html" class="conf-macro output-inline" style="text-decoration: none;" data-macro-name="sp-plaintextbody-link">Learn more about Implementation Matrix &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li data-uuid="f2d19615-1105-44df-b195-abfd0ac1b9b3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The UAF template for the Cameo Collaborator was updated in accordance with the UAF 1.3 Profile changes.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="UAF 1.3 grid" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Library Support</h3><p style="text-align: left;">New<span> </span><strong>DISR and UJTL library versions</strong><span> </span>are now available:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="93bec326-5595-4374-b9e9-9fca50a0145e"><strong>UJTL (20250715)</strong></li><li data-uuid="9d26c2b7-f548-4013-91f1-d17d3b279433"><strong>DISR (20240606)</strong></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">You can choose the library version when creating a new project.<span> </span><a class="conf-macro output-inline" style="text-decoration: none;" data-macro-name="sp-plaintextbody-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/using-libraries-249565388.html">Learn more about using libraries &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">UAF/UPDM Compliance Mode for BPMN2 projects</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">“UPDM Profile for BPMN”</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>is no longer available in this version. All of its stereotypes have been moved to the existing </span><strong style="text-align: left;">BPMN Customization Profile</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. In addition, a new<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">UAF/UPDM compliance mode</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>has been introduced for BPMN projects, allowing you to choose whether the compliant UAF/UPDM stereotypes (depending on the plugin) should be applied to BPMN elements.</span></p><h3>Validation</h3><ul><li data-uuid="d515cea3-6573-45b0-abb3-7d8d0b6a11e2"><span>The new correctness validation rules<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">dedicated to checking if the client and supplier are correct<span> </span></span></span><span>have been introduced for the following relationships:</span><ul><li><strong>Opposes, Defines</strong>. New relationships, which were implemented with the UAF 1.3 Profile support.</li><li>Existing relationships: Phases, Compares To, Governed By, Maps To Goal, Owns Value, Creates, Enables, Evoked By, Presented By, Sequence</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="196e2f1e-05b4-4e11-9405-74e5cb694b42">The new correctness validation rules were implemented to verify whether the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">classifiers are correct for the following elements:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Actual Mission Scenario</strong>. The classifier must be Mission Scenario or its specialization.</span></li><li><strong>Actual Mission Vignette</strong>. The classifier must be Mission Vignette or its specialization.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="4bda767c-14aa-4af3-9f3f-41f92ecb9c2a">The new correctness validation rule was implemented to verify whether the behavior is correct for the<span> </span><strong>Mission Task Action</strong>. The behavior must be a Mission Task or its specialization.</li></ul><h3>Discontinued Products</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The<span> </span><strong>AutoStyler plugin</strong><span> </span>is deprecated, with the intention of removing it with the next major version release, the 2028x version.</span></p><h3>Other Enhancements</h3><ul><li data-uuid="949bd868-77dc-4574-aedd-c818f4aef999">A new backwards compatibility feature allows<span> </span><strong>UPDM 2.1</strong><span> </span>projects created in the<span> </span><strong>2026x</strong><span> </span>version to be <strong><em>Saved As Older Version</em><span> </span>in 2024x</strong><span> </span>compatible file format. This makes them directly compatible with the UPDM 2.1 plugin in 2024x, allowing you to open and work with these projects immediately in the 2024x version. This approach does not apply to the UAF 1.3 projects. <a style="text-align: left;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MT/?contextKey=saving-projects-to-earlier-versions&amp;version=2026x&amp;variant=default" rel="nofollow">Learn more &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="248fbf0e-074d-4361-b713-2a8fd2e9d2f8">The<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">UAF Enterprise Architecture Framework is now renamed to<span> </span><strong>EA-BPMN</strong>. The perspective and project creation template was already renamed in the 2024x version.</span></li><li data-uuid="39c86a51-ebb2-4c70-aad3-2b987917475c"><span class="ghx-inner">The «ActualDODAFProperties» stereotype </span><span>will no longer be applied automatically after creating instances for «DODAFProperties». Moreover, the «</span><span>DODAFProperties» stereotype is renamed to<span> </span><strong>«UAFProperties»</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="01581152-b72d-4f74-b829-8daddda97846">In this version, the Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table was renamed to the<span> </span><strong>Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table</strong>.<span> </span><a class="conf-macro output-inline" style="text-decoration: none;" data-macro-name="sp-plaintextbody-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF13PTWRT/pages/228984296/Working+with+Strategic+Actual+Strategic+Phase+Table">Learn more &gt;&gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="0c89986f-cdf9-4aed-ac2f-05134293994f">A new<span> </span><strong>UAF BoK Sample</strong><span> </span>is now available. This sample is based on the<span> </span><strong>UAF WOW! book</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="6001ef7c-89da-425a-992e-770d5269ee49">This version includes new functionality that lets you select whether the Definition or Comment will<span> </span><strong>serve as the Documentation</strong><span> </span>for UAF elements.</li></ul><h3><span>Documentation</span></h3><ul><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="UAF 1.3 modeling" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UAF 1.3 Plugin 2026x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

Check the product compatibility [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product Compatibility' space='PC'].
